recipes.ingredients: 500 g plain flour • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 280 ml warm water • 600 g floury potatoes • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 leeks • 4 spring onions • 4 garlic cloves • 1 long green chilli • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon ground coriander • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • A small bunch of coriander • Vegetable oil • 300 g plain yogurt • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons fresh mint • ½ teaspoon salt

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Firni
recipes.overview: Rice flour (very finely ground from soaked basmati, traditionally) is whisked into cold milk to a slurry. The rest of the milk warms with sugar; the slurry pours in; the mixture cooks slowly until thick and creamy. Cardamom and rosewater finish; the lot pours into shallow dishes to set. Crushed nuts on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1.2 litres whole milk • 100 g rice flour • 200 g caster sugar • 1 teaspoon ground cardamom • 2 tablespoons rosewater • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract • A pinch of saffron • 50 g pistachios • 30 g flaked almonds • 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut • A few rose petals

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Kabuli Pulao
recipes.overview: Lamb shoulder browns then braises in spiced stock until tender — the stock is the base for the rice. Carrots cut into matchsticks fry slowly in butter and sugar until golden and soft. Raisins plump in butter. Rice parboils in salted water, then layers in the cooking pot with lamb on the bottom, rice piled on top, drizzles of stock, and the lid clamped on for the steam-cook.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg lamb shoulder • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 large onions • 6 garlic cloves • 2 cm ginger • 4 cardamom pods • 1 cinnamon stick • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • 1 teaspoon black pepper • 1.2 litres water • 2 teaspoons salt • 4 large carrots • 50 g unsalted butter • 4 tablespoons caster sugar • 100 g raisins • ¼ teaspoon ground cardamom • 600 g basmati rice • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 tablespoon caramelised sugar • 50 g flaked almonds • 50 g shelled pistachios

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: The crossroads of Persian, Indian and Central Asian cooking. Long-grain rice cooks with carrots, raisins and lamb in Kabuli pulao, the national dish; bolani (stuffed flatbreads), aushak (leek dumplings) and qabili palau define daily eating. Cardamom, cumin, coriander and dried lime drive the seasoning; yogurt, mint and fresh chillies finish nearly every plate.

subcategories.label: American

recipes.title: Apple Pie
recipes.overview: All-butter pie pastry rests in two discs. The filling — a mix of apples (Bramleys for tartness and break-down, Granny Smith and Braeburn for hold), brown sugar, cinnamon, lemon, cornflour — pre-cooks slightly to release water and keep the crust from going soggy. Bottom crust lines a pie dish; filling piles in tall (it sinks); top crust seals on, vented. Egg-washed and sugar-dusted; baked until deep gold.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g plain flour • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 1 teaspoon salt • 250 g cold unsalted butter • 6-8 tablespoons ice water • 1.4 kg mixed apples • 150 g light brown sugar • 50 g caster sugar • 3 tablespoons cornflour • 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground nutmeg • ¼ teaspoon ground allspice • ¼ teaspoon salt • Juice of 1 lemon • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 large egg • 2 tablespoons demerara or coarse sugar

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Buttermilk Fried Chicken
recipes.overview: Chicken pieces brine in buttermilk seasoned with garlic, paprika, salt and a splash of hot sauce — the buttermilk's acid tenderises while the seasoning penetrates. Dredged in heavily-seasoned flour, then dipped back in buttermilk briefly, then dredged again — the double coat is what gives the craggy, extra-crisp crust. Fried in 175°C oil 12-15 minutes per piece.
recipes.ingredients: 8 chicken pieces • 600 ml buttermilk • 4 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons hot sauce • 2 teaspoons salt • 1 teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon paprika • 400 g plain flour • 4 tablespoons cornflour • 2 tablespoons paprika • 1 tablespoon onion powder • 1 tablespoon garlic powder • 1 tablespoon salt • 2 teaspoons black pepper • 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon dried thyme • Vegetable or peanut oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Cornbread
recipes.overview: A 25 cm cast-iron skillet preheats in the hot oven with a generous knob of butter — the butter browns slightly while the pan heats. The batter is fast: cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, plus buttermilk, eggs and melted butter. The hot pan gets the batter; the cornmeal sears immediately on contact, giving the crisp golden crust. 25 minutes; tip out and slice.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g coarse yellow cornmeal • 150 g plain flour • 60 g caster sugar • 1 tablespoon baking powder • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon bicarbonate of soda • 350 ml buttermilk • 2 large eggs • 80 g unsalted butter • 50 g unsalted butter

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Mainstream American cooking shaped by waves of immigration and regional invention. The South gives buttermilk fried chicken, biscuits, pecan pie and barbecue; the Midwest gives meatloaf and apple pie; New England gives clam chowder; the West gives tacos, burritos and sourdough. Butter, bacon and brown sugar lean heavy; the Big Mac and the bagel both belong here.

subcategories.label: Arabian

recipes.title: Kabsa
recipes.overview: Chicken pieces or lamb shoulder brown; onions, garlic, ginger and tomato build the base; baharat (or kabsa spice mix) blooms; tomato puree and stock loosen. The protein simmers until tender. Rice cooks in the same liquid, absorbing all the flavour. Almonds toast in butter; raisins plump. A fresh salsa of tomato, onion, chilli and parsley balances the richness.
recipes.ingredients: 1.2 kg chicken pieces • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon salt • 2 large onions • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm ginger • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 2 medium tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin • 2 dried limes • 1 cinnamon stick • 6 cardamom pods • 4 cloves • 2 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon kabsa spice mix • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1.2 litres hot water • 600 g basmati rice • 50 g flaked almonds • 30 g pine nuts • 50 g raisins • 30 g unsalted butter • 4 tomatoes • 1 small onion • 2 long green chillies • 4 garlic cloves • Juice of 1 lemon • A small bunch coriander • 1 teaspoon salt
ingredientNames: medium tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Maamoul
recipes.overview: A short dough of fine semolina and plain flour rests overnight with butter, milk and orange blossom water — the semolina hydrates fully. Three classic fillings: date (with cinnamon and cloves), walnut (with sugar and rose), pistachio (with sugar and orange blossom). Each cookie wraps a teaspoon of filling, presses into a wooden mould (or scores by hand), bakes pale gold. Dusted with icing sugar.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g fine semolina • 200 g plain flour • 250 g unsalted butter • 80 g caster sugar • 1 teaspoon mahleb • 1 teaspoon ground mastic • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon active dry yeast • 100 ml whole milk • 2 tablespoons orange blossom water • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 300 g pitted dates • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground cloves • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • 200 g walnuts • 80 g caster sugar • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 200 g pistachios • 80 g caster sugar • 1 tablespoon orange blossom water • Icing sugar for dusting

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Mutabbal
recipes.overview: Aubergines char whole over a gas flame until completely soft and the skins blackened. Cooled, peeled, drained. The flesh chops or mashes — never blends; texture matters. Tahini, yogurt, lemon juice, garlic and salt fold in. A pool of olive oil and a sprinkle of pomegranate seeds finish.
recipes.ingredients: 3 large aubergines • 100 g tahini • 100 g plain yogurt • 4 garlic cloves • Juice of 1 lemon • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon ground cumin • A pinch of cayenne or Aleppo pepper • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • 2 tablespoons pomegranate seeds • A small handful of flat-leaf parsley • A pinch of sumac • Warm flatbread

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Saudi and Gulf cooking, built around rice, lamb, dates and aromatic spice. Kabsa (spiced rice with chicken or lamb) is the centrepiece; mansaf, saleeg and harees fill out the table. Baharat (a blend of black pepper, cardamom, cumin, coriander, cloves, nutmeg and cinnamon) seasons; saffron and dried lime perfume; mezze of hummus, mutabbal and salads share the spread.

subcategories.label: British

recipes.title: Bangers and Mash
recipes.overview: Sausages slow-pan-fried so the skins blister and the fat renders, served on top of a soft butter-and-milk mash, smothered in dark onion gravy with mustard and thyme.
recipes.ingredients: 8 good-quality pork sausages • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 kg floury potatoes , peeled and cubed • 75 g unsalted butter • 100 ml whole milk • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • 3 large onions • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • 1 tablespoon plain flour • 500 ml beef stock • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Beef Wellington
recipes.overview: Centre-cut beef fillet is seared hard for colour, smeared with English mustard, wrapped in a tight blanket of mushroom duxelles and prosciutto, then encased in all-butter puff pastry and roasted at high heat. The pastry insulates the beef so it cooks gently to medium-rare while the crust crisps. The trick: dry the duxelles thoroughly so the pastry stays crisp underneath.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg piece centre-cut beef fillet • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 2 tablespoons English mustard • 500 g chestnut mushrooms • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • 2 banana shallots • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves • 50 ml dry sherry or Madeira • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 8-10 slices prosciutto • 500 g all-butter puff pastry, chilled • Plain flour for dusting • 2 egg yolks beaten with 1 tablespoon water • Flaky sea salt
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
egg yolks beaten with 1 tablespoon water

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Bubble and Squeak
recipes.overview: Cooked potatoes and greens are roughly mashed together, pressed into a hot buttered pan, and left to brown undisturbed until a crust forms. Flipped (or turned in pieces), then served with a fried egg or alongside cold meats.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g cooked potatoes • 300 g cooked Brussels sprouts or cabbage • 1 onion • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons goose fat or beef dripping • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • A pinch of nutmeg
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Cheese and Onion Pie
recipes.overview: Onions cook slowly in butter until very soft and sweet. Mashed potato + grated cheddar binds the filling so it slices cleanly. Shortcrust pastry top and bottom; egg-washed and baked until deep golden.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g plain flour • 175 g cold unsalted butter or half butter half lard • ½ teaspoon salt • 4-6 tablespoons cold water • 1 egg • 4 large onions • 50 g unsalted butter • 300 g floury potatoes • 400 g mature cheddar • 2 teaspoons English mustard • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • Salt and black pepper
ingredientNames: cold unsalted butter or half butter half lard

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Cottage Pie
recipes.overview: Beef mince browned with aromatics, simmered into a thick red-wine gravy with tomato and Worcestershire, topped with cheddar mash and baked until the peaks crisp. Forgiving, freezer-friendly, and the textbook British family dinner.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tablespoon olive oil • 500 g beef mince • 1 onion • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 100 ml red wine • 300 ml beef stock • 1 tablespoon plain flour • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves or ½ teaspoon dried • 1 bay leaf • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 kg floury potatoes , peeled and cubed • 75 g unsalted butter • 75 ml whole milk • 75 g mature cheddar • Salt
ingredientNames: thyme leaves or ½ teaspoon dried
salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Fish and Chips
recipes.overview: Floury Maris Piper potatoes are blanched, drained, and fried twice (once at low temperature to cook through, again at high temperature to crisp). The fish dunks in a cold beer batter and goes into the same hot oil. Mushy peas alongside.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg Maris Piper potatoes • Vegetable oil or beef dripping for deep-frying • Sea salt • 4 skinless cod or haddock fillets • 2 tablespoons plain flour • Salt and pepper • 200 g plain flour • 1 teaspoon baking powder • ½ teaspoon salt • 300 ml very cold beer • 400 g frozen peas • 30 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons crème fraîche • 1 tablespoon mint • Salt and pepper • Lemon wedges • Malt vinegar • Tartare sauce
ingredientNames: vegetable oil or beef dripping for deep-frying

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Fish Pie
recipes.overview: Mixed fish poaches briefly in milk, the milk becomes a parsley-cheddar béchamel, and the lot is topped with cheddar mash. Bake until the top is golden and the sauce bubbles around the edges.
recipes.ingredients: 300 g skinless smoked haddock fillet • 300 g skinless salmon fillet • 200 g raw king prawns • 500 ml whole milk • 1 bay leaf • A few peppercorns • 50 g unsalted butter • 50 g plain flour • 100 g mature cheddar • 2 teaspoons Dijon mustard • 3 tablespoons fresh parsley • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 kg floury potatoes , peeled and cubed • 75 g unsalted butter • 75 ml whole milk • 50 g mature cheddar • Salt
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Glamorgan Sausages
recipes.overview: Leeks soften in butter; mix with grated caerphilly (or mature cheddar), breadcrumbs, mustard, sage and beaten egg. The mixture chills to firm up, then rolls into sausage shapes, gets a flour-egg-breadcrumb coat and fries until golden.
recipes.ingredients: 2 medium leeks • 30 g unsalted butter • 200 g caerphilly • 200 g fresh white breadcrumbs • 2 tablespoons fresh sage or 1 teaspoon dried • 1 tablespoon English mustard • 2 teaspoons fresh thyme leaves • 2 large eggs • 50 g plain flour • Salt and black pepper • 4 tablespoons sunflower or vegetable oil

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Lancashire Hotpot
recipes.overview: Lamb shoulder or middle-neck chunks layered with onions and stock in a deep dish, topped with sliced waxy potatoes that protect the meat below while crisping golden above. Long, low oven time builds depth without intervention.
recipes.ingredients: 700 g lamb shoulder or middle-neck • 2 tablespoons plain flour • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 onions • 2 carrots • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • 500 ml lamb or beef stock • 1 kg waxy potatoes , peeled and sliced 5 mm thick • 50 g unsalted butter • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Sausage and Butter Bean Stew
recipes.overview: Sausages browned in a casserole, joined by onion, garlic and rosemary, then a tin of tomatoes and a tin of butter beans. Twenty minutes on the hob and dinner's ready.
recipes.ingredients: 8 good-quality pork sausages • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 3 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary or 1 teaspoon dried • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 2 x 400 g tins butter beans • 200 ml chicken stock • 1 teaspoon brown sugar • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • A handful of fresh parsley
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Shepherd's Pie
recipes.overview: Lamb mince browned with onion, carrot and celery, simmered with stock, tomato purée, Worcestershire and rosemary into a thick gravy, topped with garlic-butter mash and baked until the peaks crisp golden.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tablespoon olive oil • 500 g lamb mince • 1 onion • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon fresh rosemary or ½ teaspoon dried • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves or ½ teaspoon dried • 300 ml lamb or beef stock • 1 tablespoon plain flour • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 kg floury potatoes , peeled and cubed • 75 g unsalted butter • 75 ml whole milk • 50 g mature cheddar • Salt
ingredientNames: thyme leaves or ½ teaspoon dried
salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Steak and Kidney Pie
recipes.overview: Diced chuck steak and ox kidney browned hard, simmered slowly with onions, mushrooms, stout and beef stock until meltingly tender, then topped with shortcrust pastry and baked until golden.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g chuck steak • 200 g ox kidney • 2 tablespoons plain flour, seasoned with salt and pepper • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large onion • 200 g chestnut mushrooms • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 250 ml stout • 400 ml beef stock • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • 375 g all-butter shortcrust pastry • 1 egg, beaten with 1 tablespoon water

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Sunday Roast Chicken
recipes.overview: A 1.6 kg bird rubbed with herb butter, roasted high to crisp the skin then dropped to finish through. Roast potatoes parboil-then-fluff in goose fat; Yorkshire puddings rise in screaming-hot tins; pan juices become gravy. Time the components from the chicken's resting period.
recipes.ingredients: 1.6 kg whole chicken • 75 g unsalted butter • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves • 1 lemon • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 kg Maris Piper potatoes • 4 tablespoons goose fat or beef dripping • Sea salt • 140 g plain flour • 4 large eggs • 200 ml whole milk • ½ teaspoon salt • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • Roasting tray juices • 1 tablespoon plain flour • 300 ml chicken stock • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Toad in the Hole
recipes.overview: A simple egg-flour-milk batter (Yorkshire pudding ratio) poured around browned sausages in a screaming-hot tin of dripping. The fat needs to be smoking before the batter goes in or the pudding won't rise. Onion gravy alongside.
recipes.ingredients: 8 good-quality pork sausages • 2 tablespoons beef dripping or vegetable oil • 140 g plain flour • 4 large eggs • 200 ml whole milk • ½ teaspoon salt • A few sprigs of fresh thyme • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • 2 large onions • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • 1 tablespoon plain flour • 400 ml beef stock • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Welsh Rarebit
recipes.overview: Butter and flour cook to a roux; ale and milk loosen it; mature cheddar melts in with mustard and Worcestershire. The mixture should be thick enough to spread, not pour. Pile onto toasted bread; grill until bubbling and freckled.
recipes.ingredients: 4 slices sourdough or good white bread • 30 g unsalted butter • 30 g plain flour • 100 ml ale or stout • 50 ml whole milk • 250 g mature cheddar • 1 tablespoon English mustard • 2 teaspoons Worcestershire sauce • 1 egg yolk • A grating of nutmeg • Black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Home cooking from the British Isles: Sunday roasts, slow-braised stews, baked puddings and frugal one-pots from leftovers. Worcestershire, mustard, mint, sage and thyme handle the seasoning; flour, butter, beef stock and dripping build pies and gravies. Defining techniques include the Sunday roast with all the trimmings, the steamed and baked pudding, the suet-crust pie and the dripping-fried chip.

subcategories.label: Cajun

recipes.title: Big Mike’s Mac ’n’ Cheese
recipes.overview: A creamy, spicy Cajun-style mac and cheese with sharp white Cheddar, hot sauce, and a golden grilled crust. This comforting dish combines classic comfort food with Southern heat for a crowd-pleasing side or main.
recipes.ingredients: 1 lb elbow macaroni, cooked and drained • 60 g butter, melted • 1 egg • 240 ml evaporated milk • 120 ml whole milk • 100 g white Cheddar cheese , plus extra for topping • 2 tsp dry mustard • 1 tbsp hot sauce • Salt and black pepper, to taste

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Blackened Chicken
recipes.overview: Blackened chicken is a Cajun classic where a bold spice mixture creates a charred, flavourful crust on the outside while keeping the meat tender inside. The dark finish comes from the high-heat searing of the spice-coated chicken, a technique that seals in juices and creates a distinctive smoky flavour. This versatile dish works equally well with fish and is delicious served over salads, in sandwiches, or as a standalone protein.
recipes.ingredients: 4 chicken breasts • 1 tablespoon oil • 1 tablespoon butter • 1 tablespoon salt • 1½ teaspoons garlic powder • 1½ teaspoons freshly ground black pepper • 1 teaspoon ground white pepper • 1 teaspoon onion powder • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper • ½ teaspoon paprika

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Crawfish Étouffée
recipes.overview: A blond roux (butter and flour, cooked just to the colour of peanut butter — lighter than gumbo's roux) cooks first. The trinity goes in to soften; tomato paste, Cajun spice and stock loosen; the lot simmers down to a thick gravy. Crawfish tails (or prawns) join near the end and cook briefly so they stay tender. Spring onion and parsley to finish.
recipes.ingredients: 80 g unsalted butter • 60 g plain flour • 1 large onion • 3 celery sticks • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 1 tablespoon Cajun seasoning • 700 ml seafood, chicken or vegetable stock • 2 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 tablespoon hot sauce • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 500 g crawfish tail meat • Juice of half a lemon • 4 spring onions • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Cooked white rice • Hot sauce

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Dirty Rice
recipes.overview: Chicken livers blitz to a fine paste (so they melt into the rice rather than appearing as recognisable pieces). Minced beef or pork browns hard; trinity follows; spice blooms; rice toasts; stock joins. Everything simmers covered until the rice is tender. The result is grey-brown rice studded with meat — the "dirty" name describes the look, not the cleanliness.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g chicken livers • 250 g minced pork • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil or pork fat • 1 large onion • 3 celery sticks • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon Cajun seasoning • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon dried thyme • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • ½ teaspoon cayenne • 350 g long-grain white rice • 800 ml chicken stock • 2 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 4 spring onions • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Hot sauce

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Gumbo Z'Herbes
recipes.overview: A dark roux is built first — flour cooked in oil to peanut-butter brown. The Cajun trinity (onion, celery, pepper) joins; greens — finely chopped — pile in with stock and simmer for 45 minutes until they're meltingly soft and the broth has darkened. Hot sauce, file powder and rice finish the bowl.
recipes.ingredients: 100 ml vegetable oil • 80 g plain flour • 1 large onion • 4 celery sticks • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon thyme leaves • 2 teaspoons smoked paprika • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper • 1 bunch collard greens • 1 bunch mustard greens or turnip tops • 1 bunch kale • 1 bunch flat-leaf parsley • 200 g spinach • 4 spring onions • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 1 tablespoon filé powder • Hot sauce • Cooked white rice
ingredientNames: bunch mustard greens or turnip tops

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Jambalaya
recipes.overview: Jambalaya is a one-pot Cajun rice dish that combines proteins, vegetables, and aromatic spices in a celebration of Louisiana cooking. This contemporary pasta version puts a modern twist on the classic by using penne instead of rice, layering shrimp, chicken, and andouille sausage in a creamy tomato sauce infused with cajun spices. The result is a hearty, flavourful dish that captures the essence of New Orleans cuisine.
recipes.ingredients: 12 large shrimp • 2 chicken breast fillets • 1 Andouille sausage • 250 grams penne pasta • 230 ml chicken stock • 350 ml chopped tomatoes • 230 ml double cream • 3 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 1 red pepper • 1 jalapeño • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme • 3 tablespoons fresh basil • 3 teaspoons cajun or creole spice mix • 75 grams Parmesan cheese • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Pecan Pie
recipes.overview: A simple all-butter shortcrust blind-bakes to a deep golden shell. Pecans toast briefly to bring out their oils. The filling — eggs, brown sugar, golden syrup (or corn syrup), butter, vanilla, bourbon, salt — whisks smooth and pours over the pecans. Bakes at moderate heat until the centre has just set with a slight wobble.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g plain flour • 125 g cold unsalted butter • 30 g caster sugar • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 large egg yolk • 3-4 tablespoons cold water • 250 g pecan halves • 200 g light brown sugar • 100 g caster sugar • 200 ml golden syrup • 80 g unsalted butter • 4 large eggs • 2 tablespoons bourbon • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract • ½ teaspoon salt

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Red Beans and Rice
recipes.overview: Trinity (onion, celery, pepper) softens in oil; smoked paprika and Cajun seasoning bloom. Soaked red kidney beans go in with stock, bay and thyme; simmer until tender. The last 30 minutes, beans get partially mashed against the side of the pot to thicken the broth into a gravy. Vinegar and hot sauce at the finish.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g dried red kidney beans • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large onion • 4 celery sticks • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon smoked paprika • 1 tablespoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon dried thyme • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper • ½ teaspoon cayenne • 2 bay leaves • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 teaspoons salt • 1 tablespoon cider vinegar • Hot sauce • Cooked long-grain white rice • 4 spring onions

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Shellfish Gumbo
recipes.overview: Gumbo is a traditional Cajun soup that's served over rice as a main course. This shellfish version showcases the holy trinity of Cajun cooking, onion, celery, and sweet pepper, combined with fresh mussels, prawns, and crab in a rich, flavourful roux-based broth. The addition of fresh chilli adds a modern twist while honouring the classic Cajun tradition of bold, layered flavours.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams fresh mussels • 450 grams raw prawns • 1 cooked crab • Small bunch of parsley • 150 ml vegetable oil • 115 grams plain flour • 1 green pepper • 1 large onion • 2 celery sticks • 1 fresh green chilli • 3 garlic cloves • 75 grams smoked spiced sausage • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme • 6 spring onions • 275 grams white long-grain rice • Tabasco sauce • Salt to taste

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

subcategories.overview: Louisiana cooking born of French, Spanish, African and Caribbean influences. Heat and depth come from the holy trinity of onion, celery and bell pepper, layered with cayenne, paprika, bay and smoked sausage. Defining techniques are the long-cooked roux, blackening over high heat and one-pot rice dishes like jambalaya and gumbo.

subcategories.label: Cambodian

recipes.title: Amok Trey
recipes.overview: Kroeung is pounded fresh from lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, garlic, shallots, kaffir lime zest and coriander root. The paste fries briefly to bloom; coconut cream and stock loosen it; eggs whisk in to set the custard. Fish chunks fold through with chopped greens; the mix is spooned into banana-leaf cups (or ramekins) and steamed.
recipes.ingredients: 3 stalks lemongrass • 4 cm galangal • 3 cm fresh turmeric • 6 garlic cloves • 4 shallots • Zest of 2 kaffir limes • 1 small bunch coriander roots • 1 long red chilli • 1 teaspoon shrimp paste • 600 g white fish fillet • 250 ml thick coconut cream • 100 ml fish stock or water • 2 large eggs • 2 tablespoons fish sauce • 1 tablespoon palm sugar • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 small handful Thai basil • 100 g spinach or noni leaves • 4 banana leaves • Hot cooked jasmine rice • 4 tablespoons coconut cream • 1 long red chilli • A few kaffir lime leaves

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Chruok
recipes.overview: Vegetables are julienned thin so the brine penetrates fast. Salt drains them briefly to expel water; brine pours over; everything sits at room temperature for an hour, then refrigerates. Fish sauce is traditional but soy works for a vegetarian version.
recipes.ingredients: 1 medium daikon radish • 2 medium carrots • 1 small cucumber • 1 teaspoon salt • 100 ml white cane vinegar • 60 ml lime juice • 80 g palm sugar • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 2 bird's-eye chillies • 100 ml water

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Nhom Trav
recipes.overview: Banana flower is sliced thin and immediately submerged in lemon water to stop browning. Tofu cubes (or shredded chicken in non-vegetarian versions) join the salad. Peanuts toast; shallots fry crisp. The dressing is lime, palm sugar, soy and chilli; everything tosses together with herbs at the last minute.
recipes.ingredients: 1 banana flower • Juice of 1 lemon • 200 g firm tofu • 1 small carrot • 50 g beansprouts • A small bunch Thai basil • A small bunch coriander • A small bunch mint • 4 spring onions • 50 g roasted peanuts • 4 shallots • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 3 tablespoons lime juice • 2 tablespoons light soy sauce • 2 tablespoons palm sugar • 1-2 bird's-eye chillies • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons water

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Sangkhya Lapov
recipes.overview: A small kabocha or buttercup pumpkin has its top cut off and seeds scooped out. A custard of coconut milk, palm sugar, eggs and a pinch of salt fills the cavity. The pumpkin (top replaced) sits in a steamer over boiling water and steams 1 hour or so until the custard is set. Slice in wedges to serve.
recipes.ingredients: 1 small kabocha or buttercup pumpkin • 5 large eggs • 250 ml thick coconut milk • 200 g palm sugar • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract • A few pandan leaves — optional but classic
ingredientNames: few pandan leaves — optional but classic

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Khmer cooking, gentler and more herbal than its Thai and Vietnamese neighbours. Kroeung, a paste of lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime, turmeric and shallot, sits behind every meal. Coconut milk, palm sugar, prahok (fermented fish paste) and a long list of fresh herbs (mint, basil, rice paddy herb) round out the seasoning. Steaming inside banana leaves, soured soups (samlor machu) and grilled fish are characteristic; the curries are perfumed rather than fiery.

subcategories.label: Chilean

recipes.title: Ensalada Chilena
recipes.overview: Sweet white onion is sliced thin and soaked in cold salted water 15 minutes — this draws out the sharpness and leaves a clean, mild onion flavour. Tomatoes slice into half-moons; everything tosses with olive oil, lime and salt. Coriander on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large sweet white onion • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 large ripe tomatoes • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • Juice of 2 limes • ½ teaspoon flaky sea salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • A small bunch of fresh coriander • 1 long green chilli

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Mote con Huesillos
recipes.overview: Dried peaches soak overnight to soften, then simmer with sugar, cinnamon and cloves into a deep amber syrup. Husked wheat (mote, or pearled wheat as a substitute) cooks separately until tender. Both chill thoroughly. Served in tall glasses with a big spoonful of cooked wheat at the bottom, two peach halves on top, and the cold syrup poured over.
recipes.ingredients: 12 dried peaches • 1.5 litres water • 200 g panela or dark brown sugar • 1 cinnamon stick • 4 cloves • 1 strip orange peel • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 200 g pearled wheat • 1 litre water • A pinch of salt

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Pastel de Choclo
recipes.overview: Pino (the beef-onion mince base) cooks first: onions soften slowly with cumin, paprika and oregano; mince browns in; raisins, olives and a splash of stock simmer everything to a moist filling. Chicken pieces poach separately and shred. The corn topping blends fresh sweet corn (or kabocha-corn mix in winter) with milk, butter and basil into a thick batter. Layer in clay dishes (or one large baking dish): pino, chicken, hard-boiled egg slices, corn batter on top. A heavy dust of sugar makes the top crisp under the grill.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 3 large onions • 6 garlic cloves • 600 g beef mince • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet smoked paprika • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 100 ml beef stock • 80 g raisins • 100 g pitted black olives • 4 chicken thighs • 1 small onion • 1 bay leaf • 500 ml water • 1 teaspoon salt • 800 g sweet corn kernels • 200 ml whole milk • 50 g unsalted butter • 4 tablespoons fresh basil leaves • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons cornflour mixed with 3 tablespoons cold water • 4 hard-boiled eggs • 3 tablespoons caster sugar
ingredientNames: cornflour mixed with 3 tablespoons cold water

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: A long thin country between desert and Antarctica gives a kitchen built on Pacific seafood (loco, jaibas, machas), Andean potatoes and corn, and a deep tradition of slow-cooked stews. Cazuela, pastel de choclo, empanadas de pino, ceviche and a national obsession with avocado define the everyday plate. Pebre (the table salsa of tomato, onion, coriander and ají) appears with almost every meal; pisco sour and mote con huesillos handle the drinks.

subcategories.label: Chinese

recipes.title: Beef and Broccoli
recipes.overview: Sliced beef velvets briefly in cornflour and soy, broccoli florets blanch to bright green, and the lot stir-fries hard with garlic and ginger in a soy-oyster-rice-wine sauce. Served over steamed rice.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g rib-eye, sirloin or rump • 1 tablespoon soy sauce • 1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine • 1 tablespoon cornflour • 1 teaspoon sugar • 4 tablespoons oyster sauce • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil • 100 ml chicken stock or water • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1 head broccoli • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 4 spring onions • Cooked rice, to serve

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Beef in Oyster Sauce
recipes.overview: A quick and elegant stir-fry that balances savoury oyster sauce with tender beef. This dish exemplifies the Chinese technique of high-heat cooking to seal flavours while keeping meat moist. Quality oyster sauce is essential, it should deepen the dish rather than dominate it.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams lean beef steak • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1½ tablespoons groundnut oil • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1½ tablespoons oyster sauce • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1½ tablespoons spring onions

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Braised Prawns
recipes.overview: A simple, elegant preparation favoured by street vendors throughout southern China. This quick-braising method takes only minutes and produces tender, fragrant prawns. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity, fresh ginger and spring onions infuse the delicate sweetness of prawns. Equally delicious served hot immediately or chilled for an exotic picnic dish.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams prawns • 1½ tablespoons spring onions • 2 teaspoons fresh ginger • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Cashew Chicken
recipes.overview: This dish exemplifies the Chinese penchant for contrasting textures. Tender succulent pieces of chicken are paired with sweet, crunchy cashew nuts and a delicate sauce. While the original Chinese version would have used peanuts (as cashews were not traditionally featured in Chinese cookery), this adaptation showcases how the cuisine evolves while maintaining its core philosophy of textural harmony.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams boneless chicken breasts • 1 egg white • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 150 ml groundnut oil • 50 grams cashew nuts • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon spring onions

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chicken and Peanut
recipes.overview: When cooking Chinese food, it's essential to think about the flavours and textures of ingredients working in harmony. Juicy chicken combined with succulent baby corn, tender vegetables, and salty, crunchy cashew nuts create a balanced dish where each element complements the others. A glossy sauce ties everything together without overwhelming delicate flavours.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tbsp groundnut oil • 3 garlic cloves • 1/2 tsp fresh ginger • 2 chicken breast fillets • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 40g tinned water chestnuts • 30g tinned bamboo shoots • 3 baby corn cobs • 2 tbsp oyster sauce • 1 tbsp dark soy sauce • 80ml chicken stock • 1/4 tsp salt • 1/4 tsp white pepper • 1 tbsp cornflour • 30g salted, roasted cashew nuts • 1 tsp sesame oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chicken in Black Bean Sauce
recipes.overview: A classic Chinese dish bursting with the fragrance of black bean and garlic. Chicken wings are ideal for this preparation as they cook quickly in a high-heat wok while remaining succulent and flavourful. The fermented black beans add depth and a distinctive umami character that defines authentic Chinese cooking.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams chicken wings • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 2 teaspoons groundnut oil • 1 tablespoon fresh ginger • 1 tablespoon garlic • 1½ tablespoons black beans • 1 red pepper • 1½ tablespoons spring onions • 150 ml Chinese chicken stock

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chicken with Sesame Seeds
recipes.overview: This is a version of the fragrant Sichuan dish popularly known as 'Strange taste chicken' or bang-bang chicken because it incorporates many flavours simultaneously, hot, spicy, sour, sweet, and salty all in perfect balance. The sesame seeds add a crunchy texture that contrasts beautifully with tender chicken meat. Equally delicious served hot or at room temperature, making it ideal for entertaining.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams boneless chicken breasts • 1 egg white • ½ teaspoon salt • 2 teaspoons cornflour • 150 ml groundnut oil • 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon cider or black rice vinegar • ½ teaspoon chilli bean sauce • ½ teaspoon sesame oil • 1 teaspoon sugar • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • ½ teaspoon Sichuan peppercorns • 2 teaspoons spring onions • 1 tablespoon white sesame seeds

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chilli Pork Spare Ribs
recipes.overview: This showstopper from western Chinese cooking combines spices and sauces in a signature style. Deep-fried spareribs are braised until tender, then finished in the oven with a glossy, spicy coating. The combination of chilli bean sauce, hoisin, and yellow beans creates a complex, savoury flavour profile characteristic of the region. The spareribs can be finished in the oven, under a grill, or on a barbecue.
recipes.ingredients: 700 grams pork spareribs • 570 ml groundnut oil • 900 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon chilli bean sauce • 2 teaspoons sugar • 70 ml dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons garlic • 1 tablespoon spring onions • 1 tablespoon whole yellow bean sauce • 1½ tablespoons hoisin sauce

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chow Mein
recipes.overview: Chow mein literally means 'stir-fried noodles' and this contemporary dish is equally popular throughout southern China as it is worldwide. Fresh egg noodles are quickly stir-fried with protein and vegetables, creating a harmonious balance of textures and flavours. The keys to success are properly cooked noodles, high-heat wok cooking, and precise timing.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams fresh egg noodles • 100 grams chicken breast • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • 2 teaspoons groundnut oil • 1 tablespoon groundnut oil • 1 teaspoon garlic • 50 grams mange tout • 25 grams Parma ham • 1 teaspoon light soy sauce • ½ teaspoon sugar • 1 tablespoon spring onions • 1 teaspoon sesame oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Crispy Sichuan Duck
recipes.overview: In China, duck is a special occasion treatment reserved for banquets and celebrations. Don't be intimidated by the preparation, most steps are straightforward and can be done a day ahead. The technique is masterful: steaming renders out most of the fat, leaving meat moist and succulent, while deep-frying creates a shatteringly crisp skin. The result is elegant, restaurant-quality dinner.
recipes.ingredients: 1 whole duck • 2 tablespoons five spice powder • 2 tablespoons salt • 4 slices fresh ginger • 4 spring onions • 1 litre oil

winePairings.note: Earthy, medium-bodied reds match game's depth without overpowering.

recipes.title: Curried Chicken with Peppers
recipes.overview: Curry blends beautifully with chicken when prepared in the style of southern Chinese cuisine, as a light, subtle sauce that enhances rather than overpowers the delicate chicken meat. This recipe balances spice with refinement, proving that curry in Chinese cooking is more elegant whisper than aggressive shout.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams chicken breasts • 1 egg white • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 225 grams green peppers • 150 ml groundnut oil • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock • 2 teaspoons curry powder or curry paste • 1 teaspoon sugar • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon cornflour

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Egg Fried Rice
recipes.overview: Day-old refrigerated rice (or freshly cooked, then cooled and dried) tosses in a smoking-hot wok with garlic, eggs and spring onion. Soy sauce and a splash of sesame oil season the lot. The high heat gives the wok-hei smoky edge that defines proper fried rice.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g cooked long-grain or jasmine rice • 4 large eggs • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 spring onions • 4 garlic cloves • 100 g cooked ham, char siu or prawns • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil • ½ teaspoon ground white pepper • Salt to taste

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Fish in Hot and Sour Sauce
recipes.overview: The combination of hot and sour is a signature of western Chinese cuisine. This quick, simple dish is perfect for a light family meal or elegant entertaining. The interplay between chilli heat, vinegar acidity, and savoury umami creates a vibrant sauce that complements delicate fish beautifully without overwhelming it.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams plaice fillets • 70 ml groundnut oil • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce • 2 teaspoons tomato purée • ½ teaspoon chilli bean sauce or chilli powder • ½ teaspoon white pepper • 1 tablespoon cider vinegar or black rice vinegar • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1 tablespoon spring onions
ingredientNames: chilli bean sauce or chilli powder
cider vinegar or black rice vinegar

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Five Spice Spare Ribs
recipes.overview: This delightful meat dish engages the senses with many contrasting tastes. The spareribs are first marinated, then deep-fried until crisp, and finally slowly braised in an unusual, piquant sauce. Five spice powder provides the warm, complex backbone, while vinegar and orange peel add brightness and complexity. The result is both elegant and deeply satisfying.
recipes.ingredients: 700 grams pork spareribs • 570 ml groundnut oil • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon white rice vinegar • ½ teaspoon sesame oil • 1 tablespoon garlic • 1 tablespoon five spice powder • 1½ tablespoons spring onions • 1 tablespoon sugar • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons freshly grated orange peel • 70 ml cider vinegar

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Fried Fish with Ginger
recipes.overview: Ginger is the Chinese cook's answer to lemon in European fish cookery. In this elegant preparation, fresh ginger imparts a subtle fragrance that enhances rather than masks the delicate flavour of fish. The simplicity of the technique belies the sophistication of the result, the interplay between crispy exterior and tender flesh, balanced with aromatic sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams cod fillets • ¼ teaspoon salt • 1½ tablespoons cornflour • 75 ml groundnut oil • 1½ tablespoons fresh ginger • 1 tablespoon Chinese chicken stock • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 1 teaspoon sugar

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: General Tso’s Chicken
recipes.overview: This iconic American-Chinese dish combines deep-fried chicken with a sweet, spicy, and slightly tangy sauce. General Tso's chicken exemplifies the bold flavours of outside China Chinese cooking, where heat from dried chillies, sweetness from sugar, and complexity from vinegar create a sauce that is bold yet balanced. Restaurant-quality results require proper oil temperature and crispy, well-coated chicken.
recipes.ingredients: 455g boneless, skinless chicken thighs • 240ml rice wine • 60ml soy sauce • 125g flour • 1 tablespoon salt • 2 litres vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 2 cloves fresh garlic • 1 teaspoon fresh ginger • 45g dried chili pod • 60ml rice wine • 60ml soy sauce • 60ml rice wine vinegar • 50g sugar • 1/2 tablespoon cornstarch • 35g spring onion • White rice for serving

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Kung Pao Chicken
recipes.overview: This iconic hot and spicy chicken from western China showcases contrasting flavours, heat from chillies and Sichuan peppercorns balanced with subtle sweetness. The numbing quality of Sichuan peppercorns and the fragrance of slow-braising creates an aromatic dish that is equally delicious served immediately or reheated the next day.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams chicken pieces • ½ teaspoon salt • 2 spring onions • 150 ml groundnut oil • 1 teaspoon oil • 1 dried red chilli • ½ teaspoon fresh ginger • ½ teaspoon chilli powder • 300 ml Chinese chicken stock • ½ teaspoon Sichuan peppercorns • ½ teaspoon sugar • 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Lemon Chicken
recipes.overview: A Hong Kong specialty that pairs delicate chicken with a tart yet subtly sweet lemon sauce. The technique of shallow-frying chicken strips until just cooked keeps the meat tender, while a glossy sauce coats each piece perfectly. The balance between citrus acidity and the subtle spice of dried chilli makes this dish elegant yet comforting.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams chicken breasts • 1 egg white • 2 teaspoons cornflour • 70 ml groundnut oil • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1½ tablespoons fresh lemon juice • 2 teaspoons sugar • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons dry sherry or rice wine • ½ teaspoon garlic • 1 dried red chilli • 1 teaspoon cornflour

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Mapo Tofu (Vegetarian)
recipes.overview: Silken tofu poaches briefly in salted water (firms it up so it doesn't break). Dried shiitake mushrooms rehydrate and chop fine. Doubanjiang fries in oil until the oil reddens; mushrooms, garlic, ginger and chilli flakes follow. Stock loosens; tofu joins gently; cornflour slurry thickens. Sichuan peppercorn dust at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g silken tofu • 1 teaspoon salt • 30 g dried shiitake mushrooms • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 3 tablespoons doubanjiang • 1 tablespoon fermented black beans • 1 teaspoon Sichuan chilli flakes • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 300 ml mushroom-soaking liquid + 150 ml vegetable stock • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sugar • 2 tablespoons cornflour mixed with 4 tablespoons cold water • 1 tablespoon Sichuan peppercorns • 4 spring onions • 2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil • Cooked white rice
ingredientNames: cornflour mixed with 4 tablespoons cold water

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Peking Duck
recipes.overview: The Chinese revere duck as a symbol of wholesomeness and fidelity. With Peking duck, Chinese cooks mastered the art of maximising the duck's rich, succulent flesh while minimising bone and fat. This simplified method produces similar results to traditional restaurant preparations. While the authentic version involves air-pumping and wood-burning ovens, this approach creates shatteringly crisp skin through a honey-syrup glaze and extended drying.
recipes.ingredients: 1 whole duck • 1 lemon • 1 litre water • 3 tablespoons honey • 3 tablespoons dark soy sauce • 150 ml dry sherry or rice wine • 12 Chinese pancakes • 6 tablespoons hoisin sauce • 24 spring onion brushes

winePairings.note: Earthy, medium-bodied reds match game's depth without overpowering.

recipes.title: Pork with Black Bean Sauce
recipes.overview: Pork goes particularly well with black beans; their salt and spicy flavour is distinctly Chinese. This quick-cooking dish balances tender pork with the bold, pungent character of fermented black beans. The result is authentic, flavour-forward, and ready in minutes. Perfect for weeknight meals or elegant entertaining.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams lean pork • 2 teaspoons dry sherry • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • ½ teaspoon cornflour • 1 tablespoon oil • 1½ tablespoons black beans • 1½ teaspoons garlic • 1 tablespoon spring onions • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sugar • 2 teaspoons Chinese chicken stock

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Shredded Beef with Chillies
recipes.overview: The essence of this recipe lies in knife technique: the beef must be cut into very thin strips for authentic texture and rapid cooking. A brief freeze makes slicing easier and more uniform. The result is a dish of tender, fragrant beef balanced with fresh ginger, crisp vegetables, and bold chilli heat.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams tenderloin beef • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce • 1 tablespoon medium-dry sherry • 1 teaspoon dark brown soft sugar • 6 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large onion • 3 cm piece fresh root ginger • 1 carrot • 3 fresh chillies • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Fresh chives
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Sichuan Chicken
recipes.overview: This straightforward Sichuan preparation showcases the region's bold use of chillies and spices. The technique is simple: season, rest, and cook in layers, building flavour as you go. Oyster sauce adds depth, while seven spices (chilli power, Sichuan pepper, salt, garlic, ginger, pepper) create a full-bodied dish.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams chicken fillets • 2 cloves garlic • 2 cm ginger • ½ tsp salt • ½ tsp red chilli powder • 1 tbsp oil • 3 cloves garlic • ½ red or green bell pepper • 5 dried chillies • 2 tbsp oyster sauce • 4 tsp soy sauce • ¼ tsp black pepper • ¼ tsp sugar • 250 ml water

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sichuan Chicken with Kung Pao Sauce
recipes.overview: This recipe from the Sichuan region of western China showcases the region's trademark use of chillies combined with the modern addition of cashew nuts. The sauce is complex and layered, savoury from fermented beans and hoisin, spicy from dried chillies, and balanced with vinegar's acidity and sugar's sweetness. The result is distinctly Chinese in technique yet contemporary in execution.
recipes.ingredients: 2 skinless chicken breasts • 1 egg white • 2 teaspoon cornflour • ½ teaspoon salt • 3 tablespoons sunflower oil • 3 dried red chillies • 2 tablespoon yellow salted beans • 1 tablespoon hoisin sauce • 1 teaspoon soft light brown sugar • 1 tablespoon medium-dry sherry • 1 tablespoon wine vinegar • 4 garlic cloves • 150 ml chicken stock • 115 grams roasted cashew nuts • Fresh coriander

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sichuan Dry-Fried Green Beans
recipes.overview: Beans dry-fry in just a thin slick of oil over high heat for 6-8 minutes — they need to blister and wrinkle, not stay green and snappy. Aromatics — garlic, ginger, dried chillies, Sichuan peppercorns and zhacai (Sichuan preserved mustard tuber) — flash-cook. Beans return; soy and rice wine glaze; sesame oil to finish.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g long beans • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 6 dried red chillies • 1 teaspoon Sichuan peppercorns • 50 g zhacai — optional but classic • 1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil • 4 spring onions

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Sichuan Pepper Beef
recipes.overview: This fast, easy, and delicious supper showcases how Chinese five-spice powder flavours an entire dish via a marinade approach. Overnight marination develops deep, complex flavours that distinguish this simple stir-fry from quickly-thrown-together meals. The combination of Sichuan pepper's numbing quality with five-spice complexity creates an unforgettable sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 2 Sirloin steaks • 1 tablespoon Shaoshing rice wine • 2 teaspoons ground Sichuan pepper • 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce • ½ teaspoon Chinese five-spice powder • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoon groundnut oil • 1 medium red chilli • ½ onion • 1 small handful broccoli • 1 small handful chopped mangetout • 1 small handful chopped carrots • 1 small handful chopped babycorn • 300 ml hot fresh beef stock • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon cornflour • 1 spring onion • Salt and ground white pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Sichuan Prawns in Chilli Sauce
recipes.overview: Sichuan cooking is becoming increasingly popular in Western restaurants, and this is one of the best-known dishes from that region. Quick and easy to execute, it makes a wholesome and delicious meal when served with stir-fried vegetables and steamed rice. The vibrant sauce perfectly complements the firm texture of prawns.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams prawns • 2 teaspoons groundnut oil • 2 teaspoons fresh ginger • 1 tablespoon spring onions • 2 teaspoons tomato purée • ½ teaspoon chilli powder • ¼ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon sugar • ¼ teaspoon sesame oil

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Sizzling Rice Prawns
recipes.overview: This is a dramatic dish sure to earn you compliments. Moderately easy to make but requiring organisation and some Chinese cooking experience. The key to success is that both the prawn sauce mixture and rice cake must be fairly hot, this creates a dramatic, theatrical sizzle when they combine. A showstopping presentation perfect for entertaining.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams prawns • 2 tablespoons groundnut oil • 2 teaspoons ginger • 1½ tablespoons spring onions • 110 grams red or green pepper • 1 tablespoon cider or black rice vinegar • 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce • 2 dried red chillies • 1½ tablespoons tomato purée • 1 teaspoon light soy sauce • 1½ tablespoons dry sherry or rice wine • 1 teaspoon sugar • 300 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon cornflour • 1 rice cake • 1 litre groundnut oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Spicy Chicken with Peanuts
recipes.overview: This is a classic western Chinese dish better known in China as Gongbao chicken (or Kung Pao chicken). Named after Chinese official Ding Baozhen, Governor of Sichuan province in the nineteenth century, it represents the region's bold approach to flavour: spicy, slightly sour, sweet, and savoury all at once. The combination of roasted peanuts and dried chilli creates an unforgettable sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams boneless chicken breasts • 1 dried red chilli • 1½ tablespoons oil • 75 grams peanuts • 1 tablespoon Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon dry sherry • 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1 teaspoon garlic • 2 teaspoons spring onions • ½ teaspoon fresh ginger • 1 teaspoon white rice vinegar • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon sesame oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Steamed Beef Meatballs
recipes.overview: The secret to light and fluffy meatballs lies in proper technique: egg white and cornflour incorporate air into the mixture, creating a delicate texture. This gentle steaming method keeps them moist and tender while the aromatics infuse throughout. These meatballs reheat beautifully by steaming and are perfect for dinners or as an appetizer at parties.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams minced beef • 1 egg white • 1 tablespoon very cold water • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper • 2 teaspoons sesame oil • 1 tablespoon fresh coriander • 1½ tablespoons spring onions • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1 teaspoon sugar

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Steamed Fish
recipes.overview: Steaming is a great southern Chinese tradition and is considered the preferred method of cooking fish as it brings out the purest flavours. Because it is such a gentle cooking technique, nothing masks the fresh taste of the fish, which remains moist and tender. Simple aromatics, ginger, garlic, and spring onion, infuse the fish without overwhelming its delicate character.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams firm white fish fillets • 1 teaspoon coarse sea salt • 1 tablespoon fresh ginger • 2 tablespoons spring onions • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon groundnut oil • 1 teaspoon sesame oil • 2 garlic cloves

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Stir-Fried Beef with Ginger
recipes.overview: This typically Cantonese dish is one of the quickest and tastiest ways to cook beef. The ginger adds a subtle and fragrant spiciness that enhances without overwhelming the tender beef. Freezing the beef before slicing is essential for uniform, thin strips that cook instantly.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams lean beef steak • ¼ teaspoon salt • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons dry sherry • ½ teaspoon sesame oil • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1 slice fresh ginger • 1 tablespoon oil • 1 tablespoon Chinese chicken stock • ½ teaspoon sugar

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Stir-Fried Beef with Orange
recipes.overview: This is a northern Chinese beef speciality that lends itself to using dried tangerine peel. The Chinese always use peel that has been dried, and the older the peel, the more prized the flavour. The combination creates a sophisticated, citrus-forward sauce that balances the richness of beef beautifully.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams lean beef steak • 2 teaspoon dark soy sauce • 2 teaspoons dry sherry • 1 teaspoon fresh ginger • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 1 teaspoon sesame oil • 70 ml groundnut oil • 2 dried red chillies • 1 tablespoon dried tangerine peel • ½ teaspoon roasted Sichuan peppercorns • 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce • ¼ teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon sugar • ½ teaspoon sesame oil

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Stir-Fried Pork with Spring Onions
recipes.overview: This simple stir-fried dish in the southern Chinese tradition showcases how elemental technique and timing create exceptional results. The key to success is not to overcook the pork. A brief marinade tenderizes the meat and infuses flavour, while spring onions added late retain their fresh bite and delicate onion flavour.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams boneless lean pork • 2 teaspoons dry sherry • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • ½ teaspoon cornflour • 2 teaspoons oil • 4 spring onions • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon sugar

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Sweet and Sour Pork
recipes.overview: Often mis-made in the West as doughy balls drowned in cloying sauce, properly prepared sweet and sour pork is a delicate balance of flavours, neither strictly sweet nor sour, but harmoniously combined. The key is quality ingredients, proper deep-frying technique, and a refined sauce that complements rather than masks the tender pork. Tinned lychees add authentic sweetness and texture.
recipes.ingredients: 350 grams lean pork • 1 tablespoon dry sherry • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 egg • 2 tablespoons cornflour • 400 ml groundnut oil • 50 grams green pepper • 50 grams red pepper • 50 grams carrots • 50 grams spring onions • 150 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • ½ teaspoon salt • 1½ teaspoons cider vinegar • 1 tablespoon sugar • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 1 teaspoon cornflour • 75 grams tinned lychees

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sweet and Sour Prawns
recipes.overview: This is a very popular Chinese dish where the sweet and pungent flavours of the sauce combine beautifully with firm, succulent prawns. Simple to make and elegant enough for entertaining, it can be served as part of a larger Chinese meal or as a standalone starter. The balance of sweet, sour, spicy, and savoury creates an unforgettable sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 225 grams prawns • 110 grams tinned or fresh water chestnuts • 75 grams red or green pepper • 2 spring onions • 2 teaspoons groundnut oil • 2 teaspoons garlic • 70 ml Chinese chicken stock • 1 tablespoon dry sherry or rice wine • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 1 tablespoon cider vinegar • 1 tablespoon sugar • 2 teaspoons cornflour

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: A cuisine of regional variety, spanning Cantonese restraint, Sichuan numbing heat, Hunan smoke and northern wheat tradition. Aromatics lean on garlic, ginger and spring onion, with depth from soy, rice wine, sesame oil and fermented bean pastes. Wok-based stir-frying, steaming, red-braising and dough work for dumplings and noodles dominate the kitchen.

subcategories.label: Colombian

recipes.title: Ajiaco
recipes.overview: Chicken poaches with onion, garlic and herbs into a clean, golden broth. The first potato (sabanera, or floury maris piper) drops in to start; the small yellow papa criolla follows to break down and thicken; pastusa or red potatoes go in last to hold shape. Cobs of sweet corn cook in the same pot. Guascas — the dish's signature herb — adds at the very end. Each bowl tops with shredded chicken, avocado, capers and a generous spoon of cream; rice on the side.
recipes.ingredients: 1.5 kg chicken pieces • 1 large onion • 6 garlic cloves • 4 spring onions • 1 small bunch coriander stems • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 2 teaspoons salt • 2.5 litres water • 600 g sabanera or maris piper potatoes • 600 g papa criolla • 600 g pastusa or red-skinned potatoes • 3 ears sweet corn • 4 tablespoons dried guascas • A small bunch coriander • 2 ripe avocados • 4 tablespoons capers • 200 ml double cream or crème fraîche • 1 lime • Cooked white rice

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Arepas de Queso
recipes.overview: Masarepa (pre-cooked, instant white maize flour — sold as "masarepa", "harina pan", or "P.A.N."), warm water and salt mix to a soft dough that rests 5 minutes to hydrate. Cheese folds in; the dough shapes into discs about 1 cm thick. They cook in a dry hot pan first to set the surface, then receive a thin coat of butter and cook to deep golden on both sides. The cheese melts inside.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g pre-cooked white maize flour • 1¼ teaspoons salt • 500 ml warm water • 200 g mild mozzarella • 30 g unsalted butter • Extra butter • Hogao or guacamole

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Brevas con Arequipe
recipes.overview: Whole brown figs simmer slowly in a syrup of brown sugar, water, cinnamon and cloves until they're tender and the syrup has thickened to glossy. Off the heat, each fig gets cut almost through and stuffed with a generous spoonful of arequipe. Served warm or chilled, with extra syrup spooned over.
recipes.ingredients: 12 fresh brown figs • 250 g panela or dark brown sugar • 500 ml water • 1 cinnamon stick • 4 cloves • 1 strip orange peel • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 250 g arequipe • 200 g queso fresco or mild feta • A few pistachios

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Colombian cooking is regional and varied: bandeja paisa from Antioquia, ajiaco from Bogotá, sancocho along the coasts. Maize is foundational — arepas at every meal, mazamorra at breakfast, tamales for celebrations. Plantain (green and ripe), beans, rice, beef and chicken anchor most plates; aji (a coriander-and-chilli table sauce) and arequipe (Colombian dulce de leche) sit at opposite ends of the meal. Hogao (the tomato-onion sofrito) starts nearly every savoury dish.

subcategories.label: Creole

recipes.title: Bananas Foster
recipes.overview: Brown sugar and butter melt in a wide pan; bananas split lengthwise then halved go in cut-side down; cinnamon and a splash of banana liqueur (or extra rum) join. Dark rum pours in and flames briefly — until burnt off. The pan-syrup, slightly thickened, spoons over generous scoops of vanilla ice cream.
recipes.ingredients: 4 firm-ripe bananas • 80 g unsalted butter • 100 g light brown sugar • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ¼ teaspoon ground nutmeg • 4 tablespoons banana liqueur • 6 tablespoons dark rum • 4 generous scoops vanilla ice cream

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Maque Choux
recipes.overview: Onion and pepper soften in butter; corn kernels (cut from the cob, with the milky pulp scraped after) join in; tomato adds for body. The lot stews 25 minutes until the corn is tender and the mixture is thick. A splash of cream finishes; spring onion and hot sauce on top.
recipes.ingredients: 8 ears fresh corn • 60 g unsalted butter • 1 large onion • 1 green pepper • 1 red pepper • 4 garlic cloves • 2 medium tomatoes or 200 g tinned chopped • 1 long green chilli or ½ teaspoon cayenne • 1 teaspoon Creole seasoning • 1 teaspoon sugar • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 100 ml double cream • 4 spring onions • A small handful of flat-leaf parsley • Hot sauce
ingredientNames: medium tomatoes or 200 g tinned chopped
long green chilli or ½ teaspoon cayenne

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Shrimp Creole
recipes.overview: The trinity (onion, celery, green pepper) softens in butter; garlic, Creole seasoning and herbs join. Tomato paste deepens; chopped tomatoes and stock loosen; the lot simmers 20 minutes into a thick sauce. Prawns drop in at the end and cook 3 minutes — pink and just-set. Hot sauce, lemon and parsley to finish.
recipes.ingredients: 50 g unsalted butter • 1 large onion • 3 celery sticks • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes • 400 ml seafood, chicken or vegetable stock • 2 bay leaves • 4 sprigs fresh thyme • 2 teaspoons Creole seasoning • 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 tablespoon hot sauce • ½ teaspoon salt • 600 g raw prawns • Juice of half a lemon • 4 spring onions • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Cooked white rice • Extra hot sauce

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: New Orleans Creole cooking, refined and city-bred, distinct from rural Cajun. French, Spanish, African and Caribbean strands fold together; tomato is welcome (where it isn't in Cajun), butter and cream do heavy work, and seafood (shrimp, crab, oyster) carries the table. Holy trinity (onion, celery, pepper), Creole seasoning, hot sauce and slow simmers define dishes like shrimp creole, jambalaya, étouffée and bananas Foster.

subcategories.label: Cuban

recipes.title: Cuban-Style Pork Burgers
recipes.overview: These Cuban-style burgers are a vibrant fusion of Spanish and Cuban influences, featuring tender pork patties infused with spicy chorizo, aromatic garlic, and fresh peppers. The patties are griddled to perfection and served with a distinctive potato filling and creamy dressing that makes them distinctly Cuban. The combination of flavors, savory, spicy, and tangy, makes this a signature Cuban comfort food.
recipes.ingredients: 27 grams Spanish chorizo • 454 grams pork mince • 36 grams dried bread crumbs • 1 large egg • 1 small yellow onion • 50 grams green bell pepper • 1 clove garlic • 1/4 tsp. kosher salt • 6 hamburger buns • 6 Tbsp. thousand island dressing • 300 grams potatoes • 6 lettuce leaves

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Moros y Cristianos
recipes.overview: Black beans simmer with bay and oregano until tender. Sofrito (onion, pepper, garlic, cumin) cooks in olive oil; the cooked beans plus their liquid join in; rice is stirred through and steamed in the bean broth until tender and tinted.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g dried black beans • 1 bay leaf • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large onion • 1 green pepper • 6 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika • 250 g long-grain rice • 600 ml vegetable stock • 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar • Salt and black pepper • Fresh coriander

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Plátanos Maduros
recipes.overview: Very ripe (black-spotted, almost fully black) plantains are sliced thick on the diagonal and pan-fried in oil. The natural sugars caramelise; the inside softens to spoonable. A pinch of salt at the end balances the sweetness.
recipes.ingredients: 3 very ripe plantains • 4 tablespoons vegetable or sunflower oil • A pinch of flaky sea salt • A pinch of ground cinnamon

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Yuca con Mojo
recipes.overview: Cassava boils in salted water until a knife slides through easily. Garlic is crushed to a coarse paste with salt; sour orange juice (or lime + orange) mixes in; hot olive oil is poured over, blooming the garlic. The drained yuca goes into a serving dish; the mojo gets poured generously on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg cassava • 1 tablespoon salt • 8 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon dried oregano • 100 ml olive oil • Juice of 2 sour oranges • 1 small onion • Black pepper • Fresh parsley or coriander

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Cuba on a plate, where Spanish, African and Caribbean strands are woven together with rice, beans, pork and citrus. Sofrito (onion, garlic, pepper, cumin), oregano and bay set the base, while sour-orange mojo brightens roasted and braised meats. Long gentle braising sits alongside griddled and fried preparations.

subcategories.label: Egyptian

recipes.title: Basbousa
recipes.overview: A simple batter of semolina, yogurt, sugar, butter and coconut bakes in a shallow tray. The classic move: cut into diamonds before baking so the syrup soaks through the cuts later. Hot syrup pours over the warm cake and disappears in seconds, leaving the surface tacky-glossy. Topped with a single almond on each diamond.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g fine semolina • 200 g desiccated coconut • 300 g caster sugar • 200 g plain yogurt • 200 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon baking powder • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract • 4 tablespoons milk • About 24 blanched almonds • 400 g caster sugar • 250 ml water • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 1 tablespoon orange blossom water

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Ful Medames
recipes.overview: Whole dried fava beans soak overnight, then simmer slowly until the skins soften and the beans are completely tender — they'll break down on the edges, stay whole at the heart. Garlic, lemon, cumin and salt mash through; a generous pour of olive oil at the end. Tomato, onion, parsley and a hard-boiled egg or two on top.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g dried fava beans or 2 x 400 g tins • 1 bay leaf • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon ground coriander • 6 garlic cloves • Juice of 2 lemons • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon hot paprika or chilli flakes • 1 teaspoon salt • 5 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • 4 hard-boiled eggs • 2 medium tomatoes • 1 small red onion • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 4 tablespoons tahini sauce • Pickled chillies • Warm pita or aysh baladi
ingredientNames: fava beans or 2 x 400 g tins

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Hawawshi
recipes.overview: Mince mixes raw with finely chopped onion, garlic, parsley, chilli and Egyptian spices. The mixture spreads onto flatbread and folds shut; an egg-and-oil wash glazes the surface; a hot oven (or skillet) crisps the bread while the filling cooks through. The spice blend — cumin, coriander, cinnamon, allspice, sumac, paprika — is the dish's signature.
