recipes.ingredients: 500 g minced beef • 1 large onion • 6 garlic cloves • 1 long green chilli • 1 small bunch flat-leaf parsley • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1½ teaspoons ground coriander • 1 teaspoon ground allspice • 1 teaspoon paprika • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1½ teaspoons salt • 4 large pita breads or thin flatbreads • 1 large egg • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 teaspoon sesame seeds • Tahini sauce • Pickled chillies and pickled turnips • Tomato and cucumber salad
ingredientNames: large pita breads or thin flatbreads

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Ancient cooking that runs from street food to home stews. Ful medames (slow-cooked fava beans) is breakfast for millions; koshari (rice, lentils, pasta, chickpeas, fried onion) is lunch; mahshi (stuffed vegetables) is dinner. Cumin, coriander, garlic and tomato anchor the seasoning; aysh baladi (Egyptian flatbread) accompanies every meal; basbousa and umm ali finish.

subcategories.label: Ethiopian

recipes.title: Atakilt Wat
recipes.overview: Onions soften in oil with turmeric. Garlic, ginger and a small amount of berbere season. Carrots and potatoes go in first to soften; cabbage joins later. Steam-cooks under a lid until tender; the volume halves and the flavours fuse.
recipes.ingredients: 2 medium onions • 60 ml vegetable oil • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 1 teaspoon berbere • 3 medium carrots • 3 medium potatoes • ½ medium green cabbage • 1 teaspoon salt • 100 ml water • Black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Doro Wat
recipes.overview: Onions cook down for nearly an hour into a deep dark base — this is the foundation of doro wat and shouldn't be rushed. Berbere (Ethiopian spice blend) and niter kibbeh fold in. Chicken thighs and legs simmer in the sauce; hard-boiled eggs join late, taking on the deep red colour. Eaten communally from a single platter with injera; tears of injera scoop the stew.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g unsalted butter • 2 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 1 small onion • 1 teaspoon fenugreek seeds • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • 1 cinnamon stick • 4 cardamom pods • 4 large red onions • 4 tablespoons niter kibbeh • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 6 garlic cloves • 4 tablespoons berbere spice mix • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 100 ml dry red wine • 500 ml chicken stock • 1 kg chicken thighs and drumsticks • Juice of 1 lemon • 6 hard-boiled eggs • Salt • 4-6 sheets of injera

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Misir Wat
recipes.overview: Onions cook slowly in oil or niter kibbeh until they melt and turn jammy. Berbere blooms in. Tomato paste deepens; lentils and water simmer until soft and the stew thickens. Lemon juice at the end brightens.
recipes.ingredients: 3 large onions • 60 ml vegetable oil • 6 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 3 tablespoons berbere spice mix • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 250 g red lentils • 1 litre vegetable stock • 1 teaspoon salt • Juice of half a lemon • Black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Shiro Wat
recipes.overview: Onion melts in oil; berbere and tomato bloom in; water joins. Chickpea flour is whisked through, then cooked steadily, stirring, until thick and silky. The longer it cooks, the richer it tastes.
recipes.ingredients: 2 large onions • 60 ml vegetable oil • 5 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 2 tablespoons berbere • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 600 ml hot water • 100 g chickpea flour • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Communal eating built on injera (a sourdough teff flatbread) torn to scoop spiced stews called wot. Berbere (a complex chilli-spice blend) and niter kibbeh (a spiced clarified butter) drive the flavour, alongside fenugreek, korarima and a long table tradition of shared platters. Slow-cooked stews of chicken, lamb, lentils and split peas dominate; the meal eats from a single round mesob with no cutlery.

subcategories.label: Filipino

recipes.title: Adobong Sitaw
recipes.overview: Garlic browns in oil; long beans toss in to colour briefly. Soy and vinegar pour in with bay and peppercorns; the beans braise covered until tender. Lid off; the liquid reduces to a glaze. Salt at the end, not the start, since soy is salty enough.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g long beans • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 8 garlic cloves • 1 small onion • 60 ml light soy sauce • 60 ml white cane vinegar • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon whole black peppercorns • 200 ml water • 1 teaspoon sugar • Cooked rice

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Chicken Adobo
recipes.overview: Chicken thighs and drumsticks simmer in a 1:1:1 mix of soy sauce, vinegar and water with garlic, peppercorns and bay. The chicken cooks through; the sauce reduces; the meat browns at the edges. That's the entire dish. Served over rice with a few extra spoonfuls of sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg chicken thighs and drumsticks • 100 ml soy sauce • 100 ml white wine vinegar • 100 ml water • 6 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 3 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • Cooked white rice, to serve • 2 spring onions

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Ginataang Gulay
recipes.overview: Garlic, onion and ginger soften in oil; coconut milk adds; squash goes in first to start softening. Long beans and aubergine join; everything simmers until the squash is tender and the sauce has thickened. Soy sauce or salt finishes.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons coconut oil • 1 medium onion • 6 garlic cloves • 3 cm fresh ginger • 500 g kabocha squash or butternut • 400 ml coconut milk • 200 ml water or vegetable stock • 1 small aubergine • 200 g long beans • 2 tablespoons light soy sauce • 1 long red chilli • Black pepper • Cooked rice

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Kare-Kare
recipes.overview: Oxtail simmers slow with aromatics until the meat is falling-tender. The cooking liquid thickens with toasted ground rice and peanut butter, gets coloured with annatto, and becomes a deep golden sauce. Vegetables join briefly at the end so they keep their texture. Bagoong on the side is non-negotiable.
recipes.ingredients: 1.5 kg oxtail • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 2 litres water • Salt • 4 tablespoons annatto seeds — or 1 teaspoon paprika as substitute • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 onion • 6 garlic cloves • 200 g unsweetened peanut butter • 4 tablespoons toasted ground rice • 600 ml of the oxtail cooking liquid • 200 g green beans • 1 small aubergine • 1 bunch pak choi , separated into leaves • 4 spring onions • Cooked white rice • Bagoong — find at any Filipino or Asian shop
ingredientNames: annatto seeds — or 1 teaspoon paprika as substitute
bagoong — find at any filipino or asian shop

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Lumpia Shanghai
recipes.overview: A pork-mince filling with carrot, onion, garlic, soy and pepper rolls tightly into thin spring-roll wrappers, finger-sized. Deep-fries golden in 4 minutes. The wrappers are key — Filipino lumpia wrappers are thinner than Chinese spring-roll skins and crisp differently.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g pork mince • 1 small onion • 1 carrot • 4 garlic cloves • 2 spring onions • 1 large egg • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 teaspoon ground white pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon sugar • 2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil • 30 lumpia wrappers • 1 small bowl of water • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • 4 tablespoons sweet chilli sauce • 1 tablespoon rice vinegar • 1 teaspoon soy sauce

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Pancit Canton
recipes.overview: Aromatics fry, then chicken and prawns sauté fast. Vegetables and Chinese sausage join. The noodles soak briefly to soften, then go in the wok with sauce and stock to absorb everything in 5 minutes. Lemon or kalamansi at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g pancit canton noodles • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 200 g chicken thigh • 200 g raw prawns • 2 Chinese sausages • 1 carrot • 1 red pepper • 100 g green beans • ¼ small cabbage • 4 spring onions • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 tablespoons oyster sauce • 1 tablespoon fish sauce • 600 ml chicken stock • ½ teaspoon ground black pepper • Lemon or kalamansi wedges, to serve

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sinigang na Baboy
recipes.overview: Pork ribs simmer with onion, tomato and tamarind to build broth and tartness. Vegetables go in by cooking time: hardy ones early (taro, daikon), tender ones late (beans, water spinach). Fish sauce seasons; chilli gives optional heat. Served with rice.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg pork spare ribs or pork belly chunks • 1 onion • 2 tomatoes • 1.5 litres water • 4 tablespoons tamarind paste • 2 tablespoons fish sauce • 1 small daikon radish or 1 mooli • 1 small taro root • 200 g green beans or yard-long beans • 200 g okra • 1 long green chilli or jalapeño • 1 bunch water spinach or regular spinach • Cooked white rice, to serve • Lime or kalamansi wedges, to serve
ingredientNames: pork spare ribs or pork belly chunks
bunch water spinach or regular spinach

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Tortang Talong
recipes.overview: Aubergines are charred whole over flame until completely soft and the skin blackened. The skin peels away; the flesh stays intact on the stem. Eggs are beaten with a little salt; the aubergine is dipped flat into the eggs and slid into a hot pan; more egg is poured around it and the lot fries until set.
recipes.ingredients: 2 medium aubergines • 4 large eggs • ½ teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • Banana ketchup or chilli sauce • Steamed rice • Soy sauce mixed with a squeeze of calamansi or lemon
ingredientNames: soy sauce mixed with a squeeze of calamansi or lemon

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: A cuisine layered by Spanish, Chinese, American and Malay influences over its native base. Fish sauce, soy, vinegar and calamansi citrus drive the seasoning; coconut, garlic and tamarind anchor the bowl. Defining dishes include adobo (vinegar-soy braising), sinigang (sour soup), kare-kare (peanut stew) and pancit noodles, with rice at every meal and lechon at the centre of celebrations.

subcategories.label: French

recipes.title: Beef Bourguignon (Boeuf Bourguignon)
recipes.overview: Beef Bourguignon is the quintessential French braise, tender beef cubes slow-cooked in a Burgundy wine sauce enriched with pearl onions, mushrooms, and lardons. The combination of wine, beef stock, and a delicate flour thickening creates a deeply savory sauce that clings to the meat. This dish exemplifies classic French technique: patient cooking, careful skimming, and the balance of richness with restraint.
recipes.ingredients: 1 bottle of red wine • 750 ml beef broth • Peanut oil • 60 grams butter • 900 grams rump pot roast • 2 medium carrots • 2 medium onions • 2 tablespoons flour • 1 bouquet garni • 2 cloves garlic • Crushed pepper • Salt to taste • 16 small cippoline onions • 1 teaspoon sea salt • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • 125 grams lardons • 150 grams button cup mushrooms • 1 tablespoon parsley

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Blanquette de Veau
recipes.overview: Veal shoulder or breast simmers in white stock with onion, carrot and bouquet garni until tender. The strained stock is reduced and bound with a cream-yolk liaison; mushrooms and pearl onions cooked separately go in at the end. Served with rice or buttered noodles.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg veal shoulder or breast • 1.5 litres white veal or chicken stock • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 1 leek • 1 bouquet garni • ½ teaspoon white peppercorns • Salt • 200 g pearl onions or small shallots • 250 g button mushrooms • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 50 g unsalted butter • 50 g plain flour • 200 ml double cream • 2 large egg yolks • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • A grating of nutmeg • Salt and white pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Bouillabaisse
recipes.overview: A fish stock built from heads and bones is enriched with tomato, fennel, leek, saffron and orange peel. Firm fish go in first, delicate fish later. Rouille (a saffron-garlic mayonnaise) gets spread on toasted baguette and floated on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg fish heads, bones and trimmings • 1 leek • 1 fennel bulb • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tin chopped tomatoes • 2 bay leaves • A few thyme sprigs • 1 strip of orange peel • A pinch of saffron threads • 1 teaspoon fennel seeds • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 200 ml dry white wine • 1.2 litres water • Firm: monkfish, gurnard, john dory • Medium: red mullet, sea bass • Delicate: cod, hake • Optional: prawns, mussels • 1 thick slice white bread • 4 garlic cloves • 1 small red chilli • 1 egg yolk • A pinch of saffron • 200 ml extra virgin olive oil • Salt • 1 baguette • 1 garlic clove

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Breast of Lamb with Mustard and Herb Crust
recipes.overview: This composed dish showcases lamb breast's rich potential: the trimmed meat is braised until tender in an herb-infused stock, then sliced and topped with a crispy herb and mustard breadcrumb crust. The braising liquid becomes an elegant sauce thickened with cream and sharpened with mustard, providing a sophisticated accompaniment that complements both the meat and crust.
recipes.ingredients: 4 portions of lamb breast • 2 tablespoons lamb fat or cooking oil • 50 grams unsalted butter • 1 garlic clove • 1 sprig fresh thyme • 1 sprig fresh rosemary • 2 sage leaves • 1 onion • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • Half leek • 1.2 litres chicken stock • Lamb bones • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 6 slices white bread • 25 grams butter • 2 shallots • 1 teaspoon chopped mixed thyme, sage, and rosemary • 2 teaspoons Dijon or grain mustard • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 150 ml double cream • 2 teaspoons Dijon or grain mustard
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Cassoulet
recipes.overview: Dried haricot beans soak overnight, then simmer with bacon and aromatics. Pork shoulder and sausages brown separately. The lot layers in a casserole with confit duck legs, more stock, and a breadcrumb top that cooks into the surface as it bakes long and slow.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g dried haricot or cannellini beans • 200 g pork belly or pancetta in one piece • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 bouquet garni • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 1.5 litres chicken or duck stock • 4 confit duck legs • 4 Toulouse sausages • 600 g boneless pork shoulder • 2 tablespoons duck fat • 100 g coarse breadcrumbs • 2 tablespoons chopped flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: pork belly or pancetta in one piece

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Coq au Vin
recipes.overview: Lardons rendered, chicken pieces browned in the fat, then simmered in red wine with brandy, stock, garlic, herbs and tomato purée. Mushrooms and pearl onions are sautéed separately and added at the end so they keep their shape and texture.
recipes.ingredients: 1 chicken , cut into 8 pieces • 2 tablespoons plain flour, seasoned with salt and pepper • 200 g smoked bacon lardons • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 4 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 750 ml red wine • 50 ml brandy • 400 ml chicken stock • 1 bouquet garni • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 30 g unsalted butter • 200 g pearl onions or shallots • 250 g chestnut mushrooms • 2 tablespoons chopped flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Duck Confit
recipes.overview: Duck legs sit overnight under salt, garlic and herbs to season and draw out moisture. They then cook completely submerged in duck fat at 90°C for 3 hours. The cooked legs and the fat keep for weeks. To serve, lift legs from the fat and crisp the skin in a dry hot pan.
recipes.ingredients: 4 duck legs • 2 tablespoons coarse sea salt • 4 garlic cloves • 4 thyme sprigs • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 2 bay leaves • 1 kg duck fat

winePairings.note: Earthy, medium-bodied reds match game's depth without overpowering.

recipes.title: Lamb Stew (Navarin d'Agneau)
recipes.overview: Navarin d'agneau is a classic French spring stew, combining three different cuts of lamb, shoulder, neck, and breast, for complex flavor and varied texture. The meat braises in a light tomato and stock base, enriched with aromatic vegetables and finished with glazed pearl onions and tender potatoes. The balance of tender vegetables and well-cooked lamb creates a rustic yet refined dish that exemplifies peasant cooking elevated to art.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil • 1 kilogram lamb shoulder • 500 grams lamb neck • 500 grams lamb breast • 3 carrots • 3 onions • 30 grams flour • 1 bouquet garni • 4 cloves garlic • 1 large tomato • Sea salt and pepper to taste • 1.2 kilogram small, firm-fleshed potatoes • 15 grams butter • 250 grams small new pearl or cippoline onions • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • Fine salt • 2 tablespoons flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: small new pearl or cippoline onions

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Normandy Chicken
recipes.overview: Normandy Chicken showcases the bounty of France's northern apple region, combining succulent chicken legs braised in dry cider with caramelized apples, creamy crème fraîche, and aromatic tarragon. The sauce is silky-smooth without being heavy, balanced between the slight tartness of cider and the sweetness of apples, finished with a hint of Dijon mustard that adds complexity without overpowering.
recipes.ingredients: 6 chicken legs • 40 grams butter • 8 shallots • 3 sticks celery , plus leaves for garnish • 300 ml dry cider • 300 ml chicken stock • 2 apples • 2 teaspoons cornflour • 5 tablespoons crème fraîche • 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard • 1 tablespoon chopped tarragon • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Quiche aux Légumes
recipes.overview: Shortcrust pastry blind-bakes to a deep gold (essential — soggy bottom is a quiche's main enemy). Vegetables sauté separately to soften and dry; the custard mixes; everything assembles in the shell and bakes until set with a faint wobble in the centre.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g plain flour • 125 g cold unsalted butter • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 large egg yolk • 3-4 tablespoons cold water • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 large leek • 1 medium courgette • 200 g spinach • 1 garlic clove • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves • Salt and black pepper • 4 large eggs • 200 ml double cream • 100 ml whole milk • 150 g gruyère • A grating of nutmeg • Salt and black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Quiche Lorraine
recipes.overview: Buttery shortcrust pastry blind-baked first to set the base, then filled with rendered smoked lardons, grated gruyère and a rich egg-cream custard, baked at moderate heat until just set with a slight wobble in the centre.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g plain flour • 100 g cold unsalted butter • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 large egg yolk • 2-3 tablespoons ice-cold water • 200 g smoked bacon lardons • 200 g gruyère cheese • 4 large eggs • 200 ml double cream • 200 ml whole milk • A grating of fresh nutmeg • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Ratatouille
recipes.overview: Each vegetable browns in olive oil in batches so it keeps its texture; they meet in a pan with garlic, herbs and tomato to merge into a glossy stew. The slow-cooked tomato base provides the binder; the discrete vegetables provide the structure.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large aubergine • 2 courgettes • 1 red pepper • 1 yellow pepper • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 4 ripe tomatoes or 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 6 tablespoons olive oil • 2 sprigs fresh thyme • 2 sprigs fresh rosemary • 1 bay leaf • A handful of fresh basil • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: ripe tomatoes or 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes
salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Soupe au Pistou
recipes.overview: A vegetable soup of cannellini beans, kidney beans, courgette, green beans, tomatoes and small pasta in a herb-scented broth. Pistou — Provençal pesto without nuts — gets pounded fresh in a mortar. Ladled into bowls; a generous spoonful of pistou stirred in just before eating.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large onion • 2 leeks • 3 carrots • 4 garlic cloves • 4 medium tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped • 1 bay leaf • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • 1 x 400 g tin cannellini beans • 1 x 400 g tin red kidney beans • 200 g green beans • 2 medium courgettes • 100 g small pasta • Salt and black pepper • 1 large bunch basil • 4 garlic cloves • 50 g parmesan • 5 tablespoons olive oil • ½ teaspoon salt
ingredientNames: medium tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped
x 400 g tin red kidney beans

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Steak Frites with Béarnaise
recipes.overview: The frites take the longest (peel, cut, soak, fry low, drain, fry high) so start there. Béarnaise wants a clarified butter and a tarragon-vinegar reduction whisked into egg yolks. The steak is the easy part: hot pan, butter and thyme baste, rest hard.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g Maris Piper potatoes • Vegetable oil or beef dripping for deep-frying • Sea salt • 2 rib-eye or sirloin steaks • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 30 g unsalted butter • 2 thyme sprigs • 1 garlic clove • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 4 tablespoons white wine vinegar • 4 tablespoons dry white wine • 1 shallot • 1 tablespoon fresh tarragon • 4 black peppercorns • 3 large egg yolks • 200 g unsalted butter • Salt • An extra tablespoon of fresh tarragon
ingredientNames: vegetable oil or beef dripping for deep-frying
salt and freshly ground black pepper
an extra tablespoon of fresh tarragon

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: The grammar of Western cooking: refined regional and bistro traditions built on butter, cream, herbs and stocks. Shallot, mustard, wine and brandy do much of the seasoning, with mother sauces and reductions giving every dish its finish. Slow braises (boeuf bourguignon, coq au vin), pan sauces and patisserie technique anchor the canon.

subcategories.label: German

recipes.title: Jägerschnitzel
recipes.overview: Pork loin escalopes pound thin, bread, and shallow-fry. While they crisp, a sauce of bacon, onion, mushrooms, white wine, stock and cream reduces in another pan. The schnitzel plates with the sauce on top (or alongside, if you want the crumb to stay crisp), with spätzle or boiled potatoes underneath.
recipes.ingredients: 4 pork loin escalopes • 100 g plain flour • 3 large eggs • 200 g fine breadcrumbs • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil + 30 g butter for shallow-frying • 100 g smoked bacon lardons • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 small onion • 2 garlic cloves • 400 g mixed mushrooms • 100 ml dry white wine • 250 ml chicken stock • 200 ml double cream • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Spätzle or boiled new potatoes
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
vegetable oil + 30 g butter for shallow-frying

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Käsespätzle
recipes.overview: Make spätzle as the base. Caramelise onions slowly in butter. Layer the spätzle with grated cheese (and a splash of cream) in a baking dish, top with the onions, and bake until bubbling. Serve with a green salad to cut through the richness.
recipes.ingredients: 1 batch fresh spätzle , or 700 g cooked • 4 large onions • 50 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • ½ teaspoon salt • 300 g emmental or gruyère • 100 g mature cheddar • 200 ml double cream • A grating of fresh nutmeg • Black pepper • A small bunch of chives • 30 g butter

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Maultaschen
recipes.overview: A simple egg-and-flour pasta dough rolls thin. The filling is spinach (frozen, thawed and squeezed dry), breadcrumbs soaked in milk, grated cheese, eggs, parsley and nutmeg. The dough rolls long; filling drops onto the bottom half; the top folds over and seals; the lot cuts into rectangles. Boiled briefly in vegetable broth or salted water; served in broth or pan-fried.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g plain flour • 4 large eggs • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 4-6 tablespoons cold water • 100 g stale white bread • 200 ml whole milk • 500 g frozen chopped spinach • 1 medium onion • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • 100 g gruyère or emmental • 50 g parmesan • 2 large eggs • A grating of nutmeg • A small bunch of parsley • Salt and black pepper • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • OR: 50 g butter + 1 large onion sliced • 4 fried eggs
ingredientNames: or: 50 g butter + 1 large onion sliced

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Sauerbraten
recipes.overview: A beef chuck or topside joint marinates 3-4 days in red wine vinegar, red wine and aromatics. The marinade strains, the meat browns deeply, and it braises slowly in the strained marinade with stock until tender. The braising liquid is finished with crushed gingersnaps for the signature sweet-thick sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 1.5 kg beef topside or chuck • 500 ml red wine vinegar • 500 ml dry red wine • 500 ml water • 2 onions • 2 carrots • 1 leek • 2 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon black peppercorns • 1 tablespoon mustard seeds • 4 cloves • 1 tablespoon juniper berries • 1 cinnamon stick • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 tablespoons plain flour • 500 ml beef stock • 80 g ginger snap biscuits • 2 tablespoons raisins • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Potato dumplings or buttered red cabbage • A tablespoon of chopped flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
potato dumplings or buttered red cabbage

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Spätzle
recipes.overview: A loose egg-rich batter rests briefly to relax the gluten. It presses through a spätzle press, colander, or grater into a pan of boiling salted water. The noodles cook in seconds, lift out, and toss with butter or build into other dishes.
recipes.ingredients: 300 g plain flour • 4 large eggs • 150 ml whole milk • 1 teaspoon salt • A grating of nutmeg • 50 g unsalted butter

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Wiener Schnitzel
recipes.overview: Veal escalopes are pounded to 5 mm thick, dredged through flour, egg and breadcrumbs (each step distinct), then shallow-fried fast in plenty of fat until the crumb puffs into characteristic ripples. Lemon and parsley potatoes alongside.
recipes.ingredients: 4 veal escalopes • 100 g plain flour • 3 large eggs • 200 g fine breadcrumbs • Salt and freshly ground white pepper • 100 g clarified butter • 1 lemon • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 600 g new potatoes • Lingonberry jam
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground white pepper

winePairings.note: A safe default — adjust by sauce, spice and richness.

subcategories.overview: Hearty cooking built on pork, sausage, sauerkraut, potato and rye, with regional variation across the Bavarian, Swabian and northern traditions. Caraway, juniper, mustard and dill carry the seasoning; vinegar and a pinch of sweetness balance the long-braised richness. Schnitzels pan-fried hot in butter, sauerbraten marinated for days, spätzle pressed fresh into boiling water and slow-fermented sourdough breads define the canon.

subcategories.label: Greek

recipes.title: Avgolemono
recipes.overview: A whole chicken poaches in water with onion, carrot and celery, building broth and tender meat at the same time. Rice (or orzo) cooks in the strained broth. Eggs and lemon juice whisk together, get tempered with hot broth, then stir back into the soup off the heat. The eggs thicken the broth into a velvet finish.
recipes.ingredients: 1 whole chicken or 4 chicken legs • 2 onions • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 2 litres water • 100 g long-grain rice or orzo • 3 large eggs • Juice of 2 lemons • Salt and freshly ground white pepper • A small bunch of dill
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground white pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Fasolada
recipes.overview: Soaked beans simmer slow with chopped vegetables, tomato and herbs in a generous pour of olive oil. Lemon juice goes in at the end to brighten. Served with a final drizzle of oil, maybe a sprinkle of feta and a heel of bread.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g dried cannellini or butter beans • 6 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • 4 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1.2 litres vegetable stock or water • Juice of 1 lemon • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 100 g feta
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Gemista
recipes.overview: Tomatoes and peppers get tops cut off and centres scooped out. The tomato flesh is reserved and chopped. A rice filling cooks half-done in a pan with the chopped tomato flesh, onion, garlic and herbs. Vegetables fill; tops go back on. Potato wedges arrange around them in a tray; everything bakes slowly in olive oil and the reserved tomato juices until the rice is cooked through and the potatoes are golden.
recipes.ingredients: 4 large beef tomatoes • 4 large red, yellow or green peppers • 4 medium potatoes • 5 tablespoons olive oil • 2 large onions • 4 garlic cloves • 200 g long-grain rice • 50 g pine nuts • 50 g currants • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • A small bunch of mint • A small bunch of dill • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 200 ml hot water • 1 teaspoon sugar • Salt and black pepper • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 teaspoon sugar • 200 ml hot water • 200 g feta • Crusty bread

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Lamb Gyros
recipes.overview: Lamb mince blends with onion, garlic, oregano, cumin and a bit of breadcrumb into a stiff paste. Packed into a loaf tin and roasted. Once cool enough to slice, the loaf is sliced very thin and pan-crisped to mimic shawarma. Pita, tzatziki, salad.
recipes.ingredients: 800 g lamb mince • 1 small onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • ½ teaspoon ground allspice • 2 teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 30 g fine breadcrumbs • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 6 pita breads • Tzatziki • 1 small red onion • 2 tomatoes • A small handful of fresh oregano or parsley • Optional: 200 g hot chips
ingredientNames: small handful of fresh oregano or parsley

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Moussaka
recipes.overview: Aubergines and potatoes pan-fry or roast separately. Lamb mince cooks down with onion, garlic, cinnamon and tomato into a rich ragù. The layers go into a deep dish, topped with a cheese-rich béchamel that sets golden in the oven.
recipes.ingredients: 2 large aubergines • 3 large potatoes • Olive oil • Salt • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 800 g lamb mince • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 150 ml red wine • 1 cinnamon stick • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • ¼ teaspoon ground allspice • 1 bay leaf • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 75 g unsalted butter • 75 g plain flour • 700 ml whole milk • 2 large eggs • 100 g grated kefalotyri or parmesan • A grating of nutmeg
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pastitsio
recipes.overview: Pasta tubes get a quick boil and a buttery toss. A beef ragù with cinnamon, allspice and a touch of tomato cooks while the pasta drains. Béchamel — thick, egg-bound, cheese-spiked — pours over the top. Bakes until the surface is mahogany-bronze.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g bucatini or ziti • 30 g unsalted butter • 50 g grated kefalotyri or parmesan • 1 egg • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 700 g beef mince • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 150 ml red wine • 1 cinnamon stick • ¼ teaspoon ground allspice • 1 bay leaf • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 75 g unsalted butter • 75 g plain flour • 800 ml whole milk • 2 large eggs • 100 g grated kefalotyri or parmesan • A grating of nutmeg
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pork Souvlaki
recipes.overview: Pork shoulder cubes marinate at least two hours in a lemon-olive oil-garlic-oregano mix. Threaded onto skewers and grilled hot. Three minutes a side; rest briefly; serve with the standard mezze.
recipes.ingredients: 800 g pork shoulder • 8 wooden skewers or metal • 100 ml olive oil • Juice of 2 lemons • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon dried Greek oregano • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 4 pita breads • Tzatziki • 1 red onion • 2 tomatoes • A handful of fresh oregano • Lemon wedges

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Spanakopita
recipes.overview: Spinach wilts and squeezes dry; mixes with crumbled feta, ricotta, eggs, dill and spring onions. The filling goes between layers of filo brushed with melted butter; baked until the top shatter-crisps.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg fresh spinach • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 1 small onion • 6 spring onions • 4 garlic cloves • 300 g feta • 200 g ricotta • 3 large eggs • A small bunch of dill • A small bunch of parsley • A grating of nutmeg • Salt and black pepper • 270 g filo pastry • 100 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons olive oil

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Cooking shaped by olive oil, lemon, oregano and the Mediterranean larder. Feta, halloumi and yogurt anchor the dairy side; lamb, octopus and white-fish dominate the protein; chickpeas and pulses ground the rest. Defining techniques include slow-braised giouvetsi and stifado, charcoal-grilled souvlaki, layered moussaka and pastitsio, and the daily-bread role of phyllo wrapped around savoury and sweet fillings.

subcategories.label: Hungarian

recipes.title: Chicken Paprikash
recipes.overview: Onions soften slow with sweet paprika, tomato joins, then chicken thighs braise in the sauce until tender. Sour cream stirs in off the heat for the velvety finish. Served over buttered egg noodles, with extra paprika sprinkled on top.
recipes.ingredients: 8 bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks • 3 tablespoons lard or vegetable oil • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 3 tablespoons Hungarian sweet paprika • ½ teaspoon Hungarian hot paprika • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 1 red pepper • 400 ml chicken stock • 200 ml soured cream • 2 tablespoons plain flour • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 400 g egg noodles or spätzle • 50 g unsalted butter
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Hungarian Goulash
recipes.overview: Beef chuck cubes brown briefly, then simmer slow in a generous onion base with paprika as the dominant spice. Tomato, garlic and caraway add depth; potatoes go in late. The defining flavour is the paprika — Hungarian sweet paprika, not generic Spanish; the colour and flavour are different.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg beef chuck • 3 tablespoons lard or vegetable oil • 4 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 4 tablespoons Hungarian sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon Hungarian hot paprika • 1 teaspoon caraway seeds • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 1 red pepper • 2 bay leaves • 1 litre beef stock • 600 g potatoes • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Hungarian Mushroom Soup
recipes.overview: Onions and mushrooms cook in butter until the mushrooms have released and reabsorbed their liquid. Paprika blooms off the heat; flour stirs in to thicken; vegetable stock joins. Dill and milk finish the soup; sour cream is whisked in just before serving with a squeeze of lemon.
recipes.ingredients: 50 g unsalted butter • 1 large onion • 600 g mixed mushrooms • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons sweet Hungarian paprika • 2 tablespoons plain flour • 1 litre vegetable stock • 200 ml whole milk • 3 tablespoons fresh dill • 200 ml soured cream • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • Juice of half a lemon • Salt and black pepper • A few extra dill fronds

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Lecsó
recipes.overview: Onion softens in oil; sweet paprika blooms off the heat (don't burn it). Strips of yellow pepper go in next to soften. Tomatoes follow; the lot stews until the peppers are silky and the sauce has reduced to a thick, jammy compote. Often finished with eggs or an egg yolk for richness.
recipes.ingredients: 4 tablespoons sunflower oil • 2 large onions • 1 tablespoon sweet Hungarian paprika • ½ teaspoon hot Hungarian paprika • 6 large yellow Hungarian peppers or yellow bell peppers • 4 large ripe tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes • 1 teaspoon sugar • Salt and black pepper • 2-4 large eggs
ingredientNames: large yellow hungarian peppers or yellow bell peppers
large ripe tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Tojásos Nokedli
recipes.overview: A loose batter of flour, egg and water gets pushed through a spätzle plane (or a colander with large holes) into salted boiling water; the dumplings cook in a minute and lift out. They go into a buttery pan; beaten eggs pour over and just-set as you fold them through. Salt, pepper, parsley.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g plain flour • 2 large eggs • 150 ml water • 1 teaspoon salt • 50 g unsalted butter • 4 large eggs • ½ teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 2 tablespoons fresh parsley

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Paprika-led cooking from Central Europe, where sweet, smoked and hot Hungarian paprika are entire ingredients in their own right. Onions cook deep and slow as the base of every stew; soured cream, dill and caraway shape the finish. Goulash and pörkölt (paprika braises), chicken paprikash, and the layered cake patisserie of Budapest define the tradition.

subcategories.label: Indian
Base

recipes.title: B.I.R Curry Base
recipes.ingredients: 4 kg Onions • 1 tsp cumin seeds • 3L Water • 500ml Vegetable oil • 2 tbsp salt • 2 tbsp turmeric • 1 rounded tsp garam masala • ½ tsp chilli powder • 30g coconut cream • 2tbs tomato purée

recipes.title: Pre-Cooked Chicken
recipes.ingredients: 2 Kg Chicken Breasts, cut into bite-sized chunks • 4 Tbsp Rapeseed oil • 5 Green Cardamom pods • 10 Black Peppercorns • 1 Inch piece of Cinnamon • 1 tsp Cumin seeds • 1 tsp coriander seeds • 3 Indian Bay leaves • 2 Large Onions • ½ tsp sea salt • 2 Tbsp Garlic and Ginger paste • 1 tsp ground turmeric • 2 x 400g tins chopped Tomatoes • Water • 1 tsp Garam Masala

recipes.title: Pre-Cooked Lamb
recipes.ingredients: 3 lbs Lamb, diced with fat and skin cut off • 2 onions, finely diced • 1 tbsp Cumin seeds • 2 tbsp Garlic/Ginger paste • 2 tbsp Tomato Purée • 1 tsp Coriander Powder • 1 tsp Chilli Powder • 1 tbsp Turmeric • 1 tbsp Methi • 1 tsp Garam Masala • 1 Curry leaf • 1 tsp Sugar • 1 tsp Salt • Handful of fresh Coriander, chopped • 300ml Vegetable Oil • Water

recipes.title: Red Masala Sauce
recipes.overview: A simple spiced yoghurt marinade used for tandoori-style dishes. Combines yoghurt with tandoori paste, warming spices, and a touch of lemon juice to create a vibrant red coating for chicken, lamb, or vegetables before grilling or roasting.
recipes.ingredients: 500 ml yoghurt • Pinch of chilli powder • Pinch of garam masala • Pinch of coriander powder • 1 tsp salt • 1 heaped tbsp Pataks tandoori paste • 1 level tsp garlic and ginger paste • 1 tbsp red food colouring • Few squirts of lemon juice
ingredientNames: level tsp garlic and ginger paste

subcategories.overview: Sauces, purées and base gravies that underpin Indian curries.

subcategories.label: Breads

recipes.title: Bengali Paratha
recipes.overview: A pleated, multi-layered Bengali flatbread laminated with ghee and studded with chillies, fresh herbs and freshly crushed black pepper. The dough is folded fan-style and rolled into a spiral coil so each paratha bakes into a stack of flaky leaves. Best eaten straight off the tawa with dal, a curry, or a bowl of yoghurt.
recipes.ingredients: 180 g whole wheat flour or maida • 1 tablespoon ghee or oil • ½ teaspoon salt • Warm water • 3 tablespoons chives • 2 tablespoons flat leaf parsley • 1-2 Thai bird's eye chillies • 1 teaspoon Tellicherry black peppercorns • ½ teaspoon salt • Ghee

recipes.title: Bhatura
recipes.overview: Soft, slightly leavened Indian flatbreads traditionally served with chole (chickpea curry). The addition of yoghurt gives bhatura weight and flavor, making them a street-food favorite.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tsp sugar • 2 tsp fast-action/instant active dried yeast • 175 ml warm water • 240 g plain/all-purpose flour, plus extra for rolling • 1½ tbsp plain yogurt • 1 tbsp melted ghee • Pinch of salt • Vegetable oil, for frying

recipes.title: Chilli Pooris
recipes.overview: Small deep-fried Indian flatbreads built on a half-and-half mix of plain and wholemeal flour, spiked with chilli powder and chopped fresh chilli. Each piece puffs in seconds in the hot oil and stays crisp for a few minutes off the heat. Best served straight from the pan alongside a north Indian curry or pickles and yoghurt.
recipes.ingredients: 115 g plain flour • 115 g wholemeal flour • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon mild chilli powder • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 fresh red chilli • 100 ml water • Vegetable oil

recipes.title: Missi Rotis
recipes.overview: A Punjabi-style flatbread built on a half-and-half mix of gram (chickpea) flour and wholemeal flour, kneaded with chopped chilli, onion, fresh coriander and turmeric. The result is a thicker, rougher roti than its plain wheat counterpart, with a savoury flavour that works as well alongside a curry as it does on its own with yoghurt.
recipes.ingredients: 115 g gram flour • 115 g wholemeal flour • 1 fresh green chilli • ½ onion • 1 tablespoon coriander • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon melted butter • 120 ml lukewarm water • 2 tablespoons melted butter

recipes.title: Naan
recipes.overview: A grill-cooked version of the traditional tandoor naan: large, light and slightly sweet, with a chewy crumb and a sesame and onion-seed crust. Yoghurt and a touch of sugar in the dough give it the soft, almost briochey texture that the tandoor's blast of heat usually produces. It's the wrap that should hold a kebab, or sit alongside a Balti on the table.
recipes.ingredients: 450 g strong white flour • 1 tablespoon baking powder • 1 tablespoon granulated sugar • 2 tablespoons Greek yoghurt • 1 teaspoon aromatic salt • 2 teaspoons sesame seeds • ½ teaspoon wild onion seed • Lukewarm water • A little ghee

recipes.title: Puris
recipes.overview: Deep-fried wholemeal flatbreads built on a tight, slightly drier dough than a roti so they hold their shape long enough to puff in the oil. Each disc puffs in seconds and stays crisp for a few minutes off the heat. Best served the moment they leave the pan, ideally with chole or another sauce-rich curry.
recipes.ingredients: 310 g wholemeal flour, plus extra for dusting • ½ teaspoon salt • 100 ml water, plus more as needed • Vegetable oil, for deep frying

recipes.title: Rotis
recipes.overview: A simple wholemeal flatbread that lives or dies on technique rather than ingredients. Rolled to even thinness and cooked first on a hot tawa, then puffed over a naked flame, a properly made roti separates into two thin sheets of soft bread. The right partner for almost any curry, dal or chutney.
recipes.ingredients: 380 g wholemeal flour • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 240 ml water, or as needed

subcategories.overview: Naans, rotis, parathas and pooris, leavened and unleavened.

recipes.title: Authentic Chicken Handi

recipes.title: Agra Ginger Chicken
recipes.overview: A light, cleansing chicken curry from Agra with fresh ginger, warm spices and bright tomato notes. This vibrant dish is designed to be accessible and fresh, with spinach and lime lifting the finished curry.
recipes.ingredients: 80 ml vegetable oil • 2 large white onions, halved and thinly sliced • 5 cm fresh root ginger, peeled and grated • 6 garlic cloves, minced • 4 green cardamom pods • 2 cinnamon sticks • 3 tbsp garam masala • ½ tsp ground turmeric • ¼ tsp chilli powder • 750 g bone-in chicken thighs, quartered • 400 g canned chopped tomatoes • 450 ml water • 1 tsp salt • 1 tsp sugar • 200 g baby spinach, washed • Zest of 1 lime • 1 small bunch fresh coriander/cilantro, leaves and stalks roughly chopped

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Angry Bird
recipes.overview: Simple tandoori-style chicken thighs marinated in yogurt and spices for tender, flavorful meat. Thighs or drumsticks work best; use mild paprika for color and flavor, or chilli powder for heat. Yogurt and lemon tenderize the meat, allowing spices to penetrate deeply.
recipes.ingredients: 8 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs • 500 g plain yogurt • ½ tsp ground turmeric • ½ tsp chilli powder • 1 large green chilli, deseeded and finely sliced • 2 tsp ground cumin • 2 tsp ground coriander • 1 tsp ground cinnamon • 2 tbsp paprika • ½ tsp ground cloves • 5 cm piece fresh root ginger, peeled and grated • 3 garlic cloves, minced • ½ tsp salt • Juice of ½ lemon

recipes.ingredients: 2 large chicken breasts, chopped into 2 cm pieces • 2 tsp crushed garlic • 1 tbsp ground cumin • 1 tbsp ground coriander • 2 tsp ground cinnamon • ½ tsp salt • Juice of ½ lemon • 1 egg, beaten • 380 g gram flour • Vegetable oil, for deep frying • 1 large red chilli, finely sliced • ½ red onion, finely sliced • 1 tbsp chopped fresh coriander • 2 tbsp Tamarind Chutney • 2 tbsp Green Chilli Pickle

recipes.title: Maa’s Lamb Chops
recipes.overview: Tender lamb chops marinated in spiced yogurt, evoking the aroma of family parties. Despite poor margins, this dish captures the essence of home and Mowgli's heritage. Grilled to perfection for a nostalgic, flavorful experience.
recipes.ingredients: 8 lamb chops, about 2.5 cm thick • 500 g plain yogurt • 2 tbsp ground cumin • 2 tbsp paprika • 1 tsp ground cloves • 1 tsp ground green cardamom • 1 tsp fenugreek powder • 1 tsp ground turmeric • Juice of 1 lemon • 4 garlic cloves, minced • 1 tbsp minced fresh root ginger • 2 tsp salt

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Mowgli Sticky Wings
recipes.overview: Crispy, sweet, and sticky chicken wings with a rich, fruity glaze from date syrup and treacle. These finger-licking wings blend dessert-like sweetness with savory spices, perfect for satisfying cravings at the intersection of sweet and savory.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg chicken wings • 4 tbsp date syrup • 2 tbsp black treacle • 3 garlic cloves, minced • 5 cm piece fresh root ginger, peeled and grated • 1 tsp garam masala • 2 tsp ground cumin • ½ tsp black mustard seeds • 4 tsp dark rum • Juice of ½ lemon • 2 tbsp white wine vinegar • 1 tsp salt

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Simple Lahori Chicken Curry
recipes.overview: A traditional Lahori-style chicken curry built on a base of caramelised onions, tomatoes, and whole spices bloomed in oil. Yoghurt is added gradually to prevent splitting, creating a rich, silky sauce. Finished with fresh coriander and lemon juice, this is a deeply flavourful North Indian classic.
recipes.ingredients: 1 whole chicken , cut into 8–10 pieces, skin removed • ¾ tsp turmeric, divided • ½ tsp cayenne pepper, divided • Salt, to taste • 1½ medium onions, roughly diced • 5 garlic cloves • 5 cm fresh ginger, peeled and halved crosswise • 45 ml canola oil, divided • 5 cm cinnamon stick • 12 green cardamom pods • 9 whole cloves • 9 black peppercorns • 2 large tomatoes, chopped • 30 ml tomato paste • 60 ml plain yogurt, whisked smooth • 240 ml water • 30 g fresh coriander, chopped • Juice of 1 lemon

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: Main-course curries: chicken, lamb and vegetable, across regional and curry-house styles.

subcategories.label: Pastes

recipes.title: Chasni Paste
recipes.overview: A sweet and tangy paste used in Pathia and Chasni curries, combining mango chutney, tomato ketchup, and lemon juice for a vibrant flavor base.
recipes.ingredients: 175 g mango chutney • 4 tbsp tomato ketchup • 4 tbsp lemon juice • 2 tsp red food colouring

recipes.title: Fried Onion Paste
recipes.overview: Essential base for dopiaza curries, biryanis, and marinades. Fried onions add rich flavor and texture; the cooking oil can be reused in curries for extra taste.
recipes.ingredients: Rapeseed oil, for deep-frying • 2 large onions, finely sliced • Water or yoghurt, as needed for blending

recipes.title: Raw Cashew Paste
recipes.overview: A simple paste made from soaked raw cashews, used to thicken and flavor curries. Adds creaminess and richness without cooking.
recipes.ingredients: Raw cashews, quantity as needed • Cold water, for soaking and blending

subcategories.overview: Wet aromatic pastes such as onion, cashew and ginger-garlic, used as flavour bases for curries.

subcategories.label: Rice

recipes.title: Lemon and Garlic Fried Rice
recipes.overview: This is the quickest rice dish in the Indian repertoire. Cold cooked basmati rice, already separated grain-by-grain, meets hot oil infused with garlic and ginger. The heat separates the grains further and coats them with flavorful aromatics, while fresh lemon juice adds brightness and acidity. Chives provide a fresh finishing touch. This is best served immediately while still warm.
recipes.ingredients: 500 ml cold cooked basmati rice • 3 tablespoons rapeseed oil or ghee • 1 teaspoon garlic and ginger paste • Finely grated zest of 2 unwaxed lemons • Juice of 1-2 fresh lemons • 3 fresh chives • Salt to taste
ingredientNames: finely grated zest of 2 unwaxed lemons

winePairings.note: Aromatic whites and light fruity reds match soy, ginger and sesame.

recipes.title: Pilau Rice
recipes.overview: This is Indian rice cooking at its most elegant. Whole spices bloom in hot ghee, releasing their essential oils. The rice toasts lightly in this fragrant fat before being simmered gently under a tight lid. A final infusion of saffron-scented milk adds color and subtle flavor. Each grain remains separate and al dente, never mushy.
recipes.ingredients: 370 grams basmati rice • 3 tablespoons whole milk • Pinch of saffron threads • 3 tablespoons ghee • 6 green cardamom pods • 5 cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 1 onion • 1 garlic clove • 2 Indian bay leaves • 750 ml cold water or unsalted chicken stock • Salt to taste
ingredientNames: cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark
cold water or unsalted chicken stock

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Steamed Rice
recipes.overview: Basic steamed rice is deceptively important. This method produces separate, tender grains with no mushiness or stickiness. The technique is simple: bring to boil, immediately remove from heat, and rest undisturbed for 40 minutes. The key is discipline, don't lift the lid during resting. Ghee adds richness and flavor. This is the ideal rice for pairing with any curry, dal, or Indian preparation. Master this technique and you've mastered rice cooking.
recipes.ingredients: 370 grams basmati rice • 750 ml cold water • Pinch of fine sea salt • 1 tablespoon ghee or unsalted butter

winePairings.note: Aromatic whites and light fruity reds match soy, ginger and sesame.

subcategories.overview: Biryanis, pilafs and aromatic rice dishes.

subcategories.label: Sauces Pickles

recipes.title: Chilli and Red Onion Raita
recipes.overview: This is raita with structural elements and textural interest. Cool yoghurt anchors the dish, while red onion provides peppy bite, toasted cumin adds earthiness, green chilli brings heat, and a garnish of thin onion slices adds visual appeal. This is more composed than simple cucumber raita, yet still maintains its essential cooling function.
recipes.ingredients: 1 small garlic clove • 150 ml natural yoghurt • 1 large red onion • 1 small fresh green chilli • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 2 tablespoons fresh coriander • 1/2 tablespoon granulated sugar • Salt to taste • Paper-thin red onion slices • Extra fresh coriander leaves

recipes.title: Cold Onion Chutney
recipes.overview: This is simplicity and technique combined. Paper-thin raw onion slices, chilled until crisp, meet a savory tomato-spice mixture for a fresh, sharp condiment. The interplay between cool, raw onions and warm, spiced sauce creates complexity. This is especially good alongside tandoori meats and grilled foods.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large onion • Ice cubes • 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • Pinch of salt • 1 teaspoon roasted cumin seeds

recipes.title: Cucumber Raita
recipes.overview: This is the most essential Indian yoghurt-based side dish. Cooling yoghurt, refreshing cucumber, and a hint of heat from green chilli create a balance that's perfect alongside any spiced main course. The technique is simple: dice cucumber finely, fold into yoghurt with minimal seasonings, and garnish. This is a template raita; it anchors the Indian meal.
recipes.ingredients: 1 medium cucumber • 300 ml natural yoghurt • 1/4 teaspoon salt • 1/4 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 fresh green chilli • Pinch of chilli powder • 1 fresh mint sprig

recipes.title: Kashmiri Red Chilli and Garlic Chutney
recipes.overview: This is a minimal-ingredient chutney that relies on quality ingredients and proper technique. Kashmiri chillies are prized for their fruity, sweet heat; they're less aggressive than other dried red chillies. Garlic adds pungency and depth. Toasting the chillies before soaking awakens their essential oils. The soaking liquid becomes part of the chutney, adding its flavor to the final product. This is a versatile condiment, serve with pakora, samosa, or alongside curries.
recipes.ingredients: 20 Kashmiri dried red chillies • 10 garlic cloves • 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin • 1/4 teaspoon fine sea salt • Hot water from kettle

recipes.title: Mango Chutney
recipes.overview: This is the foundational chutney of Indian kitchens. Unripe green mangoes, simmered slowly with sugar and vinegar in a gentle spice base, transformed into a thick, concentrated condiment. The sweet and sour balance allows the mango's subtle character to shine. This is shelf-stable and improves with age; make it in batches and keep jars on hand year-round.
recipes.ingredients: 400 grams sugar • 250 ml distilled white vinegar • 4-5 green mangoes • 1 onion • Large handful of raisins • 5 cm piece of fresh ginger • 3 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon black mustard seeds • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • Salt

recipes.title: Mint Chutney
recipes.overview: Mint chutney is the Indian restaurant staple for good reason. A bright green blend of fresh yoghurt, mint leaves, and aromatics creates a versatile condiment that soothes and refreshes. Unlike many chutneys that are cooked, this one is assembled fresh and chilled, maintaining the mint's vibrant character. Quick to prepare, it lasts several days refrigerated.
recipes.ingredients: 270 grams plain yoghurt • 1 tablespoon garlic and ginger paste • 1-3 bird's-eye green chillies • Juice of 1 fresh lime • 1-2 tablespoons commercial mint sauce • Pinch of salt

recipes.title: Mint, Coriander, and Mango Chutney
recipes.overview: This is a contemporary fusion chutney that bridges traditional and modern Indian cooking. Fresh herbs meet the sweetness of mango chutney in an elegant balance. The texture is smooth and refined, the flavor complex and sophisticated. This is perfect alongside grilled meats and flatbreads.
recipes.ingredients: Small bunch of fresh coriander • Large bunch of fresh mint • 200 ml smooth mango chutney • 1-4 fresh green chillies • 2 garlic cloves • Juice of 1 fresh lime • Salt to taste

recipes.title: Pakora Sauce
recipes.overview: This is the quintessential dipping sauce for pakora and bhajis at Indian restaurants. A blend of yoghurt, mango chutney, tomato ketchup, and mint sauce creates a balanced sweet-sour-creamy condiment. The optional red food coloring gives it restaurant-style appearance, though it's not necessary. This sauce is quick to assemble and improves with a brief chill.
recipes.ingredients: 200 grams plain yoghurt • 2 tablespoons smooth mango chutney • 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup • 1 teaspoon mint sauce • 1/2 teaspoon roasted cumin seeds • 1/2 teaspoon chilli powder • 1 tablespoon sugar • Fresh lemon juice • 1 onion • 1 teaspoon red food coloring powder • Milk • Salt to taste

recipes.title: Tamarind Chutney
recipes.overview: This is the most complex of Indian chutneys. Tamarind provides deep, fruity sourness; sugar brings balance; fresh vegetables add texture; various spices and herbs create depth. It's simultaneously sweet, sour, spicy, and savory. This is served alongside pakora, samosa, and other fried appetizers, and as a seasoning for chaat (Indian street food snacks).
recipes.ingredients: 1-2 tablespoons tamarind concentrate • 2 tablespoons water • 3 tablespoons sugar • 4 tablespoons tomato ketchup • Juice of 1 fresh lemon • 3 fresh green chillies • 1/2 medium onion • 3 spring onions • 4 tablespoons fresh coriander • 1 large carrot • 1 teaspoon Madras curry powder • Salt to taste

subcategories.overview: Chutneys, raitas and pickles to serve alongside curries.

subcategories.label: Side Dishes

recipes.title: Bombay Potatoes
recipes.overview: Bombay Potatoes is comfort food at its finest. Small potatoes are partially cooked, then coated in a richly spiced oil infused with seeds and aromatics. Some potatoes are mashed to create a creamy base, while others remain whole for texture. The result is a warm, golden, deeply spiced side dish that's utterly satisfying. This is a vegetarian staple of Indian home cooking.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams new potatoes • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • ½ teaspoon black mustard seeds • ½ teaspoon onion seeds • 2 dried red chillies • 6 curry leaves • 2 onions • 2 fresh green chillies • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • ¼ teaspoon asafoetida • ½ teaspoon cumin seeds • ½ teaspoon fennel seeds • ½ teaspoon kalonji seeds • 50 grams fresh coriander leaves • Lemon juice • Salt • Fried fresh curry leaves • Sesame seeds

recipes.title: Lamb Seekh Kebab
recipes.overview: Seekh kebab is restaurant-quality barbecue. Unlike simple meatballs, seekh kebab is defined by its fine, dense, bound texture achieved through vigorous kneading. The spice profile is warm and aromatic without aggression. When charred over hot coals, the exterior develops a smoky, charred, visible "lace" pattern while the interior stays succulent. This is elegant Indian street food made at home, served with yoghurt and lemon.
recipes.ingredients: 1kg lean minced lamb • 2 onions • 2 tablespoons finely chopped green chillies • 1 egg • 1 tablespoon freshly roasted ground coriander • 1 tablespoon garam masala • Large bunch of fresh coriander • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper to taste • Oil for basting • Lemon wedges

recipes.title: Onion Bhajis
recipes.overview: Onion bhajis are the simplest fritters yet utterly addictive. Thinly sliced onions are coated in a spiced chickpea flour batter, then briefly deep-fried until golden and crispy. They're light and delicate, with an irresistible crispness that shatters between your teeth. Serve immediately while hot and crunchy, accompanied by chutney or yoghurt relish. This is takeaway food at its finest, made fresh at home.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams gram flour • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • Salt to taste • 3 large onions • 6 fresh curry leaves • Water • Sunflower oil

recipes.title: Samosas
recipes.overview: Samosas are the ultimate Indian snack: crispy, angular parcels of golden filo pastry enclosing spiced, fragrant filling. The technique is simple yet satisfying, triangular folding, egg wash sealing, and oven-baking creates a light, shattering crust. Unlike deep-fried versions, these are baked for a lighter result while maintaining crispness. Serve warm with chutney, raita, or lemon juice. These are elegant enough for entertaining, casual enough for snacking.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons sunflower oil • 300 grams minced chicken • 1 onion • 1 tablespoon medium curry powder • 50 grams cooked potato • 4 tablespoons fresh coriander leaves • 4 tablespoons fresh mint leaves • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 5 sheets filo pastry • 1 egg
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

recipes.title: Tandoori Broccoli, Cauliflower & Red Onion
recipes.overview: These are grilled vegetables with spiced coating, crispy outside, tender inside. Par-cooking prevents the vegetables from drying out on the grill; the gram flour coating becomes golden and fragrant. The yoghurt adds richness and helps the spices cling; the lemon juice adds brightness. This is satisfying enough to serve as a starter or vegetarian main course, yet simple enough for a weeknight side. Serve warm or at room temperature.
recipes.ingredients: 1 head of broccoli • 1 head of cauliflower • 1 red onion • 3 tablespoons gram flour • 2 tablespoons plain yoghurt • 1 tablespoon rapeseed oil • Juice of 1 lemon • 1 tablespoon garam masala • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • Pinch of ground turmeric • Salt to taste

recipes.title: Tandoori Chicken Tikka
recipes.overview: Tandoori Chicken Tikka is restaurant-quality barbecue, sophisticated yet accessible. The chicken undergoes a two-stage marinade: first, a quick acid and spice bath to begin tenderizing; second, a rich yoghurt-based marinade infused with warming spices, fresh herbs, and umami-rich Parmesan. The extended marination (up to 48 hours) allows deep flavor penetration and tenderness. The result is succulent, fragrant, lightly charred chicken with a burnished exterior and a creamy, spiced crust. Serve with lemon and fresh coriander.
recipes.ingredients: 1kg skinless, boneless chicken breasts • Juice of 2 lemons • 3 tablespoons garlic and ginger paste • Salt • Red food colouring powder • 210g Greek yoghurt • 1 tablespoon ground cumin • 1 tablespoon ground coriander • 1 tablespoon garam masala • 1 tablespoon tandoori masala powder • 1 teaspoon amchoor • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon paprika or chilli powder • 2 fresh green chillies • 3 tablespoons finely grated Parmesan cheese • 20g fresh coriander leaves • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon freshly ground black pepper

recipes.title: Tandoori King Prawn
recipes.overview: Tandoori King Prawns are elegant, restaurant-quality appetizers or mains. The prawns undergo two rapid marinades: first, an oil-based bath with garlic, ginger, and turmeric to season quickly; second, a creamy, spiced marinade with yoghurt, cream cheese, and fresh herbs for silky texture and depth. The double approach respects the delicate nature of prawns while ensuring flavor on all sides. The brief cooking (5-8 minutes) preserves succulence. Serve with lemon and herbs. This is luxurious seafood preparation made simple.
recipes.ingredients: 500g raw king prawns • 1 tablespoon rapeseed oil • 2 teaspoons garlic and ginger paste • 1/4 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon salt • 1/2 teaspoon finely ground white pepper • 2 tablespoons Greek yoghurt • 1 tablespoon cream cheese • 1 tablespoon single cream • 1cm piece of fresh ginger • 1 fresh green chilli • 1 tablespoon fresh coriander • 1 teaspoon mace and cardamom • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon garlic powder • 1 teaspoon ajwain seeds

subcategories.overview: Vegetables, dals and other accompaniments.

subcategories.label: Spice Mixes

recipes.title: Base Curry Powder
recipes.overview: A balanced, all-purpose curry powder used as the foundation for most BIR-style dishes.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp coriander powder • 1 tbsp cumin powder • 1 tbsp turmeric • 1 tbsp paprika • 1 tsp chilli powder • 1 tsp garlic powder • 1 tsp ginger powder

recipes.title: Chaat Masala
recipes.overview: Chaat masala has a quite distinctive flavour and is usually used in small amounts, sprinkled over finished dishes and included in marinades to give them extra kick. This version uses the more authentic and healthier amchoor (dried mango powder) instead of commercial citric acid, which gives the spice blend a nice citric flavour. The distinctive black powdered salt adds a characteristic sulphur aroma that becomes highly addictive.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp cumin seeds • 3 tbsp coriander seeds • 1 tsp chilli powder • 4 tbsp amchoor • 3 tbsp powdered black salt • 1 tbsp freshly ground black pepper • Pinch of asafoetida • 1 tbsp dried mint • 1 tbsp garlic powder • 1 tsp ajwain seeds

recipes.title: Chole Masala Powder
recipes.overview: A unique Punjabi spice blend specifically designed for chole masala (chickpea curry). While this blend is generally only used for chole masala, making it homemade ensures authentic flavour. Ready-made versions are available at many Asian grocers and online if you prefer convenience, but homemade is worth the effort.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp cumin seeds • 3 tbsp coriander seeds • 3 tbsp dried pomegranate seeds • 3–4 dried red chillies • 2 tsp ajwain seeds • 2 tbsp dried fenugreek leaves • 8–10 green cardamom pods, lightly bruised • 1 tsp black peppercorns • 5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 8 cloves • 1 tsp dried ginger powder • 1 tsp amchoor
ingredientNames: 5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

recipes.title: Curry Powder
recipes.overview: Commercially prepared curry powders date back to the 18th century, when Indian merchants prepared spice blends for British army and government officials returning to Britain during the British Raj. Unlike garam masalas (warming spices), curry powders contain complementary ingredients with chillies added for spiciness. This version is moderately spiced but can be adjusted to your preference by omitting or reducing dried chillies and chilli powder.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tbsp coriander seeds • 6 tbsp cumin seeds • 4 tbsp black peppercorns • 2 tbsp fennel seeds • 2 tbsp black mustard seeds • 12cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 4 Indian bay leaves • 3 tbsp fenugreek seeds • 3 star anise • 15 cardamom pods, lightly bruised • 8 Kashmiri dried red chillies • 2 tbsp ground turmeric • 2 tbsp hot chilli powder • 1 tsp garlic powder • 2 tsp dried onion powder
ingredientNames: 12cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

recipes.title: Dhansak Spice Mix
recipes.overview: A complex blend balancing sweet, sour, and savoury flavours, suited to lentil-based dhansak curries.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tsp coriander • 1 tsp cumin • 1 tsp turmeric • 1 tsp paprika • ½ tsp cinnamon • ½ tsp mustard powder

recipes.title: Garam Masala
recipes.overview: A blend of warming, aromatic spices essential to Indian cooking. In India, the ingredients used can vary greatly from region to region and even from family to family. This version uses the same spices popular in northern India, Bangladesh and Pakistan, essential for achieving that curry-house flavour. Garam masala is an important part of mixed powder used in most BIR curries, and is also added on its own to many recipes.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tbsp coriander seeds • 6 tbsp cumin seeds • 5 tsp black peppercorns • 4 tbsp fennel seeds • 3 tsp cloves • 7.5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 5 dried Indian bay leaves • 20 green cardamom pods, lightly bruised • 2 large pieces of mace
ingredientNames: 7.5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

recipes.title: Korma Spice Mix
recipes.overview: A mild, aromatic blend for creamy, nut-based curries. Emphasises warm, fragrant spices with no strong heat.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tsp coriander powder • 1 tsp cumin • ½ tsp turmeric • ½ tsp cardamom • ½ tsp cinnamon

recipes.title: Madras Spice Mix
recipes.overview: A medium-hot spice blend with strong chilli and earthy notes, suited to Madras-style curries.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tsp base curry powder • 1 tsp chilli powder • 1 tsp paprika • ½ tsp cumin • ½ tsp coriander

recipes.title: Mixed Powder
recipes.overview: A curry house-style spice blend and one of the secret ingredients that makes BIR curries what they are. Mixed powder combines the flavours of cumin, coriander, paprika, and turmeric, spices essential to most BIR curries. You will use it in almost all classic curries. This blend makes it possible to add multiple spices in one go, along with curry powder and garam masala, for consistent and efficient seasoning.
recipes.ingredients: Homemade garam masala • Homemade curry powder • Additional ground coriander, cumin, paprika, and turmeric

recipes.title: Mixed Powder
recipes.overview: Classic British Indian Restaurant (B.I.R.) curry powder used for most classic curries. Provides the signature B.I.R. flavor; can be made with commercial or homemade curry powder and garam masala.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp ground cumin • 3 tbsp ground coriander • 4 tbsp curry powder • 3 tbsp paprika • 3 tbsp ground turmeric • 1 tbsp garam masala

recipes.title: Panch Poran (Indian Five Spice)
recipes.overview: A traditional whole spice blend commonly available in Asian spice shops already mixed. Panch poran varies from region to region across the Indian subcontinent, but this is the most common blend in UK curry houses. Equal amounts of each ingredient are used, making it easy to scale this recipe up or down.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tbsp cumin seeds • 1 tbsp fenugreek seeds • 1 tbsp brown mustard seeds • 1 tbsp fennel seeds • 1 tbsp nigella seeds

recipes.title: Tandoori Masala
recipes.overview: A versatile spice blend traditionally used for tandoori cooking. Most commercial tandoori masalas rely on salt, citric acid powder, and cheap ground spices with red food colouring. This homemade version uses more quality spices and substitutes natural amchoor (dried mango powder) for artificial tanginess, giving you greater control over the final flavour.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp coriander seeds • 3 tbsp cumin seeds • 1 tbsp black mustard seeds • 5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • Small piece of mace • 3 dried Indian bay leaves • 1 tbsp ground ginger • 2 tbsp garlic powder • 2 tbsp dried onion powder • 2 tbsp amchoor • 1 tbsp red food colouring powder
ingredientNames: 5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

recipes.title: Vindaloo Spice Mix
recipes.overview: A hot, sharp spice mix designed to complement vinegar-based curries. High in chilli with warm supporting spices.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tsp chilli powder • 1 tsp cumin • 1 tsp coriander • ½ tsp black pepper • ¼ tsp cloves • ¼ tsp cinnamon

subcategories.overview: Garam masalas, BIR mix powders and other Indian spice blends.

subcategories.label: Stocks

recipes.title: BIR Spice Stock
recipes.overview: A fragrant aromatic stock made from whole spices and fresh herbs. This versatile base adds depth to curries when extra liquid is needed to thin or lighten a sauce. While water works, this stock imparts subtle layers of flavour that elevate the final dish.
recipes.ingredients: Handful of green cardamom pods, lightly bruised • 15 Indian bay leaves • 2.5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 20 black peppercorns • Large handful of star anise • 1 tsp roasted cumin seeds • Large bunch of coriander , stems and leaves roughly chopped • 1 or more fresh green chillies, to taste, halved lengthways • 1 litre water
ingredientNames: 2.5cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

subcategories.overview: Spiced stocks used in base gravies and BIR curries.

A vast, regional cuisine where spice work is the defining art. Blends like garam masala, panch phoron and individual masala mixes layer warmth, heat and aromatics across curries, rice, breads and pickles. Tandoor cooking, slow-simmered gravies, dum-style steaming and the British curry-house BIR method (pre-cooked meat finished in seconds in spiced base gravy) all sit here.

subcategories.label: Indonesian

recipes.title: Beef Nasi Goreng
recipes.overview: Indonesia's national fried rice, traditionally a way to put yesterday's leftovers to work and now a fixture from street stalls to weeknight kitchens. Beef mince keeps the cooking time short, while kecap manis, soy, shrimp paste and a crumbled stock cube layer the savouriness from four directions. The trick is pressing the rice into the wok and leaving it alone long enough to pick up a proper char before tossing.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 250 grams beef mince • 1 onion • 3 garlic cloves • 1 long red chilli • ½ teaspoon shrimp paste • 1 beef stock cube • 4 cups cooked rice • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 teaspoons kecap manis • ¼ cup fried shallots • Sliced cucumber • Sliced tomato • Sambal oelek

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Beef Rendang
recipes.overview: Rendang is a spicy meat dish which originated from the Minangkabau ethnic group of Indonesia, and is now commonly served across the country. One of the characteristic foods of Minangkabau culture, it is served at ceremonial occasions and to honour guests. This rich, aromatic curry features beef slowly simmered in coconut milk and spices until deeply flavoured.
recipes.ingredients: 100 grams grated fresh coconut • 8 dried Kashmiri chillies • 2 tablespoons coriander seeds • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 1 teaspoon turmeric powder • 225 grams shallots • 30 grams garlic • 50 grams peeled galangal or ginger • 6 hot red bird's eye chillies • 100 ml water • 3 tablespoons coconut oil • 1.5 kg braising steak • 800 ml canned coconut milk • 4 lemongrass stalks • 12 dried kaffir lime leaves • 2 cinnamon sticks • 125 ml tamarind water • 1 tablespoon palm sugar • Salt to taste

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Gado-Gado
recipes.overview: A mix of raw and lightly-blanched vegetables; firm tofu pan-fried; hard-boiled eggs halved. The peanut sauce is built fresh from roasted peanuts blended with garlic, chilli, kecap manis, tamarind, lime and water. Pour over at the table; toss; eat.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g new potatoes • 200 g green beans • 200 g savoy cabbage • 200 g beansprouts • 200 g firm tofu • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 large eggs • ½ cucumber • 50 g prawn crackers • 200 g roasted unsalted peanuts • 3 garlic cloves • 2 long red chillies • 1 cm fresh ginger • 3 tablespoons kecap manis • 2 tablespoons light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon tamarind paste • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • Juice of 1 lime • 200-300 ml hot water • Salt to taste

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Indonesian Chicken Noodle Soup (Soto Ayam)
recipes.overview: A clear, golden chicken broth fortified with a freshly pounded turmeric, lemongrass and shallot paste, finished with coconut milk and a squeeze of lime. The dish is built in three layers: a slow-simmered chicken stock, a fried spice paste that becomes the soup's backbone, and quick-soaked rice vermicelli to carry it all. Comforting and aromatic in equal measure.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 chicken thigh fillets • 1 stalk lemongrass • 2 shallots • 3 slices fresh ginger • 3 makrut lime leaves • 8 cups water • Sea salt to taste • 1 stalk lemongrass • 6 small red Asian shallots • 3 garlic cloves • 4 cm fresh turmeric • 3 cm ginger • 3 teaspoons ground coriander • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 cup coconut milk • 1 tablespoon lime juice • Sea salt to taste • 200 grams dried rice vermicelli • Coriander leaves • Sambal oelek • Lime wedges

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Indonesian Meatballs
recipes.overview: Tender chicken meatballs simmered in a fragrant coconut and curry sauce, this Indonesian classic combines aromatic spices with creamy coconut milk for a comforting, flavourful dish. The meatballs stay moist and are infused with lemongrass and lime leaves for authentic flavour.
