recipes.ingredients: 500 grams minced chicken • 100 grams panko breadcrumbs • 1 red chilli pepper • 4 cloves garlic • 1 tbsp garam masala • ½ tsp ground nutmeg • 5 green onions • 4 grams fresh coriander • 1 egg • ½ tsp salt • ¼ tsp pepper • 1 tsp red curry paste • 2 tbsp peanut oil • 2 stalks lemongrass • 237 ml low-sodium chicken broth • 2 tbsp red curry paste • 391 ml unsweetened coconut milk • 1 tbsp curry powder • ¼ tsp salt • ¼ tsp pepper • 5 fresh kaffir lime leaves • 1 tbsp fresh coriander

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Indonesian Mee Goreng
recipes.overview: Fresh egg noodles tossed in a glossy, sweet-savoury sauce of kecap manis, soy, ketchup, sesame oil and shrimp paste, with pork, prawns, cabbage and bean sprouts. The dish is finished with thin egg ribbons and a scatter of spring onion. Quick to cook once the components are prepped, but rewards a properly hot wok and a sauce mixed in advance.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons kecap manis • 3 tablespoons light soy sauce • 2 tablespoons ketchup • 1 tablespoon sesame oil • 1 teaspoon shrimp paste • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • 2 eggs • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 3 garlic cloves • 200 grams sliced pork • 200 grams peeled and deveined prawns • ¼ cup sliced white cabbage • 400 grams fresh Chinese egg noodles • ½ cup bean sprouts • ¼ cup sliced spring onions • Sliced red chilli • Lime wedges

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Sayur Lodeh
recipes.overview: A spice paste of shallots, garlic, candlenuts (or cashews), turmeric and chilli is fried in oil until fragrant. Lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves and galangal infuse the coconut milk. Vegetables go in by cooking time — firmer first, leafier last. Tofu joins; everything simmers gently. The broth should be thin, the vegetables tender but not collapsed.
recipes.ingredients: 6 shallots • 5 garlic cloves • 3 candlenuts or cashews • 2 long red chillies • 2 cm fresh turmeric • 1 cm fresh ginger • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 stalks lemongrass • 4 kaffir lime leaves • 3 cm galangal • 400 ml coconut milk • 600 ml vegetable stock or water • 200 g long beans • 200 g chayote or kabocha squash • 1 small aubergine • 200 g firm tofu • 100 g cabbage • 2 tablespoons palm sugar • 2 tablespoons light soy sauce • 1 teaspoon salt • Juice of half a lime

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Tempeh Goreng
recipes.overview: Tempeh is sliced into thick fingers; marinated in a paste of garlic, coriander seed, tamarind, salt and water. The batons drain briefly, then deep-fry in batches until crisp and deep golden. Best eaten hot, with kecap manis or sambal for dipping.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g tempeh • 5 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • 1 tablespoon tamarind paste • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon palm sugar • 200 ml water • Vegetable oil • Kecap manis • Sambal oelek or sliced bird's eye chillies • Steamed rice
ingredientNames: sambal oelek or sliced bird's eye chillies

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Bold, layered cooking from across the Indonesian archipelago. Centred on chilli, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaf and shrimp paste, often softened with coconut milk and palm sugar. Long-braised rendang, sambal-led plates, satay grilling and quick-fried rice and noodle dishes show the range, where sweet, salty, spicy and sour all sit on the same plate.

subcategories.label: Iran

recipes.title: Khoresh-e Fesenjan
recipes.overview: Walnuts toast and grind to a paste. Onions soften slowly with turmeric and saffron; chicken thighs sear, then poach in a stock that the walnut paste loosens into. Pomegranate molasses, sugar and lemon balance the sauce as it darkens; chicken returns to absorb flavour. Slow-cook until the oil splits out — the sign Fesenjan is ready.
recipes.ingredients: 6 chicken thighs or 1 duck • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 2 large onions • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • A generous pinch of saffron • 400 g shelled walnuts • 1 litre chicken or vegetable stock • 4 tablespoons pomegranate molasses • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • Juice of half a lemon • 1½ teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • Hot chelo • Pomegranate seeds • A few mint leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sholeh Zard
recipes.overview: Rice cooks slowly in lots of water until tender and mashable. Sugar dissolves with bloomed saffron; the lot simmers until thick — like loose porridge. Rosewater and a knob of butter join near the end; the pudding pours into shallow dishes to set. Cinnamon dust and pistachio patterns top each serving.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g short or medium-grain white rice • 1.5 litres water • 350 g caster sugar • A generous pinch of saffron • 50 g unsalted butter • 3 tablespoons rosewater • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • 50 g flaked almonds • Ground cinnamon • 50 g pistachios • A few rose petals

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Tahdig
recipes.overview: Basmati rice is rinsed and soaked. It parboils briefly in salted water (al dente), drains. The pot heats with butter, oil and saffron-water. A layer of yogurt-and-rice mixture forms the crust at the bottom; the rest of the rice piles on top in a mound. The lid wraps in a tea towel; everything steams 45 minutes; the bottom crisps. Inverted onto a platter, the golden tahdig sits on top.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g basmati rice • 2 tablespoons salt • 4 tablespoons unsalted butter • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • A generous pinch of saffron • 4 tablespoons plain yogurt • 1 large egg yolk

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Persian cooking: the alchemy of saffron, dried lime, pomegranate molasses, walnuts and mountains of fresh herbs. Slow-built khoresh stews (fesenjan, ghormeh sabzi, gheymeh) ladle over chelo (steamed rice with a crispy tahdig bottom); sabzi polo, zereshk polo and a dozen other rice dishes share the table. Saffron is a first-rank ingredient, not a flourish; the New Year (Nowruz) feast is the cuisine in concentrated form.

subcategories.label: Iraq

recipes.title: Klaicha
recipes.overview: A buttery cardamom-scented dough enriched with milk and a touch of yeast (gives a tender bite without rising). Date paste sweetens with cardamom, cloves and a pinch of cinnamon. Each cookie wraps a teaspoon of filling, then gets pressed into a patterned mould or scored on top. Bakes pale gold; the surface stays soft, the inside spice-heavy.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g plain flour • 200 g unsalted butter • 100 g caster sugar • 1 teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon ground nigella seeds • 1 teaspoon active dry yeast • 200 ml whole milk • 1 large egg • ¼ teaspoon salt • 400 g pitted dates • 50 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon ground cloves • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • 2 tablespoons sesame seeds • 1 large egg yolk • 1 tablespoon sesame seeds

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Quzi
recipes.overview: A bone-in lamb shoulder rubs with baharat, dried lime, garlic and yogurt; sits overnight; roasts long and slow until the meat falls from the bone. Saffron rice cooks separately with onion, raisins and toasted nuts. Everything assembles on a wide platter — rice mounded under the lamb, sauce spooned over, almonds and pine nuts scattered.
recipes.ingredients: 2 kg lamb shoulder • 4 tablespoons plain yogurt • 8 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons baharat • 1 tablespoon ground turmeric • 2 dried limes • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 tablespoon salt • 500 ml lamb stock or water • 500 g basmati rice • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 2 large onions • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 1 cinnamon stick • 6 cardamom pods • 4 cloves • 2 bay leaves • A generous pinch saffron • 1 litre hot water • 1 teaspoon salt • 100 g flaked almonds • 50 g pine nuts • 100 g raisins • 30 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 1 garlic clove • 200 ml lamb cooking liquid • 1 teaspoon baharat • 1 tablespoon lemon juice

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Timman Ahmar
recipes.overview: Onion softens in oil; tomato paste fries until darker and aromatic; rice toasts briefly; hot stock pours in with baharat and cinnamon. Sliced potatoes layer at the bottom of the pot for the crust; rice piles over; the lot steams covered until tender. The crust pops out at the end as a single golden cake.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g basmati rice • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large onion • 4 garlic cloves • 4 tablespoons tomato paste • 1½ teaspoons baharat • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon turmeric • 1.1 litres hot water • 1½ teaspoons salt • 2 medium potatoes • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 30 g toasted almonds

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Mesopotamian cooking with deep Persian, Ottoman, Bedouin and Levantine layers. Long-grain rice (timman) sits beside slow-cooked lamb (quzi), grilled river fish (masgouf), and stuffed vegetables (dolma). Baharat, dried lime, sumac and fresh herbs season; tahini, dates and pomegranate finish. The kebab and the dolma are universal; the date and the river fish are unmistakably Iraqi.

subcategories.label: Irish

recipes.title: Boxty
recipes.overview: Half the potatoes are boiled and mashed; the other half are grated raw and squeezed dry. Both fold together with flour, milk and an egg into a thick batter. Spoonfuls fry in butter until deep golden on both sides.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g floury potatoes • 250 g floury potatoes • 100 g plain flour • 1 teaspoon baking powder • 1 large egg • 100 ml whole milk • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 50 g unsalted butter

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Champ
recipes.overview: Spring onions infuse hot milk for 10 minutes off the heat. Floury potatoes boil and dry; everything mashes together with a generous amount of butter. The classic finish: a hollow scooped out of the centre of the mound on each plate, filled with melting butter.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg floury potatoes • 250 ml whole milk • 1 large bunch spring onions • 100 g unsalted butter • Salt and white pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Colcannon
recipes.overview: Floury potatoes boil; cabbage or kale wilts in butter with spring onions; the lot is mashed with hot milk and lots of butter. The cabbage softens but keeps a little texture; the mash should be smooth, with green flecks throughout.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg floury potatoes • 80 g unsalted butter • 6 spring onions • 300 g savoy cabbage or kale • 200 ml whole milk • Salt and white pepper • A grating of nutmeg

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Irish Stew
recipes.overview: Irish stew is the epitome of rustic, peasant cooking elevated to comfort food status. Middle neck of lamb simmers gently with potatoes, onions, carrots, and cabbage in a light broth, with the potatoes gradually breaking down to thicken the sauce naturally. The result is a one-pot wonder that's wholesome, deeply flavorful, and warming, the kind of dish that feeds both body and soul on cold days.
recipes.ingredients: 750 grams middle neck of lamb • 100 grams butter • 1.5 litres chicken stock • 1 bouquet garni • 1 garlic clove • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 4 onions • 450 grams potatoes • 175 grams carrots • 6 celery sticks • 225 grams savoy cabbage • 2 teaspoons parsley
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Hearty, weather-driven cooking built on potatoes, root vegetables, lamb, beef and dairy. Flavours stay restrained, leaning on onion, parsley, mustard and the occasional pour of stout, letting good ingredients lead. One-pot stews, soda breads and griddled cakes reflect a tradition of slow, frugal home cooking.

subcategories.label: Israel

recipes.title: Israeli Salad
recipes.overview: Vegetables are diced into uniform 5 mm cubes — knife work matters. Lemon juice, olive oil and salt are the only dressing; sometimes a teaspoon of sumac or a clove of crushed garlic. Eaten freshly made; doesn't keep — the cucumbers weep.
recipes.ingredients: 4 medium tomatoes • 2 medium cucumbers • 1 small red onion • 1 small green pepper • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • A small bunch of mint • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • Juice of 1 large lemon • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ½ teaspoon ground sumac

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Jerusalem Mixed Grill
recipes.overview: Chicken thigh, liver and heart sear hard in a wide pan with onions; spices bloom; everything cooks together until the meat is just done and the onions are deeply caramelised. Stuffed into pita with hummus, salad, tahini, pickles. The signature is the spice blend — cumin, turmeric, paprika, black pepper, cardamom — and the onion-to-meat ratio (a lot of onion).
recipes.ingredients: 400 g boneless chicken thighs • 200 g chicken livers • 200 g chicken hearts • 4 large onions • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1½ teaspoons ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • 1½ teaspoons salt • Juice of 1 lemon • 4 pita breads • 200 g hummus • Tahini sauce • Pickled cucumbers • Sliced tomato and onion • Hot sauce

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Malabi
recipes.overview: Cornflour whisks into a small amount of cold milk to a slurry. The rest of the milk warms with cream and sugar; the slurry pours in; the mixture cooks 4-5 minutes until thick and silky. Rosewater stirs in off the heat; the lot pours into glasses to set in the fridge. The syrup of rosewater, sugar and a touch of grenadine pours over before serving.
recipes.ingredients: 1 litre whole milk • 200 ml double cream • 100 g caster sugar • 80 g cornflour • 1 tablespoon rosewater • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract • 100 g caster sugar • 100 ml water • 2 tablespoons grenadine syrup • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 50 g pistachios • 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut • A few rose petals

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: A young, eclectic cuisine pulling from Ashkenazi, Sephardic, Mizrahi, Yemenite, Arab and modern Mediterranean traditions. Hummus and falafel sit beside shakshuka and sabich; the Israeli salad and labneh on bread show up at every meal. Cumin, sumac, za'atar, paprika, garlic and lemon are the core; a culture of small plates eats from many bowls at once.

subcategories.label: Italian
Pizza

recipes.title: American Hot Deep-Pan Pizza
recipes.overview: A breadlike yeasted dough is pressed into 23 cm cake tins, given a thick rim, and baked over a deeply reduced tomato sauce sharpened with red wine, vinegar, Worcestershire and Tabasco. Two kinds of salami sit beneath grated mozzarella and cheddar, with jalapeños scattered on top. The pan technique gives a chewy crumb and a crisp, cheese-lacquered base.
recipes.ingredients: 7 grams sachet fast-action dried yeast • 1½ teaspoons caster sugar • 1½ teaspoons fine sea salt • 340 ml lukewarm water • 600 grams strong plain flour • Vegetable oil • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 3 shallots • 2 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • ½ teaspoon sea salt • 1 tablespoon white wine vinegar • 150 ml red wine • 300 grams passata • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • 1 teaspoon Tabasco sauce • 12 slices spicy salami • 12 slices regular salami • 150 grams cooking mozzarella • 50 grams strong cheddar • 1 to 2 jalapeño chillies • 2 x 23 cm cake tins

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Basic Pizza Dough
recipes.overview: A high-hydration overnight dough that sits in the fridge for 8 to 24 hours, slowly building flavour while you sleep. The blend of plain flour for tenderness and strong bread flour for chew gives a base that's crisp at the edges and soft in the middle. Each portion stretches into a 25 to 30 cm round, ready to top and bake on a hot stone.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams plain flour • 450 grams strong white bread flour • 2 teaspoons fine sea salt • 1 tablespoon dried active yeast • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 570 ml cold water

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Calabrese Pizza
recipes.overview: A pizza built on layered heat: nduja, roquito peppers, fresh green chilli, Calabrese sausage and a touch of dried oregano. After baking, torn fior di latte and a watercress-pesto salad are scattered on top for cool, peppery contrast. A bold pizza that needs a properly hot oven and a confident hand with the spice.
recipes.ingredients: ½ quantity pizza dough • 80 grams tomato sauce • 45 grams mozzarella • 30 grams nduja sausage • 30 grams roquito peppers • 4 large fresh green chillies • ¼ yellow pepper • ¼ red pepper • 20 grams Grana Padano • 30 grams Calabrese sausage or spicy salami • Pinch of dried oregano • 20 grams watercress • 1 teaspoon fresh pesto • 50 grams fior di latte mozzarella or buffalo mozzarella
ingredientNames: fior di latte mozzarella or buffalo mozzarella

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Calzone
recipes.overview: The calzone is pizza's elegant cousin, pizza dough folded like an envelope and sealed, enclosing a luxurious filling of pancetta, cherry tomatoes, ricotta, and basil. The sealed construction creates its own moist environment, keeping the filling tender while the exterior becomes crisp and golden. This is rustic Italian cooking with refinement.
recipes.ingredients: 320 grams pizza dough • 200 grams cherry tomatoes • 200 grams pancetta • 40 small fresh basil leaves • 60 ml extra virgin olive oil • Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste • 200 grams fresh ricotta cheese • Extra olive oil for brushing • Flour for dusting

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Calzone Pollo Spinaci
recipes.overview: A solo calzone built around a béchamel base rather than the more common tomato sauce, giving the filling a richer, lasagna-like character. Cooked chicken, prosciutto, button mushrooms and spinach pile onto a single oval of thin pizza dough, drenched in white sauce and folded into a sealed half-moon. Two finishing flourishes lift the bake: a final brush of garlic butter and a snowfall of grated Riserva that bubbles in the last minute.
recipes.ingredients: 110 grams mozzarella • 1 small chicken breast • 2 slices prosciutto • 50 grams spinach • 5 button mushrooms • 60 grams béchamel sauce • Pinch of fresh rosemary • 200 grams pizza dough ball • Plain flour • Garlic butter • 1 teaspoon Riserva cheese • Red pepper tapenade

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Cherry Tomato, Burrata and Herb Pesto Pizza
recipes.overview: The dough is baked plain with just tomatoes and oil, then crowned off the heat with torn burrata, a herb pesto of basil, mint and parsley, and a tangle of rocket and parmesan shavings. The pesto is what carries the flavour; the burrata adds creamy contrast to the bright, lightly cooked tomatoes. A pizza that depends entirely on good produce.
recipes.ingredients: 3 prepared pizza dough bases • 1 kg mixed heirloom tomatoes • Olive oil • Salt and pepper • 30 grams mixed fresh herbs • 1 garlic clove • 2 teaspoons lemon juice • 1 teaspoon dijon mustard • Pinch of caster sugar • 75 to 100 ml extra-virgin olive oil • 2 x 100 gram balls burrata or buffalo mozzarella • Rocket leaves • Parmesan shavings
ingredientNames: x 100 gram balls burrata or buffalo mozzarella

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Chilli Beef Pizza
recipes.overview: This is a pizza with substance and spice. A generous topping of seasoned beef and kidney beans sits over a thin crust, topped with a blend of mozzarella and cheddar cheeses. The beef is properly browned and simmered to develop flavor, while chillies and cumin add warmth and complexity. This is not minimalist Italian pizza; this is bold, flavorful, filling pizza.
recipes.ingredients: 220 grams pizza dough • 200 grams plum tomatoes • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • Salt and pepper to taste • 220 grams Pizza Sauce • 175 grams lean ground beef • 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil • 1 red onion • 1 garlic clove • 1/2 red bell pepper • 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin • 2 fresh red chillies • 115 grams canned red kidney beans • Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste • 50 grams mozzarella • 75 grams cheddar cheese • 1 tablespoon fresh oregano

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Chorizo Margherita Pizza
recipes.overview: Three margherita-style pizzas with a long-cooked plum tomato ragù as the base, topped with thinly sliced chorizo, capers, olives and torn buffalo mozzarella. The chorizo crisps and renders its smoky, paprika-rich oil over the cheese as it bakes. A scatter of fresh basil at the end adds the herbal lift.
recipes.ingredients: 500 grams ripe plum tomatoes • 1 large garlic clove • 2 tablespoons olive oil • ¼ teaspoon sugar • 3 prepared pizza dough bases • 100 grams chorizo • 3 tablespoons capers • 30 grams pitted black olives • 250 grams buffalo mozzarella • Handful of fresh basil leaves

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Kale and Chilli Calzone
recipes.overview: A weeknight-friendly calzone built on a packet ciabatta mix that handles the dough work without a long prove. The filling is garlicky kale wilted briefly in olive oil, sharpened with fresh red chilli, then folded over a generous dollop of ricotta or mozzarella into pasty-shaped half-moons. Baked at moderate heat for 15 to 20 minutes; the dough puffs, the cheese melts, and the kale stays bright.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams ciabatta bread mix • Plain flour • 3 tablespoons olive oil • 2 garlic cloves • 1 red chilli • 100 grams kale • 8 tablespoons ricotta

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Meatball and Pepperoni Pizza
recipes.overview: Mini beef meatballs are first browned, then finished simmering in a quick passata sauce sharpened with vinegar, oregano and chilli flakes. They're scattered over two pizza bases alongside diced mozzarella, sliced pepperoni, spicy cured sausage, green pepper and red onion. A short, hot bake melts everything together; oregano and chilli at the end keep the heat going.
recipes.ingredients: 1½ tablespoons olive oil • 300 grams mini beef meatballs • 1 to 2 garlic cloves • 400 grams tomato passata • Splash of red wine vinegar • ¼ teaspoon dried oregano • 2 pinches of chilli flakes • Plain flour • 2 balls of pizza dough • 100 grams cooking mozzarella • 2 spicy cured sausages, e.g. Peperami • 12 slices pepperoni • ½ green pepper • ½ onion

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pancetta and Rocket Pizza
recipes.overview: A small pizza built on tomato sauce and cubed mozzarella, baked until crisp and finished with sun-dried tomatoes, rocket, a drizzle of Caesar dressing, oven-crisped pancetta, oregano and Grana Padano. The trick is crisping the pancetta in advance so the finished pizza has texture, not just flavour. Comes together quickly once the dough is shaped.
recipes.ingredients: ½ quantity pizza dough • 80 grams pizza sauce • 65 grams mozzarella • 4 thin slices Italian smoked pancetta • 4 whole sun-dried tomatoes • 40 grams rocket leaves • 2 tablespoons Caesar dressing • Pinch of dried oregano • 15 grams Grana Padano

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Pissalàdiers
recipes.overview: This is the French answer to Neapolitan pizza. Where Italian pizza celebrates tomato, French pissaladières celebrate caramelized onions cooked until they're almost jam-like in their sweetness and tenderness. A lattice of anchovy fillets and scattered black olives provide salt and briny depth against the onions' sweetness. Baked on a thin pizza crust, this is elegance and restraint.
recipes.ingredients: 200 grams pizza dough • 100 ml light extra virgin olive oil • 800 grams onions • 2 garlic cloves • 1 bouquet garni • Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste • 18 anchovy fillets • 40 small black olives • 1 teaspoon tiny fresh thyme sprigs

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Pizza Margherita
recipes.overview: This is the essence of pizza. A thin, crisp crust topped with a simple tomato sauce, creamy fresh mozzarella, marinated cherry tomatoes, and just-wilted basil. The beauty lies in ingredient quality and the technique of assembly. Each component matters. Too much topping drowns the crust; too little leaves an empty plate. This is balance and respect for simplicity.
recipes.ingredients: 220 grams pizza dough • 200 grams plum tomatoes • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil • Salt and pepper to taste • 120 grams cherry tomatoes • 60 ml extra virgin olive oil • Salt and pepper to taste • 150 grams buffalo mozzarella • 24 fresh basil leaves • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling • Salt and cracked pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Pizza Sauce
recipes.overview: Pizza sauce is deceptively simple yet entirely dependent on quality ingredients and proper technique. San Marzano tomatoes are preferred for their low acidity and sweet flavor. The sauce is briefly cooked, never long-simmered, to preserve the fresh tomato character. Garlic, oregano, and basil are the only seasonings; they should enhance, never dominate. This is the classic sauce of Naples and New York, used for Margherita and simple cheese pizzas.
recipes.ingredients: 1 can whole San Marzano tomatoes • 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 1 onion • 3 cloves garlic • 3/4 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon dried basil • 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes • 2 teaspoons sugar • 1 teaspoon kosher salt

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Sausage, Fennel and Chilli Griddle-Pan Pizza
recipes.overview: The dough is a quick mix of plain flour, baking powder and Greek yogurt, rolled out and griddled in a hot pan rather than baked. Fennel wedges are charred separately, then a sausagemeat-and-spinach mixture is spread over the cooked base with ricotta and parmesan, and the lot is finished under the grill. A 35-minute pizza that doesn't need a stone or a long prove.
recipes.ingredients: 200 grams plain flour • ½ teaspoon baking powder • ½ teaspoon chilli flakes • Pinch of salt • 200 grams Greek yogurt • 1 small fennel bulb • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 200 grams sausagemeat • 1 small red onion • 1 large garlic clove • 1 teaspoon fennel seeds • 60 grams young leaf spinach • 3 tablespoons ricotta • 25 grams parmesan

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Sloppy Joe Pizza
recipes.overview: Two pizza bases topped with passata, salami, dollops of bolognese and grated mozzarella, drizzled with chilli oil and baked until bubbling. Fresh basil and more chilli oil at the table finish the dish. The pizza relies on a good bolognese; using leftover sauce from the fridge actually improves it.
recipes.ingredients: 220 grams pizza dough • Plain flour • 50 ml passata • 80 grams salami • 50 ml fresh bolognese sauce • 100 grams grated mozzarella • Chilli oil • Handful of fresh basil leaves • Extra chilli oil

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Smoked Pancetta Pizza with Onions
recipes.overview: A no-yeast dough rolled thin and topped with a crème fraîche, parmesan, lemon and nutmeg sauce, finished with thinly sliced salad onions, red onion and smoked pancetta. The lack of tomato keeps the flavour focused on the savoury richness of the pancetta and the slight sweetness of the onions. A 30-minute pizza that needs nothing more than a hot oven.
recipes.ingredients: 125 grams full-fat crème fraîche • 30 grams parmesan • Grated zest and juice of 1 lemon • Pinch of freshly ground nutmeg • Black pepper • 250 grams plain flour • ½ teaspoon baking powder • ½ teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons neutral oil • 120 ml warm water • 1 bunch salad onions • 1 small red onion • 8 to 9 slices smoked pancetta • Chilli flakes • Salad of choice

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Spicy Sausage Calzone
recipes.overview: A make-ahead calzone built around a deeply reduced chorizo ragù sharpened with paprika and chilli flakes. The sauce simmers for 20 minutes until thick before being parcelled inside ciabatta-mix dough with cubes of mozzarella, sealed with a steam hole, and frozen flat. Pull one (or six) from the freezer, bake at 220°C, and you have a crisp, bubbling calzone in 30 minutes.
recipes.ingredients: 500 grams ciabatta bread mix • Plain flour • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large onion • 2 garlic cloves • 180 grams chorizo or spicy salami • Pinch of chilli flakes • Pinch of paprika • 400 gram tin chopped tomatoes • 200 ml passata • Bunch of basil • Salt and pepper to taste • 250 grams mozzarella block

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Spinach and Pine Nut Pinsa Romana
recipes.overview: Wilted spinach with onion, garlic and thyme is spread over pre-prepared pinsa bases, topped with fontina or mozzarella, pine nuts and parmesan, then baked briefly until the cheese is golden. The slow-fried onion is the flavour foundation; everything else cooks fast in the oven. A vegetarian pizza that lets the cheese and pine nuts do the heavy lifting.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 small onion • 1 garlic clove • 2 teaspoons fresh thyme • 250 grams spinach leaves • Salt and pepper • 2 pinsa romana bases • 125 grams fontina cheese or mozzarella • 2 tablespoons pine nuts • 2 tablespoons grated parmesan

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Stromboli
recipes.overview: A bricklayer's calzone: pizza dough rolled into a 12 by 18 inch rectangle, brushed with garlic-oregano butter, layered with deli meats, fresh basil and two cheeses, then rolled tight and baked on a hot pizza stone. The crust crisps fast at 200°C; the cheeses melt into the meats inside; a final brush of garlic butter and a scatter of parmesan make the outside glossy. Cuts into thick pinwheel slices, ideal for sharing.
recipes.ingredients: 459 grams prepared pizza dough • 1 egg white • 1 tablespoon water • Plain flour • 300 grams sliced Italian deli meat • Handful fresh basil leaves • 300 grams sliced provolone • 300 grams mozzarella • 2 tablespoons salted butter • ¼ teaspoon dried oregano • ¼ teaspoon garlic powder • Finely chopped fresh parsley or basil • Flaky sea salt • Grated parmesan • Marinara sauce

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: The Ultimate Margherita Pizza
recipes.overview: A fresh-yeasted bread-flour dough proves for an hour while a deeply reduced tomato sauce simmers with shallots, garlic, red wine, basil and oregano. The dough is rolled into 30 cm circles, topped with sauce and grated mozzarella, and baked on a screaming-hot baking sheet. Whole basil leaves added off the heat keep the herbal lift fresh on the plate.
recipes.ingredients: 3.5 grams fast-action dried yeast • 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • ½ teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon caster sugar • 170 ml lukewarm water • 300 grams strong white bread flour • 3 shallots • 2 garlic cloves • 100 ml red wine • Bunch of fresh basil • A few sprigs of fresh oregano • 350 grams tomato passata • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce • ¼ teaspoon caster sugar • Salt and pepper • 300 grams grated mozzarella • Extra-virgin olive oil

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Neapolitan and home-style pizzas, with high-hydration doughs slow-fermented for flavour and structure. Toppings stay restrained: san marzano tomato, fior di latte or fresh mozzarella, basil, olive oil and a few well-chosen extras. Whether wood-fired or baked at home, the goal is a charred, airy crust under fresh, simple toppings.

recipes.title: Beef Tagliata
recipes.overview: Beef Tagliata is an elegant simplicity itself: thick-cut sirloin seared until a golden crust forms, rested to retain its juices, then sliced thin. The accompanying rosemary-infused oil with lemon zest and juice creates a sophisticated dressing that adds depth without heaviness, while peppery rocket and Parmesan shavings provide textural contrast. This is a dish that celebrates the quality of the beef and the importance of proper technique.
recipes.ingredients: 2 sirloin steaks • Olive oil • 120 ml extra virgin olive oil • 3 garlic cloves • 4 sprigs fresh rosemary • 1 lemon • 60 grams rocket leaves • 40 grams Parmesan shavings • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Bolognese
recipes.overview: Bolognese is Italy's classic meat sauce, built on a foundation of soffritto, the aromatic trinity of onion, carrot, and celery, combined with quality ground beef, wine, and tomato. The result is a rich, velvety sauce where individual ingredients dissolve into a cohesive whole greater than the sum of its parts. This is soul food, best made with time and attention.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 1 celery stick • 500 grams minced beef • 1 glass red wine • 700 ml passata • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Bucatini Carbonara
recipes.overview: Bucatini Carbonara is a masterclass in simplicity and technique. Five ingredients, pasta, eggs, Pecorino Romano, pancetta, and salt, combine to create a rich, creamy sauce without any cream. The success lies in temperature control: hot pasta cooks the raw eggs to silken perfection without scrambling. This is Roman street food elevated to art.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams smoked pancetta • 3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 15 grams salted butter • 4 large eggs • 4 tablespoons Pecorino Romano • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 500 grams bucatini • 4 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Additional Pecorino Romano for serving
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Cacio e Pepe
recipes.overview: Black pepper toasts in a dry pan; pasta cooks; the starchy pasta water plus finely grated Pecorino emulsify into a sauce off the heat. Heat shock scrambles the cheese; the technique is to take the pan off the heat before adding cheese, and toss energetically.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g spaghetti or tonnarelli • 100 g Pecorino Romano • 2 teaspoons coarsely cracked black peppercorns • Salt for the pasta water

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Cannelloni Filled with Rocket, Spinach, and Ricotta Cheese
recipes.overview: This layered pasta masterpiece begins with fresh, delicate pasta sheets encasing a creamy, herbaceous filling. Ricotta and rocket provide lightness and peppery freshness, while spinach adds earthiness and color. The whole affair is bound with silken béchamel, enriched with tomato passata, and finished with a Pecorino crust that turns golden and crispy in the oven, a true celebration of Italian cooking.
recipes.ingredients: 400 grams fresh pasta dough • 720 ml passata • 15 fresh basil leaves • 30 grams Pecorino • 500 grams ricotta cheese • 150 grams frozen spinach • 150 grams rocket leaves • 60 grams Pecorino • 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg • Salt and pepper to taste • 100 grams salted butter • 100 grams plain flour • 1 litre full-fat milk • 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Chilli Ravioli with Crab
recipes.overview: These striking ravioli unite fresh crab meat with silken mascarpone, sharpened by fresh lemon and parsley, all enveloped in a bold chilli-infused pasta. The combination of delicate seafood with the subtle heat of dried chillies creates sophisticated complexity, while lemon butter provides the perfect light sauce. This is restaurant-quality food made at home.
recipes.ingredients: 300 grams strong white flour • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 teaspoons crushed dried red chillies • 3 eggs • 175 grams mascarpone cheese • 175 grams freshly picked crab meat • 2 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Rind of 1 lemon • Pinch of crushed dried chillies • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 75 grams butter • Juice of 1 lemon • Fresh basil leaves
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Crispy Topped Pasta with Courgettes and Salami
recipes.overview: This is baked pasta at its most elegant. Fresh courgettes sear quickly to maintain their texture, salami provides salty richness, and a silky homemade béchamel unites everything. Baked under a golden Parmesan crust until crispy and bubbling, this dish works as a first course, main course, or served at room temperature as a cold dish. The textural contrast between the creamy sauce and crispy top is essential.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 large courgettes • 250 grams salami Milano • 3 tablespoons fresh flat leaf parsley • Salt and pepper to taste • 350 grams penne rigate • 50 grams salted butter • 50 grams plain flour • 500 ml full-fat milk • 1/2 teaspoon paprika • Pinch of freshly grated nutmeg • Salt and pepper to taste • 100 grams Parmesan • Butter for greasing

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Fettuccine with Chicken and Dolcelatte
recipes.overview: This satisfying sauce combines golden-fried chicken breast strips with melting Dolcelatte cheese (a creamy Italian blue), double cream, and crisp white wine. The result is a rich, sophisticated sauce that clings to fresh pasta. Fresh chives bridge all the flavors with their subtle onion notes. (Gorgonzola can substitute if Dolcelatte is unavailable.)
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil • 350 grams boneless, skinless chicken breast • 200 grams Dolcelatte cheese • 150 ml double cream • 50 ml dry white wine • 3 tablespoons fresh chives • Salt and pepper to taste • 400 grams fresh fettuccine

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Fettuccine with Sweet Onion, Rosemary, and Lamb
recipes.overview: This elegant pasta dish marries tender minced lamb with a fragrant base of onions, carrot, and fresh rosemary. A touch of wine and stock create a light sauce that clings to fettuccine. The key is quality lamb and patience allowing the onions to become golden and sweet. This is sophisticated comfort food.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tablespoons olive oil • 3 large onions • 1 carrot • 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary • 200 grams top-quality minced lamb • 200 ml dry white wine • 300 ml vegetable stock • Salt and pepper to taste • 400 grams fettuccine • 100 grams Parmesan

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Gnocchetti with Courgettes in Butter and Sage Sauce
recipes.overview: These handmade potato gnocchetti showcase Italian rustic cooking at its finest. Tender pillows of light potato dough are tossed with sweet courgettes, fresh sage, and golden butter, then finished with abundant Parmesan. The key to success is gentle handling, don't overwork the dough or the gnocchetti become tough. Fresh sage is absolutely essential.
recipes.ingredients: 300 grams floury potatoes • 1 small egg • 100 grams plain flour • Salt to taste • 100 grams salted butter • 2 medium courgettes • 1 tablespoon fresh sage • 300 grams Parmesan • Salt and pepper to taste

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Gnocchi
recipes.overview: These cloud-like gnocchi begin with whole, unpeeled potatoes baked until fluffy. When hot, they're passed through a ricer and built into a delicate dough with just flour and an egg yolk. The technique is straightforward but demands attention to detail: over-mixing creates dense, gluey gnocchi, while proper lightness produces tender pillows that absorb sauce beautifully.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg flour potatoes • 1 egg yolk • 90 grams plain flour • Salt to taste

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Ham-Filled Pasta (Mezzelune)
recipes.overview: These tender, handmade pasta parcels are filled with a harmonious mixture of ricotta, cooked ham, sun-dried tomatoes, and basil, bound with Pecorino cheese. They're served simply with a warm oil infused with crispy chorizo. The key is making fresh, tender pasta and not overfilling; quality matters more than quantity.
recipes.ingredients: 400 grams fresh pasta dough • 2 eggs • 100 gram piece Pecorino cheese • 750 grams ricotta cheese • 200 grams cooked ham • 150 grams sun-dried tomato in oil • 15 fresh basil leaves • 100 grams Pecorino cheese • Salt and pepper to taste • 200 ml extra virgin olive oil • 200 grams chorizo

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Lasagne
recipes.overview: A rich, layered Italian baked pasta combining slow-cooked meat ragù with silky béchamel enriched with basil pesto. Homemade fresh pasta sheets are essential for the melt-in-the-mouth texture that sets this lasagne apart. The pesto adds a distinctive herbaceous note that complements the béchamel beautifully.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons of olive oil • 1 onion • 1 large carrot • 1 celery stick • 500 grams minced beef • 350 ml Italian red wine • 700 ml passata • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 12 fresh lasagne sheets • 50 grams cold salted butter • salt and pepper to taste • 100 grams salted butter • 100 grams plain flour • 1 litre cold full-fat milk • 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg • 100 grams Parmesan • 40 grams fresh basil leaves • 1 garlic clove • 30 grams pine nuts • 120 ml extra virgin olive oil • 20 grams Parmesan • pinch of salt

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Linguine with Cherry Tomatoes, Pancetta, and White Wine
recipes.overview: This Roman classic showcases the magic of combining quality ingredients in the right proportions. Crispy pancetta, yielding its fat to the pan, mingles with soft onions and fierce chilli. A splash of white wine adds acidity and complexity. The result is a robust, warming sauce that transforms simple pasta into something memorable. Pecorino Romano provides a sharp finish.
recipes.ingredients: 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large red onion • 250 grams diced pancetta • 100 ml dry white wine • 1/2 teaspoon dried chilli flakes • 800 grams cherry tomatoes • 3 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • 100 grams Pecorino Romano • Salt to taste • 500 grams linguine

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Linguine with Crab, Fresh Chilli, and Lemon Zest
recipes.overview: This ethereal pasta dish captures the essence of the Amalfi coast. Fresh crab meat is briefly warmed in fruity olive oil with garlic, chilli, and lemon. The sauce is delicate and elegant, allowing the sweet briny notes of the crab to shine. The pasta is simply a vehicle for this liquid gold. This is not a dish to improvise with; quality ingredients and technique are non-negotiable.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams dressed crab • 5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 garlic cloves • 1 medium-hot red chilli • 3 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Zest of 1 lemon • 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice • Salt to taste • 500 grams linguine

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Linguine with Genovese Basil Pesto
recipes.overview: True pesto is made by hand, not blended, but a food processor creates acceptable results efficiently. The beauty of pesto lies in the balance: each ingredient must be the highest quality. The basil must be fresh, the pine nuts toasted, the oil fruity but not aggressive, the Parmesan aged. Together they create something transcendent.
recipes.ingredients: 50 grams fresh basil leaves • 50 grams pine nuts • 1 garlic clove • 130 ml extra virgin olive oil • 25 grams Parmesan • Salt and pepper to taste • 500 grams linguine

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Pasta Puttanesca
recipes.overview: Anchovies dissolve into hot olive oil with garlic and chilli; tomatoes simmer briefly with capers and black olives; the lot tosses with hot spaghetti. Twenty minutes from cupboard to plate. The salty, savoury, briny depth comes from ingredients that keep almost forever.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g spaghetti • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 6 anchovy fillets in oil • 4 garlic cloves • ½ teaspoon dried chilli flakes • 2 tablespoons capers in brine • 100 g pitted black olives • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • A handful of flat-leaf parsley • Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Pasta Shells and Beans with Squid and Chorizo
recipes.overview: This colorful, flavorful dish combines tender squid, spiced chorizo, creamy beans, and bright tomatoes. The squid is scored to create attractive curl patterns when seared at high heat. Orecchiette (small shell pasta) catches the light sauce beautifully. This is as much about presentation as it is flavor, perfect for entertaining.
recipes.ingredients: 100 grams tinned chick peas • 100 grams tinned borlotti beans • 15 cherry tomatoes • 1 medium-hot red chilli • 1 garlic clove • 3 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice • 8 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • Salt to taste • 400 grams squid • 80 grams chorizo • 500 grams orecchiette shells

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Pasta Shells with Sprouting Broccoli, Chilli, and Pine Nuts
recipes.overview: This simple vegetarian dish relies on technique and proper timing to shine. Tender sprouting broccoli is stir-fried with garlic, toasted pine nuts, and a hint of chilli. A splash of white wine adds acidity and complexity. The broccoli must stay al dente, retaining bite and bright green color. Shell pasta combines beautifully with this light style sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 8 tablespoons olive oil • 300 grams sprouting broccoli • 2 garlic cloves • 6 tablespoons pine nuts • 1 medium-hot chilli • 100 ml dry white wine • Salt to taste • 500 grams orecchiette shells • 150 grams Parmesan • 10 fresh basil leaves

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Penne with Red Chicory, Sausages, and Red Wine
recipes.overview: This is peasant cooking with refined sensibilities. Sausage, freed from its casing and crumbled, browns deeply and flavors the oil. Red chicory (radicchio), shredded and quick-cooked, becomes tender yet retains its distinctive bitter-sweet character. Red wine deglazes the pan and adds complexity. A small amount of cream marries everything into a sophisticated sauce that coats each penne tube.
recipes.ingredients: 150 grams top-quality pork sausages • 3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 1 red onion • 2 whole red chicory heads • 100 ml dry red wine • 30 ml double cream • Salt and pepper to taste • 500 grams penne rigate • 2 tablespoons fresh flat leaf parsley • 30 grams Parmesan

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Penne with Red Chillies, Garlic, and Chopped Tomatoes
recipes.overview: This is peasant cooking at its finest: five ingredients combine to create something utterly satisfying. The gentle cooking of tomatoes with chilli and garlic creates a sauce that clings to pasta without cream or oil to muddy it. Fresh flat-leaf parsley adds brightness. This dish proves that simplicity, when executed with care, requires nothing more.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 garlic cloves • 2 medium-hot red chillies • 800 grams chopped tomatoes • 4 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Salt to taste • 500 grams penne rigate

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Ragù with Gnocchi
recipes.overview: This ragù blends deep browning, layered aromatics, and a long, gentle oven braise to create a sauce with rich umami, silky texture, and balanced brightness. A two-milk finish adds softness, while a quick red wine gastrique lifts the sauce at the end.
recipes.ingredients: 100 grams pancetta, finely chopped • 2 carrots, finely diced • 1 onion, finely diced • 2 celery stalks, finely diced • 4 garlic cloves, grated • 400 grams beef mince • 200 grams pork belly, 1 cm dice • 3 tablespoons tomato purée • 150 milliliters whole milk • 200 milliliters red wine • 200 milliliters passata • 1 parmesan rind • 2 bay leaves • 1 pinch freshly grated nutmeg • 500 milliliters chicken or beef stock • 100 milliliters whole milk • 30 grams butter • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 100 grams red wine vinegar • 50 grams caster sugar • 500 grams red-skinned potatoes • 1 egg yolk • 65 grams 00 flour • 1 tablespoon neutral oil • 30 grams Parmesan, grated
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Restaurant-Style Ragù
recipes.overview: True ragu demands patience, precision, and respect for the process. Ground beef (or a beef and pork mix) browns deeply in batches to build caramelization without steaming. Aromatic vegetables soften slowly until sweet. Tomato paste darkens and concentrates its flavor through caramelization. Red wine deglazes and cooks off. Then comes the long, gentle simmer, 1 hour 45 minutes to 2 hours, where flavors meld and deepen into something far greater than the sum of its parts. This is not a quick sauce; it is an investment in excellence.
recipes.ingredients: 2-3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 500 grams ground beef • 75 grams pancetta or bacon • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 1 celery stalk • 3 garlic cloves • 1-2 tablespoons tomato paste • 150 ml dry red or white wine • 400 grams canned tomatoes or passata • 300 ml beef stock or water • 1 bay leaf or small sprig thyme • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 1 tablespoon butter or extra virgin olive oil • Pasta of choice • 1 cup reserved pasta water • Parmesan cheese
ingredientNames: bay leaf or small sprig thyme
salt and freshly ground black pepper
butter or extra virgin olive oil

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Risotto alla Milanese
recipes.overview: Carnaroli or arborio rice toasts briefly in butter and oil with shallot, gets a splash of white wine to evaporate, then takes ladlefuls of warm saffron-infused stock until al dente. Off the heat, beaten with cold butter and parmesan to mantecare (emulsify) into a glossy, just-flowing risotto.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1 litre chicken or vegetable stock • 50 g unsalted butter • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 1 small shallot • 300 g carnaroli or arborio rice • 100 ml dry white wine • 60 g parmesan • Salt and freshly ground white pepper
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground white pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Seafood Linguine with Chilli and White Wine
recipes.overview: This is seafood in its purest form: clams, mussels, prawns, and squid cooked simply in wine and tomato, finished with fresh herbs and pasta. Each component is delicate and must be treated with care. The sauce is light and elegant, allowing the briny sweetness of the shellfish to shine. This is restaurant-quality treatment of premium ingredients.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams clams • 250 grams mussels • 250 grams baby squid • 250 grams uncooked prawns • 100 ml dry white wine • 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 4 garlic cloves • 1/2 teaspoon dried chilli flakes • 800 grams cherry tomatoes • 4 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Zest of 1 unwaxed lemon • Salt to taste • 500 grams linguine

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Spaghetti Marinara
recipes.overview: True spaghetti alla marinara celebrates the bounty of Naples' harbor. A rich tomato base simmers gently whilst fresh shellfish and seafood are seared and simmered separately, then folded into the sauce at the last moment. Each component maintains its distinct texture and flavor while harmonizing on the plate. This is sophisticated Italian bistro cooking at its finest.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 2 garlic cloves • 425 grams tinned tomatoes • 125 ml white wine • 1 teaspoon sugar • 20 black mussels • 200 grams clams • 125 grams calamari rings • 125 grams skinless firm white fish fillets • 200 grams raw prawns • 60 ml white wine • 60 ml fish stock • 1 garlic clove • 30 grams butter • 375 grams spaghetti • 10 grams flat leaf parsley

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Spaghetti with Beef Meatballs
recipes.overview: Beef meatballs represent the heart of Italian home cooking, simple ingredients transformed through technique into something greater than their parts. Lemon zest and fresh herbs brighten the earthy beef, while coating in seasoned flour creates a golden crust that protects the tender interior. A wine-enriched sauce catches every drop of savory liquid. This is pure comfort.
recipes.ingredients: 1 onion • 750 grams beef mince • 140 grams fresh breadcrumbs • 35 grams Parmesan • 2 tablespoons flat leaf parsley • 1 egg • 1 garlic clove • Rind and juice of 1/2 unwaxed lemon • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 68 grams plain flour • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 425 grams passata • 1 tablespoon tomato purée • 120 grams beef stock • 120 grams red wine • 2 tablespoons fresh basil • 1 garlic clove • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 500 grams spaghetti
ingredientNames: rind and juice of 1/2 unwaxed lemon
salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Spaghetti with Chicken Meatballs
recipes.overview: Chicken meatballs represent the healthier, lighter side of Italian comfort food. These tender spheres, bound with Parmesan and fresh herbs, nestle into a long-simmered tomato sauce that builds depth through patient cooking. The combination is less heavy than beef but more refined, with a subtlety that lets fresh basil and oregano shine. This is family cooking at its best.
recipes.ingredients: 500 grams chicken mince • 60 grams Parmesan • 160 grams fresh white breadcrumbs • 2 garlic cloves • 1 egg • 1 tablespoon flat leaf parsley • 1 tablespoon sage • Black pepper to taste • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 1 onion • 2 kg ripe tomatoes • 2 bay leaves • 30 grams fresh basil leaves • 1 teaspoon course ground black pepper • 500 grams spaghetti • 2 tablespoons fresh oregano • Extra Parmesan

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Spaghetti with Ricotta Cheese and Toasted Pine Nuts
recipes.overview: This is pasta in its most elegant minimalist form. Ricotta cheese, warmed by fresh pasta and enriched with good olive oil, becomes a delicate sauce that clings to each strand. Toasted pine nuts add textural contrast and subtle sweetness, while sun-dried tomatoes provide concentrated flavor and color. The dish is perfect year-round as a simple first course or light main.
recipes.ingredients: 6 tablespoons pine nuts • 250 grams ricotta cheese • 100 grams sun-dried tomatoes in oil • 3 tablespoons fresh chives • 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg • 10 fresh basil leaves • 4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 tablespoons hot water • Salt and pepper to taste • 500 grams spaghetti

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Spaghetti with Yellow Peppers, Chilli, and Herbs
recipes.overview: This vegetarian dish proves that meat is optional, not essential, for creating satisfying pasta. The peppers, cooked slowly until they release their natural sweetness, create a silken sauce. Fresh herbs provide layered flavor without one dominating. This is simple food made with technique and quality ingredients. Aubergines can substitute for peppers if preferred.
recipes.ingredients: 8 tablespoons olive oil • 2 cloves garlic • 4 yellow peppers • 1 teaspoon dried chilli flakes • 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves • 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary leaves • 2 tablespoons fresh flat-leaf parsley • Salt to taste • 500 grams spaghetti

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Spaghettini with Scallops and Parsley Pesto
recipes.overview: This is an elegant pasta that celebrates premium seafood and the beauty of simplicity. Scallops, delicate, buttery, slightly sweet, are seared briefly to preserve their tender texture. A vibrant parsley pesto joins them, enriched with pine nuts and capers for complexity. The combination is sophisticated enough for guests, yet takes only minutes to prepare. Restaurant-quality speed and refinement.
recipes.ingredients: 45 grams salted butter • 250 grams small scallops • Salt and pepper to taste • 50 grams fresh flat leaf parsley • 50 grams pine nuts • 2 tablespoons salted capers • 1 garlic clove • 130 ml extra virgin olive oil • Zest of 1 unwaxed lemon • 500 grams spaghettini

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Spicy Chorizo Ricotta Ravioli
recipes.overview: These are show-stopping pasta parcels filled with delicate cheese and fiery chorizo, balanced by fresh parsley and the natural heat of chillies. Making ravioli from scratch demands patience and precision, but the reward is tender pasta envelopes that hug their savory-spicy filling. The cooking is quick, just 3 minutes in boiling water, finished with nothing but fruity olive oil and cracked pepper.
recipes.ingredients: 400 grams fresh pasta dough • 2 eggs • 2 tablespoons plain flour • 5 tablespoons olive oil • 300 grams Spanish chorizo • 3 medium-hot chillies • 750 grams fresh ricotta cheese • 3 tablespoons fresh flat leaf parsley • Salt and pepper to taste • 200 ml extra virgin olive oil • Salt • Freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Spicy Tagliatelle with Peppers, Courgettes, and Thyme
recipes.overview: This is vegetarian cooking at its most vibrant. Fresh vegetables, barely cooked to preserve their texture and color, toss with delicate egg tagliatelle in a simple, clean preparation. There's no cream here, just good olive oil and the vegetable juices mingling with warm pasta. The result is light, elegant, and completely satisfying.
recipes.ingredients: 8 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 red onions • 2 yellow peppers • 1 red pepper • 1 courgette • 1/2 teaspoon dried chilli flakes • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves • Salt to taste • 400 grams fresh tagliatelle

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Tagliatelle with Prawns and Brandy Cream Sauce
recipes.overview: This is Italian elegance designed for romantic dinners. Fresh prawns, cooked at precisely the right moment to preserve their tender sweetness, meet a luxurious brandy-cream sauce enriched with toasted walnuts and brightened by balsamic vinegar and cherry tomatoes. The sauce is silky, the flavor sophisticated, the preparation surprisingly quick. Restaurant quality achieved at home.
recipes.ingredients: 30 grams salted butter • 4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 2 shallots • 60 grams walnuts • 300 grams uncooked prawns • 10 cherry tomatoes • 60 ml brandy • 250 ml double cream • 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar • Salt and pepper to taste • 400 grams fresh egg tagliatelle • 2 tablespoons fresh flat leaf parsley

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Tagliatelle with Sausage, Rosemary, and Porcini Mushroom
recipes.overview: This is rustic Italian cooking elevated to elegance. Sausage meat, freed from its casing and crumbled, browns deeply to build complex flavor. Porcini mushrooms, reconstituted in warm water and chopped, contribute their distinctive earthiness. Fresh rosemary provides brightness against the rich cream sauce. The combination is hearty, satisfying, and deeply flavorful.
recipes.ingredients: 400 grams Italian sausage • 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 1 leek • 2 tablespoons fresh rosemary leaves • 50 grams dried porcini mushrooms • 100 ml dry white wine • 150 ml double cream • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 500 grams fresh tagliatelle
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

subcategories.overview: A tale of regions, where the same dish changes character every fifty miles: northern butter and rice, central pasta and pork, southern tomatoes, olives and seafood. Herbs (basil, rosemary, sage) and aged cheeses balance simple sauces against long-cooked ragùs. Hand-rolled pasta, slow braises, wood-fired baking and the patient build of risotto define the techniques.

subcategories.label: Jamaican

recipes.title: Callaloo
recipes.overview: Onion, garlic and tomato cook in oil until softened. Callaloo (or spinach) goes in by the handful; thyme and scotch bonnet add. Everything covers and steam-cooks until the greens are tender. Salt, pepper, a splash of water if needed; finish quickly so the colour stays vivid.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g fresh callaloo • 3 tablespoons coconut oil or vegetable oil • 1 medium onion • 4 garlic cloves • 4 spring onions • 2 medium tomatoes • 4 sprigs fresh thyme • 1 scotch bonnet chilli or sliced for more heat • ½ teaspoon ground black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • 100 ml water
ingredientNames: scotch bonnet chilli or sliced for more heat

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Caribbean Chicken Curry Stew
recipes.overview: In many Caribbean stew dishes there is an initial step of burning sugar in oil which is used to brown the meat in. This adds a very unique sweetness to the stews from this region and this sweetness paired with the unmistakable flavour (and heat) from the wonderful scotch bonnet chilli is simply astounding. This curry uses curry powder for a fragrant and delicious result that captures the essence of Caribbean cooking.
recipes.ingredients: Ginger • 6 cloves of garlic • 1 tsp salt • ½ tsp black peppercorns • 4 spring onions • Large pinch coriander • Large pinch parsley • 1 scotch bonnet • 2 tbsp vegetable oil • 55g brown sugar • 3 tsp curry powder • 600ml water or stock • 1 bay leaf • 2 thyme sprigs • 1 scotch bonnet • 1 shot of dark rum • 900g chicken

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Rasta Pasta
recipes.overview: A vibrant Jamaican pasta dish where tender prawns are coated in jerk seasoning and tossed with peppers, spinach, and a creamy sauce. The jerk spicing brings heat and characteristic Caribbean flavours to this contemporary twist on traditional cuisine.
recipes.ingredients: 227 grams penne pasta • 454 grams prawns • 1 tbsp jerk seasoning • 2 tbsp vegetable oil • 1 red bell pepper • 1 orange bell pepper • 60 grams fresh spinach • 2 spring onions • 4 garlic cloves • 170 ml pasta water • 170 ml double cream • 100 grams Parmesan cheese • Salt and pepper to taste

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Rice and Peas
recipes.overview: Soaked kidney beans simmer with coconut milk, scallion, thyme, garlic, allspice and a whole scotch bonnet (left whole, never pierced). Once tender, rice goes in with stock to top up. The pot covers; rice steam-absorbs the bean broth; the scotch bonnet stays whole and gets removed before serving.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g dried red kidney beans • 400 ml coconut milk • 1 litre water • 4 spring onions • 5 sprigs fresh thyme • 4 garlic cloves • 6 allspice berries • 1 scotch bonnet chilli • 1½ teaspoons salt • Black pepper • 500 g long-grain white rice

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Spicy, smoky cooking, fusing African, Indian and British colonial influences with native ingredients. Allspice, scotch bonnet, thyme, ginger and lime drive jerk marinades; coconut and beans anchor rice and one-pot stews. Slow grilling over pimento wood, escovitch frying and patient simmering of curries and stews carry the flavour.

subcategories.label: Japanese

recipes.title: Beef Sukiyaki
recipes.overview: A wide shallow pot is brushed with beef fat, the warishita sauce (soy-mirin-sake-sugar) is poured in, then thinly sliced beef and a colourful array of vegetables, tofu and shirataki noodles are added in batches as people eat. Each piece dips in raw egg yolk before going in the mouth. A portable hob at the table is traditional but not required.
recipes.ingredients: 200 ml soy sauce • 200 ml mirin • 100 ml sake • 4 tablespoons caster sugar • 200 ml dashi • 600 g thinly sliced beef rib-eye or sirloin • 1 small piece beef suet or 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 4 spring onions • 200 g shiitake or chestnut mushrooms • 100 g enoki mushrooms • 200 g firm tofu • ½ Chinese cabbage • 1 bunch chrysanthemum greens , trimmed • 200 g shirataki noodles • 4 raw egg yolks • Cooked Japanese short-grain rice
ingredientNames: thinly sliced beef rib-eye or sirloin
small piece beef suet or 1 tablespoon vegetable oil

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Chicken Katsu
recipes.overview: Chicken breasts are flattened, dredged in flour, egg and panko, then shallow-fried until the crust is deep golden and shatteringly crisp. Served sliced over rice with shredded cabbage and a katsu sauce built on Worcestershire and ketchup.
recipes.ingredients: 4 chicken breasts • 4 tablespoons plain flour • 2 eggs • 150 g panko breadcrumbs • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil for shallow-frying • 4 tablespoons tonkatsu sauce, OR • 4 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce + 4 tablespoons ketchup + 2 teaspoons soy sauce + 2 teaspoons sugar • 300 g cooked Japanese short-grain rice • ¼ small white cabbage • 1 lemon
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
worcestershire sauce + 4 tablespoons ketchup + 2 teaspoons soy sauce + 2 teaspoons sugar

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Gyudon
recipes.overview: Thinly sliced beef simmered briefly in dashi with mirin, sake, soy and sugar, alongside sliced onion. The whole pile spooned over rice with the broth. Topped with pickled ginger and a soft-boiled egg if you like.
recipes.ingredients: 200 ml dashi • 3 tablespoons soy sauce • 3 tablespoons mirin • 2 tablespoons sake • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 1 onion • 300 g thinly sliced beef • 2 bowls cooked Japanese short-grain rice • 1 spring onion • 2 tablespoons benishōga • 2 soft-boiled eggs • Shichimi togarashi

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Honey Soy Salmon (One-Pan)
recipes.overview: Salmon fillets sear skin-side down for crisp skin, then bathe in a quick honey-soy-mirin-ginger reduction that lacquers them. Broccoli or bok choy steams in the same pan with a splash of stock. Twenty minutes; one pan; serve over rice.
recipes.ingredients: 4 skin-on salmon fillets • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • Salt • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 3 tablespoons honey • 2 tablespoons mirin • 1 tablespoon grated fresh ginger • 2 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil • 1 head broccoli or 4 heads bok choy • 50 ml chicken stock or water • 2 spring onions • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds • Cooked rice, to serve
ingredientNames: head broccoli or 4 heads bok choy

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Miso Soup
recipes.overview: Make dashi (or use instant), bring just to a simmer, drop in cubed silken tofu and rehydrated wakame seaweed, then whisk in miso paste off the heat. Boiling the miso destroys its delicate flavour and aroma.
recipes.ingredients: 800 ml water • 10 g kombu , or skip and use 1 teaspoon hondashi • 10 g bonito flakes , or use hondashi • 4 tablespoons white miso paste • 200 g silken tofu • 5 g dried wakame seaweed • 2 spring onions

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Oyakodon
recipes.overview: Chicken thigh chunks simmer in dashi-mirin-soy broth with sliced onion until tender, beaten egg pours in and sets in soft curds, the lot slides over rice. The egg should still be slightly runny when you turn off the heat.
recipes.ingredients: 200 ml dashi • 3 tablespoons soy sauce • 3 tablespoons mirin • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 300 g chicken thigh • 1 onion • 4 large eggs • 2 bowls cooked Japanese short-grain rice • 1 spring onion • A pinch of shichimi togarashi

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Pork Gyoza
recipes.overview: A pork mince filling spiked with garlic, ginger, soy, sesame oil and finely chopped cabbage is wrapped in shop-bought gyoza skins, then pan-fried on the flat bottom and finished by steaming with a splash of water in the lidded pan. Serve with a soy-rice-vinegar-chilli oil dip.
recipes.ingredients: 300 g pork mince • 200 g Chinese cabbage • 1 teaspoon salt • 3 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 2 spring onions • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil • 1 teaspoon sugar • ½ teaspoon white pepper • 30 round gyoza wrappers • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 100 ml water • A small bowl of water • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 tablespoons rice vinegar • 1 teaspoon chilli oil • 1 spring onion

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Shoyu Ramen
recipes.overview: Of the four main ramen styles, shoyu (soy) is the cleanest and most aromatic, leaning on a clear roasted-chicken broth rather than the milky richness of tonkotsu or the heavy punch of miso. The technique splits the work: a long, gentle simmer with kombu and shiitake builds the broth, and a separate seasoning sauce called tare goes in bowl by bowl at the end. That last step is what lets you tune the saltiness without compromising the broth's clarity.
recipes.ingredients: 2 kg chicken wings • 2 carrots • 2 x 20 cm pieces kombu • 6 dried shiitake mushrooms • 6 spring onions • 1 head of garlic • 4 cm piece ginger • ¼ cup soy sauce • 12 cups water • ¼ cup soy sauce • 2 tablespoons mirin • Cooked ramen noodles • Chashu pork • Medium-soft boiled eggs • Reserved shiitake mushrooms • Finely sliced spring onion

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Teriyaki Salmon
recipes.overview: Skin-on salmon fillets are seared skin-side down to crisp, flipped briefly, then bathed in a four-ingredient teriyaki glaze that reduces to a glossy lacquer. The pan-sear gives texture; the glaze gives shine and savoury sweetness.
recipes.ingredients: 4 skin-on salmon fillets • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • Salt • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 4 tablespoons mirin • 2 tablespoons sake • 2 tablespoons caster sugar • 1 teaspoon grated fresh ginger • 300 g cooked Japanese short-grain rice • 1 spring onion • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds • Steamed broccoli or pak choi

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Tonkatsu
recipes.overview: Thick pork loin steaks are scored, seasoned, breaded with flour-egg-panko, and shallow-fried until the crust is shatter-crisp. Served with cabbage so finely shredded it's practically a slaw, and a sticky-sweet katsu sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 4 pork loin steaks • 4 tablespoons plain flour • 2 eggs • 150 g panko breadcrumbs • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Vegetable oil for shallow-frying • 4 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce • 4 tablespoons ketchup • 2 teaspoons soy sauce • 2 teaspoons sugar • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard • 300 g cooked Japanese short-grain rice • ¼ small white cabbage • 1 lemon • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Vegetable and Prawn Tempura
recipes.overview: Ice-cold sparkling water meets soft flour in a barely-stirred batter; the batter goes onto sliced vegetables and prawns and into 180°C oil for 90 seconds. The lumps and dry patches in the batter are the goal; smooth batter gives a heavy crust.
recipes.ingredients: 8 large raw king prawns • 1 sweet potato • 1 small aubergine • 1 courgette • 8 green beans • 8 shiitake mushrooms • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • 150 g plain flour • 50 g cornflour • 250 ml sparkling water • 1 large egg yolk • A few ice cubes • 200 ml dashi • 3 tablespoons soy sauce • 3 tablespoons mirin • 2 tablespoons grated daikon radish • 1 teaspoon grated fresh ginger

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Vegetable Tempura
recipes.overview: The batter is the technique: ice-cold sparkling water, plain flour, an egg yolk, mixed barely — the lumps and undissolved flour are deliberate. Vegetables are dusted in flour first to grip the batter, then dipped and dropped into 175°C oil. Each piece fries 90 seconds to 2 minutes. The dipping sauce of dashi, soy and mirin warms; daikon and ginger grate fresh.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g plain flour • 1 large egg yolk • 300 ml ice-cold sparkling water • A handful of ice cubes • 1 sweet potato • 8 shiitake mushrooms • 1 small kabocha squash • 1 medium aubergine • 8 green beans • 1 red onion • 8 shiso leaves • 50 g plain flour • Vegetable oil • 200 ml dashi • 4 tablespoons light soy sauce • 4 tablespoons mirin • 100 g daikon • 2 cm fresh ginger

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Yakitori
recipes.overview: Chicken thigh and spring onion are threaded alternately on bamboo skewers, grilled hot to char the outside while the centre stays juicy, and brushed repeatedly with a reduced soy-mirin-sake-sugar tare. Salt-only versions (shio) and tare-glazed (tare) are both classics.
recipes.ingredients: 100 ml soy sauce • 100 ml mirin • 50 ml sake • 2 tablespoons caster sugar • 1 garlic clove • 1 cm ginger • 600 g boneless chicken thigh • 6 spring onions or 2 leeks • 12 bamboo skewers • A pinch of salt • Shichimi togarashi

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Yasai Ramen
recipes.overview: Mushroom (dried shiitake) and kombu (kelp) infuse hot water for the broth base. Garlic-and-ginger oil fries; miso paste, soy and mirin make the tare. Noodles cook fresh; everything assembles in deep bowls with toppings stacked artfully.
recipes.ingredients: 1.2 litres hot water • 25 g dried shiitake mushrooms • 1 piece kombu • 4 spring onions • 4 garlic cloves • 4 cm fresh ginger • 2 tablespoons sesame oil • 4 tablespoons white or red miso paste • 3 tablespoons light soy sauce • 2 tablespoons mirin • 1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine • 1 teaspoon sugar • 200 g firm tofu • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon soy sauce • 2 large eggs • 1 small bok choy or pak choi • 100 g sweet corn • 4 spring onions • 1 sheet nori • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds • A few drops chilli oil • 200 g fresh or 150 g dried ramen noodles
ingredientNames: small bok choy or pak choi

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Precise, restrained cooking that prizes seasonality, simple knife work and the umami of dashi, miso and soy. Flavours come from soy sauce, mirin, sake, miso, kombu, bonito and the bright counterpoints of yuzu, ginger and pickled vegetables. Defining techniques include the long, slow extraction of dashi, careful poaching and simmering, the high-heat sear of yakimono, and the layered seasoning of a proper ramen broth and tare.

subcategories.label: Jewish

recipes.title: Chocolate Babka
recipes.overview: A rich enriched dough is mixed and kneaded; first rise overnight in the fridge for the best flavour and easier handling. The next day, the cold dough rolls into a wide rectangle; chocolate filling spreads thick to the edges; rolls into a tight log. The log is sliced lengthwise, twisted into a braid (cut sides facing up to expose the chocolate), tucked into a loaf tin and proofed. Bakes; brushes with sugar syrup hot from the oven.
recipes.prepTime: 30 minutes (plus 2 hour first rise + 1 hour second rise)
