recipes.ingredients: 500 g strong white bread flour • 100 g caster sugar • 1 sachet fast-action yeast • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 large eggs • 100 ml whole milk • 150 g unsalted butter • 200 g dark chocolate • 150 g unsalted butter • 80 g icing sugar • 3 tablespoons cocoa powder • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • 100 g caster sugar • 100 ml water

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Chopped Egg and Onion
recipes.overview: Eggs hard-boil and cool. Onions cook slowly in oil or schmaltz until deeply golden and sweet. Both chop together with a fork or knife (not a food processor — the texture matters); salt and pepper season. Serve at room temperature.
recipes.ingredients: 6 large eggs • 2 medium onions • 4 tablespoons schmaltz or sunflower oil • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon white pepper • Rye bread, matzo, or crackers • Sliced cucumber or radish

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Jewish Brisket
recipes.overview: Brisket sears in a heavy pot; onions caramelise on the rendered fat; garlic and tomato paste deepen with paprika and brown sugar. Wine and stock loosen; the brisket goes back in fat-side up; the lot covers and braises low and slow for 3+ hours. Cool overnight; slice cold against the grain; reheat in the sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 2 kg beef brisket • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • Salt and black pepper • 4 large onions • 8 garlic cloves • 3 tablespoons tomato paste • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • 1 tablespoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper • 250 ml dry red wine • 750 ml beef stock • 4 carrots • 4 celery sticks • 3 bay leaves • 4 sprigs fresh thyme • 2 tablespoons cider vinegar • 1 teaspoon salt

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Latkes
recipes.overview: Potatoes and onion are coarsely grated; the mixture is squeezed bone-dry in a cloth (this is the difference between great latkes and soggy ones). Egg and matzo meal (or flour) bind; the lot fries in shallow oil over medium-high heat until each side is crisp and deeply golden. Drain and salt; eat hot.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg floury potatoes • 1 large onion • 2 large eggs • 4 tablespoons matzo meal • 1½ teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • Sunflower or vegetable oil for shallow frying • Soured cream • Apple sauce • Smoked salmon
ingredientNames: sunflower or vegetable oil for shallow frying

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

subcategories.overview: Ashkenazi and Sephardic kitchen traditions joined by religious calendar and dietary law. The Ashkenazi side is rich, eggy and slow-cooked: brisket, cholent, matzo ball soup, latkes, babka. The Sephardic side leans Mediterranean: hummus, mahshi, sabich, sfenj. Schmaltz (rendered chicken fat) and dill drive Ashkenazi flavour; tahini, lemon and za'atar drive Sephardic. The Sabbath stew, the Hanukkah fry, the Passover seder and the year-round bagel-and-cream-cheese all sit here.

subcategories.label: Jordan

recipes.title: Atayef
recipes.overview: A loose batter of fine semolina, plain flour, yeast, sugar and warm water rests until bubbly. Small disks pour onto a hot dry pan and cook one side only — bubbles rise and burst, the top setting porous and lacy. Each disk folds round a filling (sweetened cheese or spiced walnut), pinches into a half-moon, then fries in oil until deep golden. Cold sugar syrup pours over hot pancakes; serve immediately.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g fine semolina • 100 g plain flour • 1 teaspoon fast-action yeast • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • ½ teaspoon salt • 600 ml warm water • 200 g walnuts • 4 tablespoons caster sugar • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 250 g akkawi or low-salt halloumi • 2 tablespoons caster sugar • 250 g caster sugar • 150 ml water • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 1 teaspoon rosewater • 1 teaspoon orange blossom water • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • Crushed pistachios

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Galayet Bandora
recipes.overview: Whole peeled garlic cloves and chillies fry in a generous pour of olive oil until aromatic. Roughly chopped ripe tomatoes go in; they collapse, release their water, then thicken into a rough stew. Salt at the end; sometimes a sprinkle of dried mint. Eaten warm with khubz (Arabic flatbread) for scooping.
recipes.ingredients: 6 large ripe tomatoes • 8 garlic cloves • 2 long green chillies • 60 ml olive oil • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon dried mint • A small handful flat-leaf parsley • Warm flatbread

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Mansaf
recipes.overview: Dried jameed balls soak overnight then break down in water to form the cooking liquid. Lamb shanks or shoulder simmer in spiced water until tender. The lamb stock combines with the jameed liquid; both reduce together with cardamom, cinnamon and bay. Rice cooks separately; flatbread layers under everything; lamb perches on top; the jameed sauce ladles over generously. Almonds and pine nuts toast as topping.
recipes.ingredients: 1.5 kg lamb shoulder • 4 jameed balls • 2 large onions • 6 cardamom pods • 1 cinnamon stick • 4 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1½ teaspoons salt • 1.5 litres water • 500 g long-grain or basmati rice • 50 g unsalted butter • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 sheets shrak bread or markook • 50 g flaked almonds • 50 g pine nuts • 30 g unsalted butter • A handful of flat-leaf parsley

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Jordanian cooking centred on the Bedouin tradition: lamb, rice, yogurt, bread. Mansaf, the national dish, slow-cooks lamb in fermented dried-yogurt (jameed) sauce over rice and shrak flatbread, eaten communally with the right hand. Maglouba (upside-down rice and meat), galayet bandora (tomato stew), kibbeh and meze plates fill the table; knafeh and atayef close the meal.

subcategories.label: Korean

recipes.title: Bibimbap
recipes.overview: Each vegetable cooks separately and gets dressed with sesame oil, garlic and soy. They arrange in colourful piles around a mound of rice; an egg fries on top. Gochujang sauce on the side. Diners mix vigorously before eating.
recipes.ingredients: 300 g short-grain rice • 200 g spinach • 200 g bean sprouts • 1 carrot • 200 g shiitake or chestnut mushrooms • 1 small courgette • 4 tablespoons toasted sesame oil • 4 garlic cloves • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 4 teaspoons toasted sesame seeds • Salt • 200 g rib-eye or sirloin • 1 tablespoon soy sauce • 1 tablespoon sugar • 1 teaspoon sesame oil • 1 garlic clove • 4 eggs • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 4 tablespoons gochujang • 2 tablespoons toasted sesame oil • 2 tablespoons rice vinegar • 1 tablespoon honey • 1 tablespoon water

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Bulgogi
recipes.overview: Rib-eye or sirloin sliced paper-thin sits in a marinade of soy, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger and grated Asian pear (or apple), then sears hard in a screaming-hot pan or on a BBQ. Served wrapped in lettuce leaves with rice and ssamjang.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g rib-eye or sirloin • 1 ripe Asian pear , grated • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • 2 tablespoons toasted sesame oil • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon grated ginger • 1 small onion • 4 spring onions • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds • 1 tablespoon mirin or rice wine • Cooked short-grain rice • 1 head butter lettuce or red-leaf lettuce • 1 cucumber • Kimchi • Ssamjang
ingredientNames: head butter lettuce or red-leaf lettuce

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Dak Galbi
recipes.overview: Boneless chicken thighs cube up and marinate in a gochujang-soy-garlic-ginger paste with mirin and sugar. They stir-fry hard with onion, sweet potato chunks, cabbage and Korean rice cakes (tteok). Often finished with cheese melted over for the modern variant; classic version skips it.
recipes.ingredients: 800 g boneless chicken thighs • 4 tablespoons gochujang • 2 tablespoons gochugaru • 3 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 tablespoons mirin • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon grated ginger • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 onion • 1 small sweet potato • ¼ small cabbage • 200 g Korean rice cakes , soaked in warm water for 10 minutes if using dried • 4 spring onions • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds • 100 g mozzarella • Cooked short-grain rice • Lettuce leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Japchae
recipes.overview: Sweet potato glass noodles (dangmyeon) boil and toss in a soy-sesame sauce. Vegetables (spinach, carrot, mushrooms, onion, peppers) sauté separately to keep their colour and texture, and beef stir-fries fast. Everything tosses together with the sauced noodles and a final sesame finish.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g sweet potato glass noodles • 5 tablespoons soy sauce • 3 tablespoons brown sugar • 3 tablespoons toasted sesame oil • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon mirin or rice wine • 200 g rib-eye or sirloin • 1 onion • 1 carrot • 1 red pepper • 200 g spinach • 200 g shiitake or chestnut mushrooms • 4 spring onions • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • Toasted sesame seeds, to finish

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Kimchi Jjigae
recipes.overview: Pork belly slices brown in a heavy pot, joined by chopped sour kimchi (the older the better) and its juice. Stock and gochujang go in; the lot simmers until the pork is tender and the broth is dark and thick. Tofu cubes finish; spring onion scattered. Eaten with rice.
recipes.ingredients: 300 g sour kimchi • 200 g pork belly or pork shoulder cubes • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon gochujang • 1 tablespoon gochugaru • 1 teaspoon doenjang • 1 teaspoon sugar • 700 ml water or stock • 200 g firm tofu • 4 spring onions • 2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil • Cooked short-grain rice, to serve
ingredientNames: pork belly or pork shoulder cubes

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Korean Fried Chicken
recipes.overview: Chicken wings (or boneless thighs) get a light cornflour-and-flour coating and fry low and slow first to cook through. They cool, then fry hot and fast for the crisp. The sauce is a quick reduction of gochujang, gochugaru, soy, garlic, ginger, honey and rice vinegar; the just-fried chicken tosses in the sauce and gets eaten immediately.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg chicken wings or boneless thighs • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon ground white pepper • 100 g cornflour • 50 g plain flour • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • 3 tablespoons gochujang • 1 tablespoon gochugaru • 3 tablespoons soy sauce • 4 tablespoons honey • 2 tablespoons rice vinegar • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon grated ginger • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil • 2 tablespoons water • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds • 2 spring onions

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: A cuisine built on banchan (the small side dishes that fill the table) and the alchemy of fermentation. Gochujang and gochugaru chillies, doenjang fermented soybean paste, soy and toasted sesame oil drive the flavour; kimchi appears at almost every meal. Live grilling at the table (bulgogi, dak galbi), wok-quick stir-fries, layered bibimbap rice bowls and slow-cooked stews like jjigae define the experience.

subcategories.label: Lebanese

recipes.title: Kibbeh
recipes.overview: Fine bulgur soaks until soft. The shell mixture combines bulgur, raw minced lamb, finely-grated onion, salt and spices, then blitzes (or pounds) into a dense, smooth, almost claylike paste. The filling is a separate cooked mince of lamb, onion, pine nuts, allspice and cinnamon. Each kibbeh shell is shaped over a finger; filling stuffs in; the lot pinches closed into a pointed oval. Deep-fries 5 minutes.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g fine bulgur • 500 g lean minced lamb • 1 large onion • 1 teaspoon ground allspice • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1½ teaspoons salt • 100 ml ice water • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 large onion • 300 g minced lamb • 50 g pine nuts • 1 teaspoon ground allspice • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • A small handful of flat-leaf parsley • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • 300 g plain yogurt • 2 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons fresh mint • ½ teaspoon salt

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Mahalabia
recipes.overview: Cornflour mixes with cold milk to a slurry; the rest of the milk warms with cream and sugar; the slurry pours in and the mixture thickens to a thin custard in 4-5 minutes. Off the heat, rosewater and orange blossom water perfume; the lot pours into glasses. Cold; topped at the table with sugar syrup and pistachios.
recipes.ingredients: 1 litre whole milk • 200 ml double cream • 100 g caster sugar • 70 g cornflour • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 1 tablespoon orange blossom water • 80 g caster sugar • 80 ml water • 1 teaspoon rosewater • 50 g pistachios • A few rose petals

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Tabbouleh
recipes.overview: Bulgur soaks briefly in lemon juice — not water — so it tenderises while taking on flavour. Parsley is washed, dried thoroughly, and chopped to fine green confetti. Tomato dices small and drains. Mint and spring onion go in fine. Everything tosses with olive oil, lemon, salt, pepper. No need to rest; eat right away.
recipes.ingredients: 50 g fine bulgur • Juice of 2 lemons • 4 large bunches flat-leaf parsley • 1 small bunch mint • 4 spring onions • 4 medium ripe tomatoes • 6 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ¼ teaspoon ground allspice • ¼ teaspoon ground cinnamon

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Lebanon's mezze culture runs all the way through: small plates, generous oils, abundant herbs. Hummus, baba ganoush, tabbouleh, fattoush, kibbeh, sambousek and kafta share the table; man'oushe (za'atar flatbread) starts the day; mahalabia and baklava end it. Olive oil, lemon, garlic, parsley, mint and the seven-spice blend (allspice, cinnamon, cloves, cumin, ginger, nutmeg, fenugreek) shape the kitchen; charcoal grilling, slow simmers and patient meze building define the table.

subcategories.label: Malaysian

recipes.title: Ayam Goreng (Malaysian Fried Chicken)
recipes.overview: Bone-in chicken pieces are marinated with a freshly pounded spice paste of shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, lemongrass and turmeric, then dipped in a thin egg and cornflour batter and shallow-fried until golden. The result is a deeply spiced, crackly-crusted fried chicken that picks up colour from the turmeric and depth from the toasted seeds. An overnight marinate is optional but rewards the wait.
recipes.ingredients: 1 teaspoon fennel seeds • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 3 red shallots • 4 garlic cloves • 4 cm piece ginger • 4 cm piece galangal • 1 lemongrass stalk • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 2 teaspoons sea salt • 8 pieces chicken • 1 egg • 1 cup cornflour • Vegetable oil for deep frying • Sea salt to taste

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Hokkien Mee
recipes.overview: Thick fresh egg noodles are stir-fried hard in rendered pork fat, then braised in a glossy gravy of dark sweet soy, oyster sauce and a touch of sugar. Pork belly, prawns and cabbage round out the wok, with a final shower of crackling pork croutons providing the signature crunch. A fast, intensely flavoured dish that hinges on a hot wok and good kecap manis.
recipes.ingredients: 200 grams skinless pork belly • 1 tablespoon soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sesame oil • ¼ teaspoon ground white pepper • 2 tablespoons oyster sauce • 2/3 cup dark sweet soy sauce • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sugar • 4 garlic cloves • 200 grams pork belly • 8 prawns • 2 cups sliced cabbage • 400 grams cooked hokkien noodles • 1 tablespoon cornflour mixed with 1 tablespoon water

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Malaysian Curry Laksa (Laksa Lemak)
recipes.overview: A two-part dish: a deeply concentrated prawn-and-chicken stock built from roasted prawn shells, layered with a freshly pounded laksa paste of dried chilli, galangal, lemongrass and candlenuts. The two are joined with coconut cream to create a glossy, fragrant broth that bathes rice vermicelli, tofu puffs and prawns. Finished at the table with sambal, lime, fresh coriander and bean sprouts.
recipes.ingredients: 600 grams whole raw prawns • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 8 cups chicken stock • 2 bone-in chicken thigh pieces • 8 large dried red chillies • 1 tablespoon dried shrimp • 1 tablespoon chopped fresh galangal • 1 tablespoon chopped ginger • 1 lemongrass stalk • 4 Asian red shallots • 5 garlic cloves • 3 coriander roots • 5 candlenuts or macadamia nuts • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • ½ teaspoon turmeric • 1½ teaspoons shrimp paste • Sea salt • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 400 ml can coconut cream • 12 fried tofu puffs • 4 tablespoons fish sauce • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • 200 grams dried rice vermicelli noodles • Sambal oelek • Lime wedges • Finely chopped coriander • Bean sprouts

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Malaysian Dhal Curry
recipes.overview: Yellow split peas (chana dal) simmer with onion, turmeric and tomato until very soft. The lentils break down into a thick soup. Right at the end, a tempering of mustard seeds, curry leaves, dried red chillies, garlic and shallots is poured sizzling-hot over the surface. The aromatic oil seeps through; the dish transforms.
recipes.ingredients: 250 g chana dal • 1 large onion • 3 garlic cloves • 2 cm ginger • 1 tomato • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • 1 long green chilli • 1.2 litres water • 1½ teaspoons salt • 4 tablespoons coconut oil or vegetable oil • 1 teaspoon black mustard seeds • 1 teaspoon cumin seeds • 4 dried red chillies • 15 fresh curry leaves • 4 garlic cloves • 2 shallots • 1 long red chilli • Juice of half a lime • Fresh coriander • Hot rice or roti canai

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Nasi Lemak
recipes.overview: A platter built around fragrant coconut and lemongrass rice, served with a fiery sambal of dried anchovies and rehydrated chillies, then ringed with hard-boiled egg, sliced cucumber and fried peanuts. The sambal does most of the heavy lifting, sweet, sour, smoky and hot. The rice itself is gentle, designed to be a calming counterpoint to everything else on the plate.
recipes.ingredients: 2 cups jasmine rice • 400 ml can coconut milk • 1 lemongrass stalk • Water • 10 dried long red chillies • 2 fresh long red chillies • 2 red shallots • 1 garlic clove • 1 teaspoon shrimp paste • ¼ cup peanut oil • 1 cup dried anchovies • 1 small red onion • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • 1 tablespoon tamarind puree • Fried peanuts • 2 hard-boiled eggs • Sliced cucumber • Ayam goreng , optional

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Sambal Terung
recipes.overview: Aubergines are deep-fried in batches until soft and lightly charred. The sambal is built fresh: shallot-garlic-chilli paste fried slowly in oil until darker and aromatic, then balanced with tamarind, sugar and salt. The aubergine returns to the wok and tosses through.
recipes.ingredients: 3 medium aubergines • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 small onion • Salt to taste • 6 shallots • 5 garlic cloves • 6 dried red chillies • 2 long red chillies • 1 cm fresh ginger • 3 tablespoons tamarind paste • 2 tablespoons palm sugar • 2 teaspoons light soy sauce • 100 ml water

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Sayur Masak Lemak
recipes.overview: A spice paste of shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass and turmeric is fried in oil. Coconut milk and water simmer with the paste; pumpkin or squash goes in first to soften, beans and aubergine follow, and tofu joins last. The broth thickens lightly; salt and a squeeze of lime finish.
recipes.ingredients: 6 shallots • 4 garlic cloves • 3 cm fresh ginger • 3 cm fresh turmeric • 2 stalks lemongrass • 2 dried red chillies • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 kaffir lime leaves • 1 stalk lemongrass • 400 ml coconut milk • 400 ml water or vegetable stock • 500 g pumpkin or butternut squash • 200 g long beans or green beans • 1 small aubergine • 200 g firm tofu • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 teaspoons sugar • Juice of half a lime

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: A multicultural cuisine where Malay, Chinese (especially Hokkien), Tamil Indian and Peranakan (Nyonya) traditions sit on the same table, shaped by hawker culture and street food. Flavours come from coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaf, belacan (shrimp paste), pandan, tamarind and the sweet darkness of kecap manis. Defining techniques include the freshly pounded rempah spice paste, fast wok stir-fries for noodle dishes, slow-simmered coconut curries and the layered assembly of nasi lemak and laksa.

subcategories.label: Mexican

recipes.title: Authentic Enchilada Sauce
recipes.overview: Rich, robust, and a thousand times better than store-bought, this authentic enchilada sauce packs serious flavour. Made from dried Mexican chillies, roasted aromatics, and traditional spices, this sauce is the foundation of great enchiladas and tacos. The balance of heat, depth, and subtle sweetness creates a versatile sauce that elevates any Mexican dish.
recipes.ingredients: 85g dried ancho peppers • 85g dried guajillo peppers • 2–3 dried arbol peppers • 1 medium white onion • 2 ripe tomatoes • 4 garlic cloves • 1 litre boiling water or chicken broth • 1 teaspoon sea salt • 1 teaspoon dried Mexican oregano • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • Small piece of semi-sweet chocolate • Pinch of brown sugar • 1 tablespoon oil

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Beef Enchiladas
recipes.overview: This Tex-Mex-inspired beef enchilada recipe is richly seasoned, easy to make, and always such a crowd favourite. Tender flour tortillas are filled with seasoned ground beef and black beans, rolled, and smothered in homemade red enchilada sauce, then topped with melted cheese and fresh coriander for a comforting, flavourful dish.
recipes.ingredients: 680g lean ground beef • 1 white onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 4 green chillies • 439g black beans • Salt and pepper to taste • 1 tablespoon oil • 8 large flour tortillas • 339g shredded cheese • 350ml red enchilada sauce • 4g fresh coriander • Sour cream

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Carne Asada
recipes.overview: Skirt or flank steak (cuts that take a marinade well and grill fast) sit in a punchy marinade of lime, orange, garlic, jalapeño, cumin, oregano and coriander for 2-4 hours, then grill quick over high heat. Sliced thin against the grain so the long fibres become tender bites.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg skirt steak • Juice of 4 limes • Juice of 1 large orange • 4 garlic cloves • 1 jalapeño • A handful of fresh coriander • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • Warm corn or flour tortillas • Charred salsa • Lime wedges • Sliced avocado • Chopped coriander

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Chilaquiles Verdes
recipes.overview: Tomatillos, jalapeño, onion and garlic blitz into a green salsa, briefly cooked. Crisp tortilla chips toss into the simmering salsa for 2 minutes — long enough to coat, short enough that they keep some crunch. Topped with eggs and the standard creamy garnishes.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g tomatillos • 1 jalapeño • 1 small onion • 3 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon olive oil • A handful of fresh coriander • ½ teaspoon salt • 200 ml chicken or vegetable stock • 4 cups tortilla chips • 4 eggs • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 100 g queso fresco , crumbled • 4 tablespoons crema or sour cream • ½ red onion • 2 tablespoons chopped coriander

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chiles Rellenos
recipes.overview: Poblanos are charred and skinned, slit and stuffed with melting cheese. They get a coating of egg-white batter (whites whipped to stiff peaks, yolks folded in), fry in shallow oil until puffy and golden. Served in a simple roasted-tomato salsa.
recipes.ingredients: 8 poblano peppers • 400 g Oaxaca cheese, queso fresco, or mozzarella • 6 ripe tomatoes • 1 onion • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon salt • 200 ml chicken stock • 4 large eggs • 4 tablespoons plain flour • A pinch of salt • Vegetable oil for shallow-frying

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Chilli con carne
recipes.overview: Chilli con carne is a hearty, flavour-packed stew that celebrates bold Mexican spicing combined with slow-cooked beef, beans, and tomatoes. This version builds deep, complex flavours through toasted whole spices, umami ingredients (marmite and soy sauce), and slow simmering. The result is a comforting, warming dish that improves with time and is perfect for feeding a crowd.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large onion • 2 garlic cloves • 1 celery stick • 1 carrot • 1 tablespoon oil • 680g minced beef • 1 tin kidney beans • 1 tin baked beans • 1½ tablespoons whole cumin seeds • 2 whole cloves • 1 star anise • 1½ teaspoons ground coriander • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • Salt and black pepper to taste • 125ml beef or vegetable stock • 1 teaspoon marmite • 2 teaspoons soy sauce • 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 2 × 500ml bottles passata

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Crispy Pork Carnitas
recipes.overview: Carnitas are a classic Mexican dish of slow-cooked, tender pork that's finished until crispy and golden. The meat is cooked low and slow in its own fat with aromatic spices, citrus, and a hint of cola for sweetness, creating incredibly flavourful, versatile shredded pork. This dish is endlessly adaptable, perfect for filling burritos, tacos, quesadillas, or topping salads.
recipes.ingredients: 1kg skinless, boneless pork shoulder • 1 small onion • 4 cloves garlic • 1 bay leaf • 2 teaspoons sea salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ½ tablespoon dried oregano • ½ tablespoon ground cumin • 1 lime • 1 large orange • ⅓ cup cola • Cooking oil spray or oil for the baking sheet
ingredientNames: cooking oil spray or oil for the baking sheet

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Esquites
recipes.overview: Corn kernels — fresh in season, frozen the rest of the year — char in a hot dry pan with a bit of oil until many kernels are blackened. Off the heat: mayo, sour cream, lime, garlic, chilli powder and cheese fold through. Coriander and more cheese top each bowl.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g corn kernels • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 30 g unsalted butter • 4 spring onions • 2 garlic cloves • 1 long green chilli or jalapeño • 100 g mayonnaise • 100 g soured cream • 100 g cotija cheese , or feta as substitute • Juice of 2 limes • 1 teaspoon chilli powder • ½ teaspoon smoked paprika • ½ teaspoon salt • Black pepper • A small bunch of coriander • Lime wedges

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Fiery Beef Burrito
recipes.overview: The combination of already flavourful spiced beef with the particularly fiery salsa makes this burrito a force to be reckoned with. This specific burrito variation combines crunchy tortilla chips, creamy nacho cheese sauce, and a bold chile de árbol red salsa to create a complex, heat-forward flavour profile that packs a punch.
recipes.ingredients: 1 extra-large flour tortilla • 1 portion cooked spiced minced beef • 1 portion spiced rice • 1 handful fried tortilla chips • 40ml nacho cheese sauce • 2 tablespoons chile de árbol red salsa • 1 tablespoon sour cream

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Fully Loaded Burrito
recipes.overview: The fully loaded burrito is where all of your hard work and efforts come together to repay you in full. Packed with your choice of seasoned meat or vegetables, flavourful rice, smooth and filling beans, cheese, salsa and more, it's an entire meal wrapped up to go. This customizable template allows you to build your perfect burrito based on available ingredients and personal preference.
recipes.ingredients: 1 extra-large flour tortilla • 1–2 tablespoons refried pinto beans • 1 portion cooked pork, beef, chicken, or additional beans as desired • 1 portion spiced rice • 1 portion fresh tomato salsa • 1 handful grated cheese • 1 tablespoon sour cream • Optional: guacamole, jalapeños, lettuce, diced tomatoes

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Huevos Rancheros
recipes.overview: Huevos rancheros is a classic Mexican breakfast dish featuring eggs poached in a rich, spiced tomato sauce with peppers and chillies. This rustic, flavourful dish is traditionally served with warm tortillas for scooping, allowing diners to customize their own wraps. The combination of runny egg yolks with the thick, delicious tomato stew creates a truly memorable dish.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large onion • 2 garlic cloves • 2 red peppers • 2 fresh red or orange chillies • 1 large dried chilli • 3 fresh bay leaves • 2 large ripe tomatoes • 2 × 400g tins quality plum tomatoes • Several good lugs of olive oil • Sea salt and black pepper to taste • 6 large eggs • 6 flour tortillas • Cheddar cheese
ingredientNames: several good lugs of olive oil

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Mole Poblano
recipes.overview: Multiple dried chillies (mulato, ancho, pasilla) toast and rehydrate, then blend with toasted nuts, seeds, dried fruit, spices, fried onion and garlic, plantain, dark chocolate and a slice of toasted bread or tortilla into a thick paste. The paste cooks down with chicken stock into a glossy mahogany sauce, served over poached chicken with rice and warm tortillas.
recipes.ingredients: 4 dried mulato chillies • 4 dried ancho chillies • 4 dried pasilla chillies • 2 dried chipotle chillies • 100 ml vegetable oil • 1 onion • 6 garlic cloves • 1 ripe plantain • 50 g blanched almonds • 50 g raw peanuts • 30 g pumpkin seeds • 30 g sesame seeds • 100 g raisins • 1 corn tortilla • 1 thick slice white bread • 1 ripe tomato • 2 tomatillos • 1 stick cinnamon • 4 cloves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • ¼ teaspoon ground anise • 60 g good-quality dark chocolate • 1 litre chicken stock • Salt • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 1 whole chicken or 8 thighs/legs • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 bay leaf • Salt • Cooked rice • Warm tortillas • A scatter of sesame seeds

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Patatas Bravas
recipes.overview: Patatas bravas is a classic Spanish tapas dish featuring crispy roasted potatoes smothered in a rich, spiced tomato sauce. While originating in Spain, this dish has become popular across Latin American cuisines. The combination of golden, crispy potatoes with a flavourful paprika and chilli tomato sauce makes for an addictive side dish or appetizer. Traditionally served at room temperature with fresh parsley garnish.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp olive oil • 1 small onion • 2 garlic cloves • 227g tin chopped tomatoes • 1 tbsp tomato purée • 2 tsp sweet paprika • Good pinch chilli powder • Pinch sugar • Sea salt to taste • 900g potatoes • 2 tbsp olive oil • Sea salt and black pepper to taste • Fresh parsley

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Pork Tamales
recipes.overview: Masa harina mixes with lard or butter, baking powder and stock into a fluffy dough. A red-chilli pork shoulder filling has been simmered separately. Soaked corn husks get a layer of dough, a spoon of filling, fold and tie. Steamed standing upright for an hour.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg pork shoulder • 4 dried guajillo chillies • 2 dried ancho chillies • 4 garlic cloves • 1 onion • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon salt • 600 ml water • 500 g masa harina • 200 g lard or unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon baking powder • 1 teaspoon salt • 750 ml warm chicken stock or pork cooking liquid • 30 dried corn husks
ingredientNames: warm chicken stock or pork cooking liquid

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Spiced Rice
recipes.overview: The combination of spices and tomato gives this rice its distinctive yellow-orange hue. This Mexican-style spiced rice is a versatile side dish that pairs perfectly with enchiladas, burritos, carnitas, and other Mexican mains. The aromatic cumin, paprika, and fresh jalapeños create an authentic flavour profile that complements any Mexican meal.
recipes.ingredients: 1 teaspoon cumin powder • ¼ teaspoon smoked paprika • Pinch of cayenne pepper • Pinch of Mexican oregano • ¼ teaspoon sea salt • Pinch of black pepper • 150g long-grain white rice • 2 teaspoons olive oil • 1 small onion • 1 garlic clove • 1 tablespoon pickled jalapeños , or fresh jalapeños if preferred • 100ml tomato passata • 100ml light chicken stock or vegetable stock • Additional water or stock as needed
ingredientNames: 100ml light chicken stock or vegetable stock

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Tacos al Pastor
recipes.overview: Pork shoulder is sliced thin, marinated in a paste of rehydrated guajillo and ancho chillies, achiote, vinegar, garlic, pineapple juice and spices, then seared hard in a screaming-hot pan. Served on warm corn tortillas with chopped onion, coriander, lime, and small chunks of charred pineapple.
recipes.ingredients: 4 dried guajillo chillies • 2 dried ancho chillies • 4 garlic cloves • 100 ml white wine vinegar • 100 ml fresh pineapple juice • 2 tablespoons achiote paste • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 2 teaspoons salt • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • 1 kg pork shoulder • 24 small corn tortillas • ½ small fresh pineapple • 1 small white onion • A bunch of fresh coriander • 4 limes

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

subcategories.overview: A cuisine built on corn, beans and chillies (fresh, fermented and smoke-dried). Ancho, chipotle, lime, coriander and cumin work alongside tomatillo, avocado and crema. Comal-toasting of spices, slow-stewed adobos, charred salsas and griddled tortillas shape the table.

subcategories.label: Middle East

recipes.title: Chicken Shawarma
recipes.overview: Boneless chicken thighs swim in a yogurt-spice marinade for at least 4 hours. They roast hard at high heat for char, then rest and slice thin. The standard accompaniments are toum (garlic sauce), pickled turnips, parsley and warm pita.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg boneless chicken thighs • 200 g Greek yogurt • Juice of 1 lemon • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 6 garlic cloves • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 2 teaspoons ground coriander • 2 teaspoons sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground allspice • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • A pinch of cayenne • Warm pita or flatbread • Toum or tahini sauce • Pickled turnips or red onion • A small bunch of parsley • Lemon wedges

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Fattoush
recipes.overview: Pita breads tear into pieces and toast (or fry) until crisp and crunchy. Chopped vegetables, mint, parsley and a sumac-lemon-olive-oil dressing toss together; the toasted pita goes in last so it stays crunchy. Eaten immediately before the bread softens.
recipes.ingredients: 2 large pita breads • 3 tablespoons olive oil • 4 ripe tomatoes • 1 cucumber • 6 radishes • 4 spring onions • 1 small romaine lettuce • A small bunch of fresh mint • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • Juice of 1 lemon • 1 tablespoon pomegranate molasses • 1 garlic clove • 2 teaspoons sumac • 1 teaspoon dried mint • ½ teaspoon salt • ¼ teaspoon black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Kibbeh
recipes.overview: Two preparations: the bulgur-and-mince shell paste and the spiced cooked filling. The shell is shaped around a portion of filling into a football shape, then deep-fried until the outside is golden and the inside is hot and savoury. Served warm with yogurt or tahini.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g fine bulgur wheat • 500 g lean lamb mince • 1 small onion • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground allspice • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 small onion • 250 g lamb mince • 50 g pine nuts • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground allspice • 1 teaspoon sumac • Salt and pepper • Vegetable oil for deep-frying • Plain yogurt or tahini sauce • Lemon wedges • Parsley

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Mujadara
recipes.overview: Brown lentils boil until almost tender; long-grain rice and water join them along with cumin and a touch of cinnamon. While they finish cooking, sliced onions fry slow in olive oil until they're deep brown and crisp. The crisp onions and their dark oil go on top.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g brown or green lentils • 200 g long-grain rice • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • 1 teaspoon salt • ¼ teaspoon black pepper • 3 large onions • 6 tablespoons olive oil • ½ teaspoon salt • Greek yogurt • Lemon wedges • A small bunch of parsley

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.overview: A pepper-onion sofrito gets cumin, paprika and chilli, then tomatoes simmer down into a thick sauce. Eggs crack into wells in the sauce; the pan covers and the eggs steam-poach until the whites are set but the yolks remain runny.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 1 red pepper • 4 garlic cloves • 1 small red chilli • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika • 1 teaspoon caraway seeds • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 800 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 6 large eggs • 100 g feta • A small handful of flat-leaf parsley or coriander • Crusty bread, to serve
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
small handful of flat-leaf parsley or coriander

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Levantine cooking from Lebanon, Palestine, Syria and Jordan: olive oil, lemon, garlic, parsley and the warming spices of cumin, coriander, allspice and sumac. Tahini, yogurt and pomegranate molasses cross from breakfast through dinner; chickpeas, lamb and wheat take centre stage. Mezze (the small-plates table), open-flame grilling, slow-cooked stuffed vegetables and hand-shaped breads anchor the table.

subcategories.label: Moroccan

recipes.title: B'stilla
recipes.overview: Chicken poaches in a heavily spiced broth (saffron, ginger, cinnamon, lemon). The shredded meat returns to a reduced sauce thickened with beaten eggs to make a soft set. Toasted almonds with sugar and cinnamon form a layer. Everything wraps in butter-brushed filo, baked golden, and dusted with icing sugar and cinnamon.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg chicken thighs • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1 cinnamon stick • 1 teaspoon ground ginger • 1 teaspoon turmeric • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 100 g unsalted butter • 600 ml chicken stock • A small bunch of fresh coriander • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Juice of 1 lemon • 4 large eggs • Salt • 200 g blanched almonds • 4 tablespoons icing sugar • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 1 tablespoon orange-blossom water • 8-10 sheets filo pastry • 100 g unsalted butter • Icing sugar • Ground cinnamon

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Bissara
recipes.overview: Skinned dried fava beans (or split peas as a substitute) simmer with garlic, cumin and a small amount of paprika until they completely break down. The soup is blended smooth, thinned to taste, and served with each bowl getting a generous topping of olive oil, ground cumin, paprika and chilli flakes. Bread is essential.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g dried split fava beans or yellow split peas • 1.5 litres water • 6 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 tablespoons olive oil • Juice of 1 lemon • Extra-virgin olive oil • A pinch of ground cumin • A pinch of paprika • Chilli flakes • Crusty bread
ingredientNames: split fava beans or yellow split peas

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Chicken Tagine with Preserved Lemon and Olives
recipes.overview: Bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks rub with a saffron-cumin-ginger-paprika paste, then braise gently with onion, garlic and stock in a tagine or heavy casserole. Preserved lemon and green olives go in toward the end so they don't disintegrate. Coriander finishes.
recipes.ingredients: 8 bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks • 1 large onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1 teaspoon ground ginger • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 500 ml chicken stock • 1 large preserved lemon • 150 g pitted green olives • A small bunch of coriander • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Couscous, to serve
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Couscous Royale
recipes.overview: Lamb and chicken poach slowly in a saffron-tomato broth with chunks of root vegetables and chickpeas. Merguez sausages grill or fry separately. Couscous steams (or is rehydrated quickly with hot broth and butter). Everything plates separately on a vast platter; diners build their own bowls and slick with harissa.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g lamb shoulder • 600 g chicken thighs and drumsticks • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ras el hanout • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 800 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 1.5 litres chicken stock • 4 carrots • 2 turnips • 2 courgettes • 200 g tinned chickpeas • A small bunch of coriander • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 8 merguez sausages • 500 g couscous • 50 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon salt • 600 ml just-boiled water • 4 tablespoons harissa • Lemon wedges
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Harira
recipes.overview: Lamb shoulder cubes simmer slow with chickpeas, lentils, tomatoes, vermicelli noodles, and a heavy spice mix (ginger, cinnamon, turmeric, cumin) in a generous broth. The soup is finished by adding the noodles in the last 10 minutes and a final scatter of fresh coriander, parsley and lemon juice.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g lamb shoulder • 3 tablespoons olive oil • 2 onions • 4 celery sticks • 4 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon grated ginger • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon black pepper • A pinch of saffron threads • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 800 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 200 g tinned or dried chickpeas • 100 g brown lentils • 1.5 litres chicken or lamb stock • 100 g vermicelli noodles • A small bunch of fresh coriander • A small bunch of fresh parsley • Juice of 1 lemon • Salt • Lemon wedges, to serve

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Lamb Tagine with Apricots and Almonds
recipes.overview: Lamb shoulder cubes brown deeply, then simmer slow with onion, garlic and a Moroccan spice blend (ras el hanout, ginger, cinnamon). Stock and a splash of honey go in; dried apricots join in the second half so they soften without dissolving. Toasted almonds and coriander on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg lamb shoulder • 3 tablespoons olive oil • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 1 thumb fresh ginger • 2 teaspoons ras el hanout • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • A pinch of saffron threads • 700 ml chicken or lamb stock • 2 tablespoons honey • 200 g dried apricots • 50 g toasted flaked almonds • A small bunch of coriander • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • Couscous or flatbread, to serve
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Vegetable Tagine
recipes.overview: Onions soften with garlic and ras el hanout. Carrots, parsnips, potato and squash join in stages — root first, fast-cooking last. Chickpeas, tomatoes, stock and apricots simmer everything together. At the end, preserved lemon, fresh coriander and toasted almonds finish; honey rounds out the spice if needed.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 2 tablespoons ras el hanout • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • 2 medium carrots • 2 parsnips • 300 g butternut squash • 2 medium potatoes • 1 x 400 g tin chickpeas • 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes • 100 g dried apricots • 600 ml vegetable stock • 1 small preserved lemon • 1 tablespoon honey • Salt and black pepper • 50 g flaked almonds • A small bunch of coriander • A small bunch of mint

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Zaalouk
recipes.overview: Aubergines are charred whole over a flame or roasted until collapsed; their flesh is scooped out and chopped. Tomatoes, garlic and spices stew in olive oil to a thick base; the aubergine joins; everything cooks down to a coarse, oily, deep-flavoured purée. Lemon and coriander finish.
recipes.serves: 4 as a side, 2 as a main
recipes.ingredients: 2 large aubergines • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 6 garlic cloves • 4 ripe tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes • 1 tablespoon sweet paprika • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • ½ teaspoon hot paprika or pinch of cayenne • 1 teaspoon salt • Juice of half a lemon • A small bunch of coriander • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Black pepper • Extra olive oil and bread
ingredientNames: ripe tomatoes or 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes
hot paprika or pinch of cayenne

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Spiced, slow-cooked plates from across the Maghreb. Ras el hanout, saffron, preserved lemon, olives and the fragrance of cumin, cinnamon and ginger drive the flavour. Tagines (slow-cooked clay-pot stews), couscous (steamed two or three times for the right texture), b'stilla pies of layered phyllo and the complex spice work of harira soups define the tradition.

subcategories.label: Native North American

recipes.title: Cedar-Planked Salmon
recipes.overview: A food-safe cedar plank submerges in water for an hour. The salmon (skin on, one side) gets a brief dry cure of brown sugar, salt and crushed juniper. Maple syrup and lemon zest go on at the end of the cure. The hot grill cooks the plank from below while the salmon cooks from the slow heat radiating up — a steam-smoke method, neither pure grilling nor pure baking. The result is a salmon that tastes faintly of forest, cured-cured-not-curried.*
recipes.prepTime: 15 minutes (plus 1 hour plank soak + 30 min cure)
recipes.ingredients: 1 large salmon fillet , skin on, pin bones removed • 1 untreated cedar plank • 3 tablespoons light brown sugar • 1 tablespoon flaky sea salt • 1 tablespoon crushed juniper berries • 1 teaspoon black pepper • 4 tablespoons pure maple syrup • Zest of 1 lemon • 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard • A small bunch of dill • Lemon wedges • Flaky salt

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Frybread with Wojapi
recipes.overview: A simple yeasted dough — flour, baking powder, milk, salt, a touch of sugar — rests until soft and pliable. Each portion stretches by hand into a 15 cm disc, then drops into hot oil for 90 seconds per side. Wojapi simmers berries, water and maple syrup to a thick sauce; thickens slightly with a small amount of cornflour. The hot frybread piles on a plate; warm wojapi spoons over.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g plain flour • 1 tablespoon baking powder • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 teaspoons caster sugar • 350 ml warm milk • Vegetable oil for shallow frying • 600 g mixed berries • 200 ml water • 4 tablespoons pure maple syrup • 2 tablespoons brown sugar • 1 tablespoon cornflour • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract • A small pinch of salt • Icing sugar for dusting • Whipped cream or vanilla ice cream
ingredientNames: whipped cream or vanilla ice cream

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Three Sisters Succotash
recipes.overview: A small dice of butternut squash softens in butter with onion and garlic; corn kernels and pre-cooked beans (kidney, pinto or borlotti) join in. Stock loosens; sage and bay perfume; the lot simmers until the squash is tender and the broth has reduced to a glaze. Maple syrup and cider vinegar balance at the end.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tablespoons unsalted butter • 1 medium onion • 3 garlic cloves • 600 g butternut or kabocha squash • 400 g corn kernels • 1 x 400 g tin red kidney or pinto beans — or 250 g cooked from dried • 350 ml vegetable stock or water • 1 bay leaf • 4 sage leaves or 1 teaspoon dried • 1 teaspoon dried thyme • 2 tablespoons maple syrup • 1 tablespoon cider vinegar • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • A small bunch flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: x 400 g tin red kidney or pinto beans — or 250 g cooked from dried

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: The cooking traditions of the indigenous peoples of North America, drawing from regional larders: Pacific Northwest cedar-planked salmon, Plains bison and wild rice, Eastern woodlands maple and corn, Southwestern beans and chillies. The Three Sisters (corn, beans, squash) are foundational; foraged greens, berries and nuts complete the table. Slow-pit cooking, smoking over hardwoods, and stews simmered in clay or cast iron define the techniques. Frybread, while a 19th-century product of forced relocation, is now an iconic across-tribes shared dish.

subcategories.label: North African

recipes.title: Brik à l'Œuf
recipes.overview: Filling builds in a small bowl: drained tinned tuna, finely-chopped onion, parsley, capers, harissa, salt. A square of malsouka (or two stacked sheets of filo) lays flat. Half the filling spreads on one half of the sheet; an egg cracks into a small well on top; the other half folds over. The seam pinches; the brik slides into hot oil for 90 seconds — out crisp and golden, yolk still soft.
recipes.ingredients: 1 x 160 g tin tuna • 1 medium onion • A small bunch flat-leaf parsley • 2 tablespoons capers • 2 tablespoons grated parmesan or aged gouda • 1 teaspoon harissa • ½ teaspoon ground cumin • Salt and black pepper • 4 sheets malsouka pastry • 4 large eggs • Vegetable oil for shallow frying • 2 lemons • Harissa • A simple salad

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Makroud
recipes.overview: Semolina mixes with melted butter and rests so it absorbs fully — the result is a fragrant, buttery, slightly grainy dough. Dates soften with butter, cinnamon and rosewater into a smooth filling. The dough rolls into a long log; the date paste extrudes into the centre; the cylinder rolls together and slices into diamonds. Fried briefly and dipped hot into orange-blossom syrup.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g coarse semolina • 250 g unsalted butter • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 2 tablespoons orange blossom water • 100 ml warm water • 400 g pitted dates • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground cloves • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 300 g caster sugar • 200 ml water • 4 tablespoons honey • 2 tablespoons orange blossom water • Juice of half a lemon • Vegetable oil for shallow-frying • Sesame seeds

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Slata Mechouia
recipes.overview: Peppers, tomatoes and chillies char whole on a gas flame (or under a fierce grill) until blackened and collapsed; the skins peel away and the flesh chops to a rough paste. Garlic chars too, mellowing as it does. Lemon, olive oil, capers and salt fold through; tuna and hard-boiled eggs sometimes top.
recipes.ingredients: 4 large red peppers • 2 large green peppers • 4 ripe tomatoes • 4 long red chillies • 6 garlic cloves • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • Juice of 1 lemon • 1 teaspoon ground caraway • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 tablespoons capers • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 1 x 160 g tin tuna • 2 hard-boiled eggs • 8 black olives • Crusty bread or flatbread

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

subcategories.overview: Cooking that runs along the Mediterranean's southern coast — Algeria, Tunisia, Libya — sharing terrain and influences with Morocco and Egypt but with their own dishes. Olive oil, harissa, preserved lemon, ras el hanout, caraway and merguez sausage define the seasoning; couscous is the daily grain; brik, slata mechouia, makroud and a rich tradition of stuffed pastries and sweets fill out the table. The hot Berber-Arab-French history shows in every plate.

subcategories.label: Palestinian

recipes.title: Knafeh Nabulsiya
recipes.overview: Kataifi pastry shreds blitz to a coarse rubble; mixes with melted ghee and a pinch of orange food colouring; spreads across the bottom of a buttered tin. Akkawi cheese (rinsed of salt) lays on top. Bakes hot until the pastry browns. Cold rosewater syrup pours over hot from the oven; the syrup soaks the pastry while the cheese stays soft. Crushed pistachios scatter on top.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g kataifi pastry • 250 g unsalted butter , melted • ¼ teaspoon orange food colouring paste • 800 g akkawi cheese or low-salt halloumi or a mix of mozzarella and ricotta • 400 g caster sugar • 250 ml water • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 1 tablespoon rosewater • 1 tablespoon orange blossom water • 80 g shelled pistachios
ingredientNames: akkawi cheese or low-salt halloumi or a mix of mozzarella and ricotta

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Maklouba
recipes.overview: Aubergines fry first to soften and develop colour. Cauliflower fries to gold. Chicken cooks in a spiced stock. The pot layers from bottom up: tomato slices, aubergine, cauliflower, chicken, parboiled rice. Stock pours over; everything steams together. Inverted onto a platter, the layers stack into a multicoloured cake.
recipes.ingredients: 8 bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks • 1 onion • 4 cardamom pods • 1 cinnamon stick • 4 cloves • 2 bay leaves • 1 teaspoon black peppercorns • 1.2 litres water • 1½ teaspoons salt • 2 medium aubergines • 1 medium cauliflower • Vegetable oil for frying • 2 medium tomatoes • 500 g basmati rice • 2 tablespoons baharat • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • 50 g pine nuts • 50 g flaked almonds • A small bunch of parsley • Plain yogurt and a tomato-cucumber salad
ingredientNames: plain yogurt and a tomato-cucumber salad

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Musakhan
recipes.overview: A whole chicken or chicken pieces marinate in olive oil, sumac, allspice and lemon. Onions cook slowly in olive oil until completely soft and sweet, then turn deep red-purple from a generous shake of sumac. The chicken roasts on top of the onions; the bread layers under at the end to catch the juices. Pine nuts toast separately.
recipes.ingredients: 1 whole chicken or 8 bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks • 4 tablespoons olive oil • 2 tablespoons sumac • 1 tablespoon ground allspice • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon • 1 teaspoon ground cardamom • 1½ teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • Juice of 1 lemon • 6 large onions • 150 ml olive oil • 4 tablespoons sumac • 1 teaspoon salt • 2 large taboon breads or 4 flatbreads • 100 g pine nuts • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley
ingredientNames: chicken or 8 bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: A cuisine that holds the Levantine table at its centre: olive oil, za'atar, sumac, tahini and lemon shape the seasoning. Musakhan (sumac chicken on flatbread) is the national dish; maqluba, mansaf, knafeh Nabulsiya and freshly-baked taboon bread are the table's other anchors. Slow-cooked stews, hand-rolled grape leaves, shared mezze and the closeness of olive groves to home cooking define the kitchen.

subcategories.label: Persian

recipes.title: Bastani Sonnati
recipes.overview: Milk and cream warm with sugar; saffron blooms in milk separately; egg yolks whisk with sugar to ribbons. The hot milk tempers into the yolks; the custard cooks back to coat-the-spoon thickness; off the heat, rosewater, saffron-milk and a pinch of mastic powder fold in. Cooled custard goes into the ice cream churn. As it freezes, ribbons of separately-frozen cream and chopped pistachios fold through.
recipes.ingredients: 600 ml whole milk • 400 ml double cream • 200 g caster sugar • 6 large egg yolks • A generous pinch of saffron threads • 2 tablespoons rosewater • ½ teaspoon ground mastic gum • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract • A pinch of salt • 200 ml double cream • 80 g shelled pistachios

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Chelo Kabab Koobideh
recipes.overview: Mince mixes with very finely-grated onion (squeezed dry), salt, pepper, turmeric and a hit of saffron-water. The mixture chills, then forms onto wide flat skewers in long sausage shapes. Charcoal grills are traditional; a hot grill pan or barbecue works at home. The kababs grill 3-4 minutes per side; whole tomatoes char alongside; rice piles on the plate; everything assembles together.
recipes.ingredients: 700 g minced lamb • 1 large onion • 1½ teaspoons salt • 1 teaspoon black pepper • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon ground sumac • A pinch of saffron • ½ teaspoon bicarbonate of soda • 4 ripe tomatoes • 2 long red chillies • 50 g unsalted butter • Chelo • Fresh basil • Mint • Spring onions • Sumac • 4 lavash flatbreads • Yogurt and cucumber dip

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Salad Shirazi
recipes.overview: Tomato and cucumber are deseeded and diced into 5 mm cubes. Onion (preferably purple/red) dices to match. Lime juice, olive oil, dried mint, salt and pepper dress; the salad rests 10 minutes for the flavours to mingle. Doesn't keep — the cucumbers weep within an hour.
recipes.ingredients: 4 medium tomatoes • 2 medium cucumbers • 1 small red onion • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • Juice of 2 limes • 1 tablespoon dried mint • A small bunch fresh mint • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ¼ teaspoon ground sumac

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: The classical, formal Persian cooking tradition: layered saffron rice (chelo) topped with elaborate khoresh stews, charcoal-grilled kababs (koobideh, joojeh, barg) and meticulous patisserie scented with rosewater, orange blossom and saffron. More restrained and herb-driven than its Indian and Turkish neighbours; the seasoning is precise, and the rice work is its own art form. Bastani, faloodeh and shirini close meals; Nowruz (Persian New Year) brings the cuisine to a peak.

subcategories.label: Polish

recipes.title: Barszcz Czerwony
recipes.overview: The brightest barszcz uses fermented beetroot kvas — beetroot, garlic and rye bread crust steeped in salted water for 24-48 hours — for the sour edge. Faster: simmer roasted beetroot, root vegetables and dried mushroom in vegetable stock with bay, peppercorns and allspice. Strain to clarity; finish with vinegar and a small amount of sugar to balance. Serve hot from a small bowl or mug.
recipes.ingredients: 4 medium beetroots • 1 large onion • 2 carrots • 2 celery sticks • 4 garlic cloves • 20 g dried wild mushrooms or porcini • 2 bay leaves • 6 allspice berries • 6 black peppercorns • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • 2 tablespoons cider vinegar • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • 1 teaspoon salt • A small bunch of fresh dill

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Bigos
recipes.overview: Pork shoulder, beef, smoked sausage and bacon all brown separately, then meet sauerkraut, fresh cabbage, dried porcini, prunes, tomato and red wine in a heavy pot. Simmers for 3 hours minimum; reheats over 2-3 days, getting better each round.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g pork shoulder • 300 g beef chuck • 200 g smoked Polish sausage • 200 g smoked bacon lardons • 2 onions • 4 garlic cloves • 800 g sauerkraut • 500 g white cabbage • 30 g dried porcini mushrooms • 200 g prunes • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 250 ml red wine • 500 ml beef stock • 2 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon caraway seeds • 1 teaspoon allspice berries • 6 juniper berries • 1 teaspoon sweet paprika • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pierogi Ruskie
recipes.overview: A simple flour-egg-warm-water dough rests then rolls thin. Discs cut, filling spooned, sealed by hand. Pierogi boil in salted water until they float, then optionally fry in butter to crisp the edges. Caramelised onions go on top with a generous spoon of sour cream.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g plain flour • 1 large egg • 1 teaspoon salt • 250 ml warm water • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 600 g floury potatoes • 250 g twaróg cheese • 1 medium onion • 30 g unsalted butter • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 onions • 30 g unsalted butter • 200 g soured cream • A small bunch of chives

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Pierogi z Kapustą i Grzybami
recipes.overview: Dried wild mushrooms (porcini if you can get them) rehydrate; sauerkraut squeezes dry. Both cook down with onion in butter until reduced and intense. The dough is plain — flour, egg, water, salt, oil — kneaded smooth and rested. Each circle gets a teaspoon of filling, folds, seals. Boiled until they float, then often pan-finished in butter with onions and a spoonful of soured cream on the side.
recipes.ingredients: 30 g dried porcini or wild mushrooms • 500 g sauerkraut • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 medium onions • 1 bay leaf • 5 black peppercorns • 200 g chestnut mushrooms • 1 teaspoon dried marjoram • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 500 g plain flour • 1 large egg • 1 teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon sunflower oil • 250 ml warm water • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 onions • Soured cream • Fresh dill

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Hearty Eastern European cooking built on pork, cabbage, beetroot, mushrooms and grains. Caraway, dill, marjoram and bay handle the herb side; fermented and pickled vegetables (sauerkraut, ogórki) supply the brightness. Defining dishes include pierogi (filled dumplings), bigos (the long-simmered hunter's stew), żurek (fermented rye soup) and the daily presence of dark, dense rye bread.

subcategories.label: Russian

recipes.title: Beef Stroganoff
recipes.overview: Strips of fillet or sirloin sear hard in a hot pan and lift out so they don't overcook. Onion and mushrooms cook in the same pan with butter; brandy or wine deglazes; mustard, stock and soured cream make the sauce. The beef returns at the end to warm through, off the heat to keep it pink.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g beef fillet or sirloin • 2 tablespoons plain flour • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 30 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 onion • 300 g chestnut mushrooms • 50 ml brandy or dry white wine • 250 ml beef stock • 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard • 200 ml soured cream • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • Lemon juice, to finish • 400 g egg noodles or 250 g long-grain rice
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper
egg noodles or 250 g long-grain rice

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Borscht
recipes.overview: Beef shin or short rib simmers slow with aromatics to build a rich broth. Grated beetroot, cabbage, carrot, onion and potato cook together with the beef and tomatoes for the second hour. A splash of vinegar at the end keeps the colour bright; soured cream and dill on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg beef shin or short rib • 2 onions • 1 carrot • 1 celery stick • 2 bay leaves • 1 tablespoon black peppercorns • 2 litres water • 4 medium beetroots • 4 garlic cloves • ¼ small white cabbage • 3 medium potatoes • 400 g tinned chopped tomatoes • 2 tablespoons tomato purée • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar • 1 teaspoon caster sugar • Salt and freshly ground black pepper • 200 g soured cream • A small bunch of dill • Rye bread
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Vareniki
recipes.overview: A simple egg-and-water dough rolls thin and cuts into circles. The filling is mashed potato folded through with deeply browned onions. Each round folds over a spoonful of filling, sealed and crimped. Boiled in batches; served drowned in butter, with extra fried onions on top and sour cream alongside.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g plain flour • 1 large egg • 1 teaspoon salt • 250 ml warm water • 1 tablespoon sunflower oil • 700 g floury potatoes • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 large onions • 2 tablespoons sunflower oil • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • 50 g unsalted butter • 1 large onion • 200 g soured cream • A small bunch of dill

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Vegetarian Borscht
recipes.overview: Onion, carrot and beetroot sauté together; the colour bleeds out; cabbage and potatoes join in stock. Tomato paste deepens; bay and dill infuse; vinegar and a touch of sugar balance at the end. Sour cream and dill go on at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 4 tablespoons sunflower oil • 1 large onion • 2 medium carrots • 3 medium beetroots • 4 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 1.5 litres vegetable stock • 3 medium potatoes • ½ small white cabbage • 2 bay leaves • 5 black peppercorns • 2 tablespoons cider vinegar • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1½ teaspoons salt • Black pepper • 4 tablespoons soured cream • A small bunch of dill • Crusty rye bread

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Vinaigrette Salad
recipes.overview: Beetroot, potatoes and carrots boil in their skins. Once cool, they're peeled and diced into 1 cm cubes. Sauerkraut is squeezed dry; pickled cucumbers are diced; spring onions and dill chopped. Everything tosses with sunflower oil, vinegar, salt and pepper. Best after a few hours' rest in the fridge.
recipes.ingredients: 3 medium beetroots • 3 medium potatoes • 2 medium carrots • 200 g sauerkraut • 3 dill pickles • 1 x 400 g tin red kidney beans or 200 g cooked chickpeas • 5 spring onions • 4 tablespoons sunflower oil • 2 tablespoons cider vinegar • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • A small bunch of dill
ingredientNames: x 400 g tin red kidney beans or 200 g cooked chickpeas

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Cold-climate cooking that prizes preservation, grains, root vegetables and dairy. Sour cream and dill finish nearly every bowl; pickles, salted fish and fermented kvass sit at the table. Long-braised stews and beetroot-based soups (borscht), buckwheat kasha, blini and yeasted breads, plus the table-bound zakuski tradition of small starters, anchor the cuisine.

subcategories.label: Somalian

recipes.title: Anjero
recipes.overview: A simple batter of plain flour, semolina, yeast and warm water is left to ferment overnight; this gives anjero its characteristic sourness and the bubbles that make the texture. The next day, batter ladles onto a hot pan and cooks just on one side — the bottom turns golden, the top sets with a lattice of bubble-craters. Stack between cloths to steam soft.
recipes.ingredients: 350 g plain flour • 100 g semolina • 1 teaspoon active dry yeast • 1 teaspoon sugar • 1 teaspoon salt • 700 ml warm water • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Halwo
recipes.overview: Cornflour is whisked into water; sugar dissolves with saffron; the lot bubbles together with ghee being drizzled in slowly. The mixture goes from opaque to translucent amber over 25 minutes; the texture goes from runny to gooey-thick like soft caramel. Cardamom and peanuts fold in at the end; everything pours into a tray to set.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g cornflour • 700 ml water • 600 g caster sugar • A generous pinch of saffron threads • 200 g ghee • 1 tablespoon ground cardamom • 100 g unsalted peanuts , roughly chopped • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Sambusa
recipes.overview: Spring roll wrappers fold around a filling of cooked lentils, peas, onion, garlic, ginger and a Somali spice mix (xawaash) of cumin, coriander, cardamom and cinnamon. Each is folded into a tight triangle, sealed with a flour-water paste, and deep-fried until golden.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g brown lentils • 600 ml water • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large onion • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 1 long green chilli • 200 g frozen peas • 2 teaspoons ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • 1 teaspoon salt • A small bunch of coriander • 16 spring roll wrappers • 4 tablespoons plain flour mixed with 4 tablespoons water • Vegetable oil for deep-frying
ingredientNames: plain flour mixed with 4 tablespoons water

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Suqaar
recipes.overview: Beef tenderloin or sirloin is cubed small. A spice mix of cumin, coriander, cardamom and cinnamon — Somalia's xawaash — seasons it. The cubes sear quickly in a hot pan with onion and pepper; tomato paste deepens; chilli and butter finish. Coriander piles on at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g beef sirloin or tenderloin • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large onion • 1 red pepper • 1 green pepper • 5 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • 1 long green chilli • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 2 medium tomatoes • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • ½ teaspoon ground cardamom • ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon • ½ teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 teaspoon salt • 100 ml water • 30 g unsalted butter • A small bunch of fresh coriander • 2 lime wedges

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Horn of Africa cooking shaped by trade with the Arab world, India and Italian colonial history. Xawaash (a spice blend of cumin, coriander, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper and turmeric) seasons nearly every meal. Rice (bariis), spaghetti from the Italian era, pancakes (anjero) and chapati form the carbohydrate base; lamb, goat and beef the protein. Slow-spiced stews, sambusa (samosas), grilled meats and the strong sweet tea (shaah) define the table; halwo and xalwo for the sweet finish.

subcategories.label: South American

recipes.title: Brigadeiro
recipes.overview: Sweetened condensed milk, cocoa powder and butter cook together in a heavy pan over medium-low heat, stirring constantly. The mixture goes from runny to slightly thick to a thick paste that pulls cleanly from the bottom of the pan when the spoon is drawn through. Off the heat, cool fully, then roll into small balls and drop in chocolate sprinkles.
recipes.ingredients: 1 x 397 g tin sweetened condensed milk • 30 g unsalted butter • 4 tablespoons cocoa powder • ¼ teaspoon salt • 100 g chocolate sprinkles
ingredientNames: x 397 g tin sweetened condensed milk

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Lomo Saltado
recipes.overview: Beef strips marinate briefly in soy and aji amarillo paste. Fries cook separately — pre-fried, set aside. The wok hits high heat; beef sears in batches; red onion and tomato add briefly so they keep their bite; soy, vinegar, lime and stock pour in to sauce. The fries go in last, just before serving — a 30-second toss so they pick up flavour without going soggy.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g beef sirloin or rump • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • 1 tablespoon aji amarillo paste • 4 garlic cloves • 2 cm fresh ginger • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 800 g floury potatoes • 800 ml vegetable oil for frying • Salt • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 1 large red onion • 4 medium tomatoes • 2 long green chillies • 4 tablespoons soy sauce • 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar • 2 tablespoons beef stock or water • Juice of 1 lime • A small bunch of coriander • Black pepper • Cooked white rice

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pão de Queijo
recipes.overview: Sweet (sour, "polvilho doce" or sweet) tapioca starch combines with hot milk-and-oil mixture (scalds the starch), then eggs and grated cheese. The mixture is sticky and unmanageable — refrigerate 30 minutes to firm up, or use damp hands to roll. Small balls bake until puffed and golden. Best straight from the oven.
recipes.ingredients: 250 ml whole milk • 80 ml vegetable oil • ½ teaspoon salt • 250 g sour tapioca starch or sweet tapioca starch • 2 large eggs • 200 g parmesan
ingredientNames: sour tapioca starch or sweet tapioca starch

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: A continent's cuisines folded together: Argentine asado and chimichurri; Brazilian feijoada, pão de queijo and brigadeiro; Peruvian ceviche, lomo saltado and aji-driven heat; Colombian and Venezuelan arepas; Chilean shellfish; Bolivian salteñas. Native ingredients (corn, potato, cassava, quinoa, cacao, yerba mate, aji chillies) meet European technique and African (especially Brazilian) and Asian (Peruvian chifa) influences. Big shared meals, charcoal grilling and street food run through every country.

subcategories.label: Spanish

recipes.title: Chorizo Pork with Crispy New Potatoes
recipes.overview: A vibrant Spanish dish combining tender pork tenderloin and spiced chorizo sausage in a rich tomato sauce, served alongside crispy roasted new potatoes. The chorizo releases its flavourful oils into the sauce while the potatoes develop golden, crispy exteriors. Bright lemon juice balances the richness of the pork and chorizo, creating a dish that is both comforting and fresh.
recipes.ingredients: 500 grams baby new potatoes • 2 tablespoons olive oil • Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 400 grams pork tenderloin • 150 grams chorizo sausage • 1 red onion • 500 grams tinned chopped tomatoes • Large pinch chilli flakes • Pinch of sugar • 1 lemon • 4 lemon quarters • Fresh rocket leaves
ingredientNames: sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Croquetas de Jamón
recipes.overview: A thick béchamel — three times the flour-to-milk ratio of standard béchamel — is studded with chopped jamón serrano, chilled until firm enough to shape, rolled into ovals, breaded with flour-egg-panko, and deep-fried at 180°C until deep golden. Eat hot.
recipes.ingredients: 100 g unsalted butter • 1 small onion • 100 g jamón serrano , finely chopped • 100 g plain flour • 700 ml whole milk • A grating of nutmeg • Salt and white pepper • 100 g plain flour • 3 large eggs • 200 g panko breadcrumbs • Vegetable oil for deep-frying

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Fabada Asturiana
recipes.overview: Soaked fabes beans simmer with cooking chorizo, morcilla (Spanish blood sausage) and a piece of pork belly or shoulder, with a touch of saffron and smoked paprika. The whole thing braises slowly until the beans are soft and the broth is dark and unctuous.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g fabes , soaked overnight in cold water • 200 g pork belly in one piece, or pork shoulder • 2 cooking chorizo sausages • 2 morcilla sausages • 1 onion • 1 head garlic • 2 bay leaves • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1 teaspoon sweet smoked paprika • Salt • Crusty bread, to serve

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.
