recipes.title: Gazpacho
recipes.overview: Ripe tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper and stale bread soak in red wine vinegar with garlic, then blitz with olive oil into a smooth velvety soup. Strained for restaurant texture or left chunky for a more rustic version. Served very cold with a swirl of olive oil and finely diced vegetables on top.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg very ripe tomatoes • 1 cucumber • 1 green pepper • 1 small red onion • 2 garlic cloves • 100 g stale white bread • 3 tablespoons sherry vinegar • 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil • 1 teaspoon salt • A pinch of cayenne or smoked paprika • 200 ml ice-cold water • 2 tablespoons each: finely diced cucumber, pepper, red onion, tomato • Croutons • A drizzle of extra virgin olive oil
ingredientNames: drizzle of extra virgin olive oil

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Paella de Mariscos
recipes.overview: A sofrito of onion, garlic and tomato is built in the paella pan, then bomba rice goes in and absorbs warm fish stock with saffron. Squid and white fish go in early; prawns, mussels and clams are nestled in for the last 10 minutes so they steam open without overcooking.
recipes.ingredients: 4 tablespoons olive oil • 1 onion • 4 garlic cloves • 2 ripe tomatoes • 1 teaspoon sweet smoked paprika • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1.2 litres warm fish stock • 350 g bomba rice • 200 g squid • 300 g firm white fish , cubed • 12 large raw prawns • 200 g mussels • 200 g clams • 100 g peas • Salt • 2 tablespoons chopped flat-leaf parsley • Lemon wedges, to serve

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Paella Valenciana
recipes.overview: Chicken thighs and rabbit pieces brown in olive oil, joined by green beans, butter beans and a sofrito of tomato and paprika. Saffron-infused stock and short-grain bomba rice go in, simmered without stirring until the rice is tender and the bottom has crisped into socarrat.
recipes.ingredients: 4 tablespoons olive oil • 4 chicken thighs • 2 rabbit legs or 4 more chicken thighs • 100 g flat green beans • 100 g butter beans, drained from a tin • 2 ripe tomatoes • 1 teaspoon sweet smoked paprika • 1 large pinch saffron threads • 1.2 litres warm chicken stock • 350 g bomba rice • 2 sprigs fresh rosemary • Salt • Lemon wedges, to serve
ingredientNames: rabbit legs or 4 more chicken thighs

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Pisto Manchego
recipes.overview: Onion softens slowly in olive oil; peppers join to break down sweetly; aubergine and courgette go in next to caramelise at the edges; tomatoes finish to bind. Each addition has time to take on the oil and contribute. The result is glossy, jam-like, deep — eaten with bread, fried eggs or alongside grilled food.
recipes.ingredients: 100 ml olive oil • 2 large onions • 2 red peppers • 2 green peppers • 5 garlic cloves • 1 large aubergine • 2 medium courgettes • 6 large ripe tomatoes or 1 x 800 g tin chopped tomatoes • 1 teaspoon sweet smoked paprika • 1 teaspoon dried oregano • 1 teaspoon sugar • Salt and black pepper • 4 large eggs
ingredientNames: large ripe tomatoes or 1 x 800 g tin chopped tomatoes

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Spanish Omelette (Tortilla)
recipes.overview: This classic Spanish omelette is a rustic, hearty dish featuring tender potatoes, caramelized onions, peppers, tomatoes, and spiced chorizo bound together with fluffy beaten eggs. The omelette is cooked slowly and gently, allowing the ingredients to meld while maintaining a creamy centre with a lightly golden exterior. Tortilla española is equally delicious served warm, at room temperature, or cold, making it versatile for any meal of the day.
recipes.ingredients: 250 grams baby new potatoes • 1 medium onion • 1 green bell pepper • 2 medium tomatoes • 1 garlic clove • 1 bouquet garni • 1 chorizo sausage • 6 large eggs • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 1 tablespoon butter • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Tortilla Española
recipes.overview: Potatoes slice thin and cook slowly in shallow olive oil with onions until soft — not fried, almost steamed in the oil. They drain (oil saved); fold into beaten eggs with salt; pour into a hot pan with a little of the saved oil; cook until the bottom is set; flip onto a plate and slide back to cook the other side. Centre should still wobble slightly when nudged.
recipes.ingredients: 600 g floury potatoes • 1 large onion • 300 ml olive oil • 6 large eggs • 1½ teaspoons salt • Black pepper

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Mediterranean cooking grounded in olive oil, garlic, smoked paprika and saffron. Cured pork (jamón, chorizo), seafood and short-grain rice anchor the table, in paella, fideuà, tortilla and the tapas tradition. Slow simmers, plancha-griddling and one-pot rice dishes dominate, with the sofrito (tomato, onion, garlic, paprika) starting nearly everything.

subcategories.label: Thai
Curry

recipes.title: Beef Panang Curry
recipes.overview: A thick, sweet Panang curry with peanuts, served over jasmine rice. Similar to red curry but sweeter and thicker; add vegetables for extra nutrition or keep traditional.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp rapeseed oil • 600 g beef rib-eye, cut thinly against the grain • 1 batch Panang curry paste • 1–2 tbsp palm sugar • 600 ml thick coconut milk • About 225 g vegetables, such as chopped baby sweetcorn, courgette , mushrooms • 3 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves finely julienned • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Chicken Jungle Curry
recipes.overview: Spicy, thin jungle curry from Chiang Mai, traditionally made with jungle ingredients and game meat. No coconut milk; features a clear, flavorful broth with chicken and vegetables. Serve with sticky rice.
recipes.ingredients: 4 chicken breasts • 2 tbsp rapeseed , peanut or coconut oil • 6 tbsp Thai red curry paste • 500 ml Thai chicken stock or water • 10 green beans, cut into 2.5 cm pieces • 1 x 227 g tin bamboo shoots, drained and cut into matchsticks • 5 baby sweetcorn, cut into small pieces • 3 tbsp fresh green peppercorns • 2–3 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 6 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves thinly sliced • Juice of ½ lime • 1 tsp palm sugar • Coriander leaves • Thai sweet basil leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Thai Beef Massaman Curry
recipes.overview: Rich, slow-cooked beef curry with Persian influences, featuring cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves. Tender beef and potatoes in coconut milk sauce; no vegetables traditionally, but can add. Serve with rice or enjoy as is.
recipes.ingredients: 700 g stewing beef • 2 potatoes, peeled and cut into bite-size pieces • ½ red onion, quartered • 2 tbsp rapeseed oil • Handful of roasted peanuts • 1 batch massaman curry paste • 1 tbsp palm sugar • 400 ml thick coconut milk • 1 tsp tamarind paste • 3 tbsp Thai fish sauce • Salt, to taste • Thai holy basil

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Thai Green Chicken Curry
recipes.overview: Spicy green curry with fresh bird's eye chillies. Use homemade paste for best flavor; adjust spice level. Creamy coconut milk base with chicken and vegetables.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp coconut oil or rapeseed oil • 1 batch Thai green curry paste • 450 g skinless chicken thigh or breast fillets, cut into bite-size pieces • 250 ml Thai chicken stock • 400 ml thick coconut milk • About 225 g vegetables, such as baby corn, bamboo shoots, aubergine , broccoli, sliced lotus root • 2 tbsp sugar • 3 tbsp Thai fish sauce • Handful of Thai sweet basil, roughly chopped • 3 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves finely julienned • 2 red spur chillies, cut into thin rings, to garnish • Basil oil and some of the goop from the bottom, to garnish
ingredientNames: skinless chicken thigh or breast fillets
basil oil and some of the goop from the bottom

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Thai Green Curry (Vegetarian)
recipes.overview: A spice paste of green chillies and aromatics blends fresh (or starts from a good Thai paste with fresh additions). Coconut cream from the top of the can is cracked in the wok until oil splits out; paste fries; coconut milk loosens; vegetables — Thai aubergine, pea aubergine, bamboo shoots, broccoli, baby corn — go in by cook time. Tofu joins; the curry simmers briefly. Soy sauce (instead of fish sauce), palm sugar, lime leaves, Thai basil to finish.
recipes.ingredients: 5 long green chillies • 4 bird's-eye chillies • 2 stalks lemongrass • 3 cm galangal • 3 cm fresh ginger • 6 garlic cloves • 4 shallots • 2 kaffir lime leaves • 1 teaspoon ground white pepper • 1 teaspoon ground coriander • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • A small bunch of coriander • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 x 400 ml tin coconut milk • 1 x 165 ml tin coconut cream • 300 g firm tofu • 1 small Thai aubergine or 1 small regular aubergine • 100 g pea aubergines • 200 g broccoli • 100 g baby corn • 1 x 220 g tin bamboo shoots • 4 kaffir lime leaves • 3 tablespoons light soy sauce • 2 tablespoons palm sugar • 200 ml vegetable stock or water • A handful of Thai basil leaves • 2 long red chillies • Juice of half a lime • Cooked jasmine rice
ingredientNames: small thai aubergine or 1 small regular aubergine

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Thai Red Chicken Curry
recipes.overview: Classic Thai restaurant favorite with red curry paste. Color from chillies; use homemade paste for best flavor. Thin sauce with chicken and vegetables.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp coconut oil or rapeseed oil • 1 batch Thai red curry paste • 450 g skinless chicken thigh fillets, cut into bite-size pieces • 250 ml Thai chicken stock • 400 ml thick coconut milk • About 225 g vegetables, such as baby aubergine , sliced red pepper, green beans • 3 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 1 tbsp light soy sauce • 1 tsp tamarind paste • 1 tbsp palm sugar • Coriander leaves • 1 tsp roasted Thai chilli oil, to garnish

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Thai Red Curry
recipes.overview: Gaeng phed gai, this classic Thai red curry features tender chicken simmered in a rich, aromatic coconut sauce infused with homemade red curry paste. The chilli paste that forms the basis of this dish has superb flavour and is worth making in quantity, as it's useful in all sorts of spicy dishes. Taking the extra time to pound herbs and spices using a mortar and pestle releases their fragrances perfectly, creating an authentic, restaurant-quality curry.
recipes.ingredients: 15 red chillies • 4 shallots • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon galangal • 2 lemongrass stalks • 3 kaffir lime leaves • 4 coriander roots • 10 black peppercorns • 1 teaspoon coriander seeds • ½ teaspoon cumin seeds • Pinch of ground cinnamon • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon shrimp paste • 2 tablespoons oil • 1 litre coconut milk • 450 grams skinless chicken breast • 2 tablespoons Thai fish sauce • 1 tablespoon granulated sugar • 225 grams bamboo shoots • 5 kaffir lime leaves • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • Fresh red chillies • Thai basil leaves • Fresh mint leaves
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Yellow Chicken Curry
recipes.overview: Mild yellow curry with Indian influences from turmeric and curry powder. Similar to chicken korma but spicier. Use "cracking the coconut milk" method for natural oil, or add oil. Serve with rice.
recipes.ingredients: 10 small waxy new potatoes, quartered • 1 carrot, cut into thin rounds • 10 baby plum tomatoes, halved • 600 ml thick coconut milk • 2 tbsp rapeseed oil • 1 batch yellow curry paste • 1 tbsp palm sugar • 1 tbsp tamarind paste • 600 g skinless chicken thigh fillets, cut into bite-size pieces • 1 tsp curry powder • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 4 tbsp fried garlic • Chopped chillies • Chopped coriander leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: Red, green, yellow, panang and massaman curries.

subcategories.label: Pastes

recipes.title: Massaman Curry Paste
recipes.overview: Spice-heavy paste for massaman curries, with Muslim roots. More spices than other curries; requires pounding for smooth texture.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tbsp coriander seeds • 1½ tbsp cumin seeds • 5 whole cloves • 1 tbsp black peppercorns • 1 whole nutmeg • Seeds from 6 green cardamom pods • 5 cm piece cinnamon stick • 12 dried red bird’s eye chillies, soaked in water for 30 minutes and cut into small pieces • 8 garlic cloves, smashed • 2–3 small shallots, thinly sliced • 1 long lemongrass stalk , thinly sliced • 1 thumb-sized piece galangal, sliced into thin rounds • Zest of ½ lime • 3 lime leaves • 1 tsp shrimp paste

recipes.title: Panang Curry Paste
recipes.overview: Milder, sweeter than red curry paste, with roasted peanuts. Key ingredient for Panang curries; adjust spice and sweetness as desired.
recipes.ingredients: 1 generous tbsp cumin seeds • 1 generous tbsp coriander seeds • 1½ tsp white pepper • 12 dried red bird’s eye chillies, soaked in water for 30 minutes and cut into small pieces • 12 garlic cloves, roughly chopped • 2 medium shallots, finely chopped • 1 thumb-sized piece galangal, thinly sliced • 2 fresh red chillies, thinly sliced • 1 lemongrass stalk, tough outer part removed and thinly sliced • 10 thick coriander stalks • Zest of ½ lime • 4 lime leaves, stems removed and finely chopped • 3–4 tbsp roasted peanuts • 1 tsp shrimp paste

recipes.title: Thai Green Curry Paste
recipes.overview: Spicy paste with green bird's eye chillies. Adjust chillies for heat; taste and balance flavors. Use fresh for best results.
recipes.ingredients: 1 tsp cumin seeds • 1 tsp coriander seeds • 1½ tsp white pepper • About 20 green bird’s eye chillies, roughly chopped • 2 lemongrass stalks , thinly sliced • 8 garlic cloves, smashed • 1 thumb-sized piece galangal, thinly sliced • 3 small shallots, roughly chopped • 10 Thai sweet basil stalks • 5 coriander stalks • Zest of ½ lime • 5 lime leaves • 1 tsp shrimp paste

recipes.title: Thai Red Curry Paste
recipes.overview: Common Thai curry paste with moderate spice. Adjust chillies for heat; add more spur chillies for vibrant red color.
recipes.ingredients: 1 generous tbsp cumin seeds • 1 generous tbsp coriander seeds • 1½ tsp white pepper • 12 dried red bird’s eye chillies, soaked in water for 30 minutes and cut into small pieces • 12 garlic cloves, roughly chopped • 2 medium shallots, finely chopped • 1 thumb-sized piece galangal, thinly sliced • 2 red spur chillies, thinly sliced • 1 lemongrass stalk, tough outer part removed and thinly sliced • 10 thick coriander stalks • Zest of ½ lime • 1 tsp shrimp paste

recipes.title: Yellow Curry Paste
recipes.overview: Versatile paste with yellow color from turmeric. Use fresh turmeric for better flavor and moisture; it stains, so be careful.
recipes.ingredients: 1½ tsp coriander seeds • 1½ tsp cardamom seeds • ½ tsp green cardamom seeds • 1½ tsp white pepper • 12 dried red bird’s eye chillies, soaked in water for 30 minutes and cut into small pieces • 12 garlic cloves • 1 thumb-sized piece galangal, thinly sliced • 1 thumb-sized piece fresh turmeric, peeled and thinly sliced, or 1–1½ tsp ground turmeric • 3 lime leaves, stalks removed and finely chopped • 3 medium shallots, halved • 10 thick coriander stalks • 2 tbsp sliced lemongrass • 1 tsp shrimp paste

subcategories.overview: Curry pastes that anchor each Thai curry style.

subcategories.label: Salads

recipes.title: Glass Noodle Salad
recipes.overview: Spicy Thai salad with glass noodles, prawns, and pork. Nostalgic dish from Thai barbecues; serve hot or at room temperature.
recipes.ingredients: 100 g raw glass noodles • 6 baby plum tomatoes, quartered • 4 shallots, thinly sliced • 1 stick celery with leaves, thinly sliced • 10 small raw prawns , peeled and deveined • 125 g minced pork • 10 roasted cashews, roughly chopped • 3 tbsp roughly chopped coriander leaves • 1 tbsp dried shrimp, soaked in warm water for 10 minutes • 3 garlic cloves, smashed • 2 tbsp finely chopped coriander stalks • 3 red bird’s eye chillies, roughly chopped • 1 tbsp palm sugar, grated • 3 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 3½ tbsp lime juice

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Green Papaya Salad
recipes.overview: Famous Thai salad (som tum) with sour, sweet, savory, and spicy flavors. Pounded dressing coats crispy papaya and vegetables. Make ahead; won't wilt.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp peanuts • 1½ tbsp dried baby shrimp • 3 garlic cloves • 2–3 red bird’s eye chillies • 12 green beans, cut into 2.5 cm pieces • 1 tbsp palm sugar, grated and finely chopped • 1 tbsp tamarind paste • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce • Juice of 1 large lime • 400 g green papaya, grated • 1 medium carrot, peeled and grated • 6 baby plum tomatoes, halved • 2 tbsp finely chopped coriander • 2 tbsp Thai sweet basil , roughly chopped

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

subcategories.overview: Som tam, glass noodle salads and other punchy Thai salads.

subcategories.label: Soups

recipes.title: Tom Yum Gai Soup (Hot and Sour Chicken Soup)
recipes.overview: When you go out for Thai food this is sure to be on the menu. I love the spiciness of this soup – you get a good hit of spice but it doesn’t linger. Some chefs add sugar to it but, for me, this is a spicy, savoury and tart soup with only a hint of natural sweetness from the fried shallots and tomatoes. Do, of course, taste the soup and adjust the flavour to your liking, adding sugar if you want. It makes a delicious starter but you could bulk it up by adding other ingredients such as noodles to make it a light main. The word ‘gai’ means chicken, so this is a chicken tom yum soup. You could substitute prawn (shrimp) stock and prawns to make a delicious tom yum goong, or go vegetarian and use water and tofu.
recipes.ingredients: 2 tbsp rapeseed oil • 2 shallots, finely chopped • 1 lemongrass stalk, smashed and cut into about 5 pieces • 8 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves thinly sliced • 2.5cm piece of galangal, thinly sliced • 3 garlic cloves, roughly chopped • 250g chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces • 8 mushrooms, quartered • 2 tomatoes, quartered • 3 spring onions , roughly chopped • Handful of chopped or sliced vegetables, such as cabbage, bean sprouts, carrots • 1 tbsp tamarind paste • 1 tbsp chilli jam • 1 tbsp roasted Thai chilli oil with some of the goop at the bottom • 3–4 tbsp Thai fish sauce* • 3 green bird’s eye chillies, smashed and cut lengthwise • 1 small handful of coriander , roughly chopped • 2 tsp palm or white sugar
ingredientNames: roasted thai chilli oil with some of the goop at the bottom

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: Tom yum, tom kha and other Thai soups.

subcategories.label: Spice Mixes

recipes.title: Thai Curry Powder
recipes.overview: I always have some of this homemade curry powder to hand. It’s slightly different to the curry powder I featured in my Indian cookbooks and it’s a good all-rounder. Thai food has many influences, including Indian, so a few of the recipes in this book call for curry powder. If you want to make your own, you’ll get great results with this.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tbsp coriander seeds • 3 tbsp cumin seeds • 2 tbsp black peppercorns • 1 tbsp fennel seeds • 1 tbsp black mustard seeds • 6cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark • 2 Indian bay leaves • 1 tsp fenugreek seeds • 2 star anise • 7 cardamom pods, lightly bruised • 4 Kashmiri dried red chillies • 1 tbsp ground turmeric • 1 tbsp hot chilli powder • ½ tsp garlic powder • 1 tsp dried onion powder
ingredientNames: 6cm piece of cinnamon stick or cassia bark

subcategories.overview: Thai dry spice blends.

subcategories.label: Starters

recipes.title: Chicken Satay with Peanut Sauce
recipes.overview: I’m a big fan of Thai chicken satay with peanut sauce. Although it isn’t necessary, it is best to marinate the chicken for at least a day. You could get away with 30 minutes but a longer marinating time will get you much tastier results. As the chicken soaks up that incredible marinade, it not only tenderizes it but makes it much juicier when cooked. This recipe could be used with thinly sliced pork or beef – both are also popular at Thai restaurants and takeaways. Pork is the meat of choice in Thailand but chicken is the most popular in the UK. I also like to serve this dish with cucumber and chilli relish.
recipes.ingredients: 1kg skinless chicken breast or thigh fillets, cut into bite-size pieces • 1 tbsp cumin seeds • 1 tbsp coriander seeds • ½ tsp ground white pepper • 2 tsp ground turmeric • 4 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves finely chopped • 1 tbsp thinly sliced lemongrass • 1 generous tbsp chopped galangal • 200ml coconut milk • A pinch of ground cinnamon • 2 tsp palm or white sugar • 1 tbsp white wine or rice vinegar • 1 tsp salt • 1 tbsp rapeseed oil • 2 tbsp Thai red curry paste • 400ml thick coconut milk • 200ml smooth or chunky peanut butter • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce* • 1 tbsp sugar • Juice of 1 lime • 2 tbsp tamarind water
ingredientNames: 1kg skinless chicken breast or thigh fillets

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Firecracker Prawns
recipes.overview: Prawns (shrimp) curl naturally into half-circles. To get your firecracker prawns looking right you need to do some cosmetic work but it’s an easy job: the underside of the prawns needs to be scored in three places so that you can straighten them up. I have seen this popular starter prepared with many different marinades but as the name implies, it’s the chilli that is important. In this recipe I suggest using both chilli paste and roasted chilli flakes. How much of each you add, however, is completely down to you and how spicy you like your food. I recommend serving these with sweet chilli sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 20 large raw prawns , peeled and deveined but tails left on • 1 garlic clove, finely minced • 1 tsp soy sauce • 1 tsp honey • 1 tbsp homemade chilli jam • 1 tsp roasted chilli flakes • ½ tsp lemon juice • 10 egg roll wrappers • 1 tsp cornflour • Rapeseed oil, for deep-frying or shallow-frying

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Fishcakes
recipes.overview: Thai fishcakes (often called ‘tod mun pla’ on menus) are known for their spongy consistency, which I’m not fond of; that sponginess comes from the egg, so I tend to leave it out. Fishcakes are usually deep-fried in street stalls and restaurants, but I find it much easier to shallow-fry them. These are great served with sweet chilli sauce, Thai seafood dipping sauce and/or cucumber and chilli relish.
recipes.ingredients: 500g meaty fish fillets, such as lemon sole, cod or salmon, skinned • 1 tbsp finely chopped coriander • 3 lime leaves, stalks removed and finely julienned • 2 spring onions , thinly sliced • 8 green beans, thinly sliced • 3–4 tbsp Thai red curry paste • 1 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 1 tbsp lime juice • 1 tsp sugar • 1 medium egg • 1 tbsp tapioca starch • 5 tbsp rapeseed oil

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Garlicky Salt and Pepper Pork Ribs
recipes.overview: These ribs are comfort food at its best and a moreish way to start a delicious Thai meal. I like to serve them with nam jim jaew or sweet chilli sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 1 rack of pork baby back ribs, cut into small 2.5cm pieces • 125ml rice wine vinegar • 2 tbsp light soy sauce • 1 tbsp oyster sauce • 1 tsp roasted chilli flakes • 6 garlic cloves, minced • 2 tsp freshly ground black pepper • 2 tsp salt • 7 tbsp cornflour • Rapeseed oil, for deep-frying • Your choice of dipping sauce
ingredientNames: rack of pork baby back ribs

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Pork and Vegetable Spring Rolls
recipes.overview: Made popular by Chinese restaurants and takeaways, these are another menu item that have been adapted to have a subtle Thai flavour. You need to plan ahead as the filling must be cold (or room temperature) before rolling your spring rolls. To make these vegetarian, simply leave out the meat.
recipes.ingredients: 450g pork shoulder, cut into thin slices against the grain • 200g Chinese broccoli or young broccoli, finely chopped • 200g fresh bean sprouts • 200g carrots, grated • 3 tbsp coriander stalks, finely chopped • 4 spring onions , finely chopped • 2 tbsp light soy sauce • 1 tbsp Chinese rice wine or dry sherry • 1 tsp sesame oil • 1 tbsp Thai fish sauce • ½ tsp palm sugar, finely grated • ¼ tsp white pepper • 1 tbsp cornflour • ½ tsp palm sugar, grated • 1 tbsp Chinese rice wine or dry sherry • 1 tbsp dark soy sauce • 1 tsp Thai fish sauce • ½ tsp white pepper • 1 tbsp cornflour , whisked with 2 tbsp water to make a paste • 6 tbsp rapeseed oil, plus 750ml for deep-frying • 30 x 10cm spring roll wrappers • 1 egg, beaten • Sweet chilli sauce
ingredientNames: chinese rice wine or dry sherry

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Pork Meatballs
recipes.overview: These meatballs are packed with delicious flavours. I like to serve them with a couple of dips like sweet chilli sauce and chilli jam. Before cooking them, I recommend frying up a bit of the prepared meat and adjusting the flavour to taste.
recipes.ingredients: 1 lemongrass stalk , bruised and cut into rings • 5 spring onions , finely chopped • 4 garlic cloves, finely chopped • 2.5cm piece of galangal, finely chopped • 3 red bird’s eye chillies, finely chopped • 3 lime leaves, stalks removed and leaves finely chopped • 30g fresh coriander , finely chopped • 500g minced pork • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 1 tsp clear honey or grated palm sugar • 3 tbsp rapeseed oil • Lime wedges, to serve
ingredientNames: clear honey or grated palm sugar

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

recipes.title: Prawn Toasts
recipes.overview: These aren’t like those you find at most takeaways: thinly layered with a bit of prawn (shrimp). No way! These are slightly larger than bite-size prawn mountains. They are amazing served with the sweet chilli sauce.
recipes.ingredients: 500g peeled and deveined prawns • 10 coriander stalks, finely chopped • 6 lime leaves, de-stalked and finely chopped • 4 garlic cloves, finely chopped • 2 tbsp Thai fish sauce • 1 egg white • ½ tsp white sugar • 1 French baguette, sliced into 2cm thick slices • White or black sesame seeds, for sprinkling • Rapeseed oil, for frying • Sweet chilli sauce

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Salt and Pepper Fried Calamari
recipes.overview: My first job out of college was working as the manager of a shoe store in Marina, near Monterey, California. I hated every second of that job but loved living in Monterey and all the amazing locally caught seafood. Squid was and still is a speciality there, and it was during my time of selling cheap shoes that I learned this method of frying calamari to perfection. I’ve tried a few other recipes but, in my opinion, this one ticks all the boxes. The crispy fried squid is delicious served with the sweet chilli sauce dip.
recipes.ingredients: 900g small squid tubes and tentacles, cleaned • 500ml full-fat milk, plus more if needed • 2 eggs • 6 red spur chillies, slit lengthways • 120g plain flour • 120g cornflour • 1 tsp salt • 1 tbsp red chilli powder • Rapeseed oil, for deep-frying • Flaky sea salt and freshly ground black pepper • Sweet chilli sauce, to serve
ingredientNames: flaky sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Sweet and Crispy Chicken Wings
recipes.overview: These sweet and savoury chicken wings are a Thai restaurant and takeaway favourite. I use palm sugar when I make this recipe, but you could just use equal amounts of white granulated sugar or honey if you like. The palm sugar is more authentic and also gives a deeper colour to the dish. Although the crispy fried garlic garnish is optional, I would encourage you to take some time to make it for this recipe. I love fried garlic and it really takes this recipe up a notch or two in my opinion.
recipes.ingredients: 12 chicken wings, bony ends cut off • 3 generous tbsp black peppercorns • 150g shallots, peeled and thinly sliced • 2 tbsp finely chopped ginger • 3 spring onions , roughly chopped, to garnish • 1 red spur chilli, thinly sliced, to garnish • 3 tbsp crispy fried garlic , to garnish • 150ml Thai fish sauce* • 135g granulated sugar or finely chopped palm sugar
ingredientNames: 135g granulated sugar or finely chopped palm sugar

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Tempura Garlic Prawns
recipes.overview: Here is another Thai starter you might think belongs in a Japanese cookbook. However, once you try these crisp prawns (shrimp) dipped into Thai seafood dipping sauce or sweet chilli sauce, you’ll find them to be 100% Thai. As in the photograph, you can fry veggies in the same way to make a tempura feast!
recipes.ingredients: 20 prawns , peeled and deveined but tails left on • 225g sifted plain flour • 2 tbsp rice flour • 500ml ice-cold sparkling water • 1 egg, beaten • 1 tsp salt • 1 tsp garlic powder • Rapeseed oil, for deep-frying • Thai seafood dipping sauce or sweet chilli sauce, to serve
ingredientNames: thai seafood dipping sauce or sweet chilli sauce

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Thai-Style Chilli Garlic Edamame
recipes.overview: I’m always happy when I go out for a Thai meal and I see edamame on the menu as a starter. It might be a very simple dish to make but it’s so addictive. I just can’t get enough and the great thing is you can make it at home with equally delicious results. You will find bags of frozen edamame in pods at most Asian grocers and they are also found in the freezer section of many supermarkets. The pods aren’t eaten but it is important to buy the edamame in the pod so you can pick them up and scrape the beans and all that amazing flavour stuck to the pod straight into your mouth.
recipes.ingredients: 500g edamame beans in the pod • 3 garlic cloves, finely chopped • 1½ tsp rapeseed oil • 1 tbsp sesame oil • 1 tbsp red chilli flakes • 1 tbsp light soy sauce* • Flaky sea salt, to taste

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

subcategories.overview: Spring rolls, fishcakes, satay and other Thai openers.

subcategories.label: Stir Fries

recipes.title: Chicken with Cashews
recipes.overview: This is a hugely popular dish at Thai restaurants and takeaways, and my family love it. It is important to cut the chicken pieces so that they are about the same size as the cashews (although this is more for presentation as large chunks also work fine). You can mix the sauce and fry the cashews, chillies and chicken a day or so in advance, making this a dish you can cook up very quickly after work with little mess. The first time I tried making this recipe, I burnt the cashews and chillies. Don’t make the same mistake or you’ll have to start all over again. They don’t take long to colour in the oil and cashews aren’t cheap, so keep an eye on them. Although there’s nothing stopping you from doing so, the dried and fried chillies are not meant to be eaten. I like to serve this curry with jasmine rice.
recipes.ingredients: 450g skinless chicken thigh fillets, cut into small cashew-size pieces • 2 tbsp light soy sauce • 1 tbsp dark soy sauce • 1 tbsp oyster sauce • 1 tsp Thai seasoning sauce • 70ml water or chicken stock • 1 tsp palm sugar, grated and finely chopped • 6 garlic cloves, roughly chopped • 4 spring onions , sliced • 1 large onion, thinly sliced • 3 red spur chillies, thinly sliced • 2 green bird’s eye chillies, cut into thin rings • 20–30 cashews • 20 dried red bird’s eye chillies • 3 tbsp cornflour • 250ml rapeseed oil, plus an extra 3 tbsp

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Garlic Prawn Pad See Ew
recipes.overview: "Pad see ew" translates literally as "stir-fried with soy sauce", and that soy is the heart of the dish: dark, sweet and clinging to wide rice noodles charred at the edges in a hot wok. Broccolini stands in for traditional Chinese broccoli (kai lan), the prawns get a brief garlic-soy marinade, and an egg is folded through right at the end. A sharp homemade chilli vinegar at the table is the traditional Thai counterweight to all that sweet soy.
recipes.ingredients: ¼ cup white vinegar • 1 long red chilli • 200 grams peeled and deveined prawns • 2 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon soy sauce • 1 teaspoon sesame oil • Pinch of ground white pepper • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil • 350 grams fresh rice noodles • 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce • ½ teaspoon caster sugar • 1 bunch broccolini • 2 tablespoons water • 2 eggs • 2 tablespoons soy sauce • Ground white pepper to taste • Chilli powder

winePairings.note: Crisp dry whites; richer fish (salmon, tuna) can take a light Pinot Noir too.

recipes.title: Pad Pak Ruam
recipes.overview: A wok gets hot; garlic flashes briefly in oil; vegetables go in by cook time — firmest first (broccoli, carrot, baby corn), then softer (mushroom, snow peas), and the leafy ones at the end (water spinach, bok choy). A small amount of vegetable stock and soy steams them through; oyster sauce (vegetarian) and sugar balance. Sesame oil to finish.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 6 garlic cloves • 1 head broccoli • 1 carrot • 100 g baby corn • 200 g mixed mushrooms • 100 g snow peas • 200 g pak choi or bok choy • 100 g beansprouts • 4 tablespoons vegetable stock or water • 3 tablespoons vegetarian oyster sauce • 2 tablespoons light soy sauce • 1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine or dry sherry • 1 teaspoon sugar • ½ teaspoon white pepper • 1 long red chilli • 1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil • Cooked jasmine rice
ingredientNames: shaoxing rice wine or dry sherry

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Prawn and Pork Pad Thai
recipes.overview: This classic Thai dish of noodles is both aromatic and lightly spicy, serving well as either a main course or a starter. Pad Thai combines stir-fried rice noodles with tender chicken, pork, and prawns in a balanced sauce of curry paste, oyster sauce, and fish sauce. Fresh herbs, crushed peanuts, and a squeeze of lime complete this iconic Thai street food favourite.
recipes.ingredients: 110 grams dried rice noodles • 3 tablespoons groundnut oil • 200 ml chicken stock • 2 cloves garlic • 3 cm cube of ginger • 1 teaspoon red curry paste • 1 teaspoon chilli sauce • 1 teaspoon oyster sauce • 100 grams skinned chicken breast • 100 grams lean pork • 8 cooked king prawns • 20 raw prawns • 1 tablespoon red bell pepper • 2 red chillies • 2 tablespoons bean sprouts • 3 tablespoons spring onions • 2 tablespoons Thai basil leaves • 1 tablespoon coriander leaves • Sweet soy sauce • Fish sauce • 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts • 1 teaspoon palm sugar • Thai basil leaves • Lime wedges

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Stir Fried Chicken with Chilli and Basil
recipes.overview: Pad krapow kai, this quick and easy chicken dish is an excellent introduction to Thai cuisine. Fiery chillies partner the holy basil, which has a pungent flavour that is spicy and sharp. Tender chicken pieces are stir-fried until just cooked through, then tossed with garlic, fresh chillies, and aromatic holy basil, creating a vibrant dish bursting with authentic Thai flavours.
recipes.ingredients: 450 grams skinless chicken breasts • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 garlic cloves • 4 fresh red chillies • 3 tablespoons Thai fish sauce • 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce • 1 teaspoon granulated sugar • 12 holy basil leaves • 2 fresh red chillies • 20 deep-fried holy basil leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Stir-Fried Beef in Oyster Sauce
recipes.overview: On Thai menus this is often called ‘pad nam mun hoy’, which means fried with oyster sauce. There are many versions of Thai oyster sauce curries, but this beef version is right up there when it comes to popularity. Stir-fried beef in oyster sauce usually also comes served with mushrooms and my favourite variety for this recipe are straw mushrooms, but you could use any type you can find – wild mushrooms work really well. Serve with a hot bowl of jasmine rice.
recipes.ingredients: 350g sirloin steak • 2 tbsp light soy sauce* • 2 tbsp rapeseed oil or sesame oil • 4 garlic cloves, roughly chopped • 4 spring onions , cut into 5cm pieces • 1 red spur chilli, cut into thin rings • Large handful of whole straw mushrooms • 1 medium onion, thinly sliced • 3–4 tbsp oyster sauce* • 4 tbsp stock or water • ½ tsp ground white pepper • 1 tbsp cornflour , mixed with 1½ tbsp water
ingredientNames: large handful of whole straw mushrooms

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Thai Holy Basil and Chilli Chicken Stir Fry
recipes.overview: Although there is no rule about how to cut your chicken for this popular Thai stir fry, I like to cut the meat quite small, slightly smaller than bite-size. This is a delicious and easy recipe that can be whipped up in minutes. Serve it with white, steamed jasmine rice.
recipes.ingredients: 8 garlic cloves, smashed • 5 red spur chillies, cut into thin rings • 2 shallots, thinly sliced • 2 tbsp rapeseed oil • 600g skinless chicken thigh fillets, cut into small pieces • 1 tbsp light soy sauce* • 1 tsp palm sugar, finely chopped • 1 tbsp oyster sauce* • 1½ tsp dark soy sauce* • Large handful of Thai holy basil leaves, finely or roughly chopped
ingredientNames: large handful of thai holy basil leaves

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Traditional Pad Thai
recipes.overview: Pad thai began as a 1930s government-promoted national dish during a campaign to reduce rice consumption, and has since become Thailand's best-known noodle export. The success of any version comes down to the sauce: equal parts fish sauce, tamarind and palm sugar, with a spoonful of finely chopped pickled radish for backbone. Once the sauce is mixed the wok work is fast, with soft rice noodles, chicken, tofu, dried shrimp and egg joining in quick succession before the dish is finished with peanuts, chives, lime and chilli at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 3 tablespoons fish sauce • 3 tablespoons tamarind puree • 3 tablespoons palm sugar • 2 tablespoons finely chopped pickled radish • 200 grams dried rice stick noodles • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 400 grams chicken thigh fillets • 2 tablespoons dried shrimp • 50 grams firm tofu • 2 eggs • ½ cup Chinese garlic chives or spring onion • ¼ cup bean sprouts • ¼ cup roasted peanuts • Chilli powder • Lime wedges
ingredientNames: chinese garlic chives or spring onion

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

subcategories.overview: Wok-fired meat, seafood and vegetable plates.

subcategories.label: Stocks

recipes.title: Thai Chicken Stock
recipes.overview: When making stocks for Asian dishes, it is important to use Asian ingredients. Chicken stock is used as a base for dishes in so many cuisines. Using a Western-style chicken stock or – even worse – chicken stock cubes to cook Thai recipes just won’t do, as the flavours will be wrong. This simple Thai stock will get you the flavour you need for your Thai dishes.
recipes.ingredients: 1.5kg meaty chicken bones • 10 coriander stalks • 1 large onion, roughly chopped • 10 garlic cloves, smashed • 2.5cm piece of galangal, thinly sliced and lightly smashed • 1 whole lemongrass stalk, bruised • 1 tsp white peppercorns

subcategories.overview: Thai-flavoured chicken and vegetable stocks.

Cooking that balances four flavours in every dish: sweet, sour, salty and spicy. Built on lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaf, fish sauce, palm sugar and chilli, often rounded with coconut milk. Pounded curry pastes, fast wok work, raw salads dressed with lime and fish sauce, and aromatic broths like tom yum show the spread.

subcategories.label: Turkish

recipes.title: Baklava
recipes.overview: Phyllo sheets layer with melted butter — half the stack on the base, the pistachio filling spread across, the other half on top. The whole tray scores into diamonds before baking; a slow bake at moderate heat ensures even gold colour and crisp layers. Cool sugar syrup pours over hot baklava and gets absorbed instantly.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g phyllo pastry • 350 g unsalted butter • 400 g shelled pistachios • 50 g caster sugar • 500 g caster sugar • 350 ml water • 1 tablespoon lemon juice • 1 tablespoon honey

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Çoban Salatası
recipes.overview: Vegetables are diced into 1 cm cubes — slightly larger than Israeli salad. Olive oil, lemon and a generous shake of sumac bring it together. Eats at room temperature; doesn't keep — make right before serving.
recipes.ingredients: 4 medium tomatoes • 1 large cucumber • 1 small red onion • 1 long green pepper • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil • Juice of 1 lemon • 1 teaspoon sumac • 1 teaspoon salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 100 g feta • A handful of kalamata olives

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Manti
recipes.overview: A simple egg-pasta dough rolls thin. Minced lamb, finely-chopped onion, parsley and spices form the filling — uncooked. Each dumpling is 1.5 cm square: a teaspoon of filling, four corners pinched up to form a star/parcel. They boil briefly. Yogurt-garlic sauce ladles into bowls; dumplings pile in; melted butter with sumac and paprika pours over hot.
recipes.ingredients: 400 g plain flour • 2 large eggs • ½ teaspoon salt • 100 ml water • 300 g minced lamb • 1 small onion • 2 garlic cloves • A small bunch of flat-leaf parsley • 1 teaspoon ground cumin • 1 teaspoon paprika • ½ teaspoon black pepper • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 tablespoon olive oil • 400 g plain yogurt • 4 garlic cloves • ½ teaspoon salt • 80 g unsalted butter • 2 teaspoons paprika • 1 teaspoon sumac • ½ teaspoon Aleppo pepper or chilli flakes • Dried mint • A small bunch of parsley

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

subcategories.overview: Bridging Europe and the Middle East, Turkey's cuisine is both Ottoman-imperial and rural-village. Mezze plates, charcoal-grilled kebabs, slow-cooked stews and patisserie traditions sit on the same table. Yogurt, eggplant, tomato, parsley, sumac, pomegranate molasses and Aleppo pepper drive seasoning; baklava, lokum and revani close meals; menemen, simit and çay shape mornings.

subcategories.label: Ukrainian

recipes.title: Deruny
recipes.overview: Floury potatoes grate into cold water with a squeeze of lemon (stops browning); a wrung-dry pile of grated potato mixes with finely-grated onion, eggs, salt and a small amount of flour. Spoonfuls fry in butter or oil until each side is crisp and golden — the inside should still be tender. Eat immediately with soured cream and dill.
recipes.ingredients: 1 kg floury potatoes • Juice of half a lemon • 1 medium onion • 2 large eggs • 3 tablespoons plain flour • 1½ teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • 4-6 tablespoons sunflower oil • 200 g soured cream • A small bunch of fresh dill • Crispy fried onions

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Holubtsi
recipes.overview: A whole cabbage is cored and lowered into boiling water; the outer leaves soften and peel off in turn. The filling — half-cooked rice mixed with beef, pork, onion, garlic, herbs, egg — gets a heaped tablespoon onto each leaf, which folds and rolls into a parcel. The rolls layer in a pot with sauce of tomato, stock and soured cream; everything braises 1 hour. Soured cream and dill at the table.
recipes.ingredients: 1 large green cabbage • 1 tablespoon salt • 100 g long-grain rice • 300 ml water • 500 g minced beef • 250 g minced pork • 1 large onion • 4 garlic cloves • 1 large egg • 1 teaspoon dried marjoram • 1 teaspoon paprika • 1½ teaspoons salt • ½ teaspoon black pepper • A small bunch of dill • 2 tablespoons sunflower oil • 1 medium onion • 2 medium carrots • 3 garlic cloves • 2 tablespoons tomato paste • 1 x 400 g tin chopped tomatoes • 600 ml beef or vegetable stock • 200 g soured cream • 1 bay leaf • 2 sprigs thyme • 1 teaspoon paprika • 1 teaspoon salt • Black pepper • Extra soured cream • Fresh dill

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Pampushky
recipes.overview: A simple enriched dough — flour, milk, butter, egg, yeast — rises until doubled. Twelve small balls form, arrange touching in a round tin and rise again. Bake until golden. While they bake, garlic-oil dressing is mashed in a mortar; the rolls come out and immediately get brushed all over with the warm garlicky oil.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g strong white bread flour • 1 sachet fast-action yeast • 1 tablespoon caster sugar • 1 teaspoon salt • 250 ml whole milk • 1 large egg • 50 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons sunflower oil • 8 garlic cloves • 1 teaspoon salt • 4 tablespoons sunflower oil • 2 tablespoons fresh dill • 2 tablespoons water

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

recipes.title: Syrnyky
recipes.overview: Drained dry cottage cheese (or quark, ricotta, farmer's cheese) mixes with eggs, flour, sugar and vanilla into a thick batter. Heaped tablespoons land on a buttered hot pan, flatten to 2 cm thick discs, and fry slowly to golden — the cheese cooks through, the surface crisps. Soured cream and jam on top.
recipes.ingredients: 500 g dry cottage cheese • 2 large eggs • 80 g caster sugar • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract • 5 tablespoons plain flour • ½ teaspoon salt • Zest of 1 lemon • 50 g raisins • 30 g unsalted butter • 2 tablespoons sunflower oil • 200 g soured cream • Cherry jam, raspberry jam, or honey

winePairings.note: Herbal, mineral whites and light reds let vegetables shine.

subcategories.overview: Hearty Eastern European cooking with deep echoes of Polish, Russian and Tatar traditions. Wheat, buckwheat, beetroot, cabbage, potato and pork sit at the centre; soured cream, dill, garlic and bay finish nearly every plate. Borscht (each family with its own version), holubtsi (stuffed cabbage), varenyky (filled dumplings), salo (cured pork fat) and pampushky (garlic rolls) define the tradition; the table is communal, generous and built around a slow Sunday lunch.

subcategories.label: Vietnamese

recipes.title: Bánh Xèo Chay
recipes.overview: Rice flour, turmeric, coconut milk and water make a thin yellow batter. Filling vegetables — mushrooms, sliced onion, tofu — sauté in a hot pan; the batter pours over to form a thin pancake; beansprouts pile in last; the lot folds in half and slides out crisp. Wrap pieces in lettuce with herbs; dip in nuoc cham.
recipes.ingredients: 200 g rice flour • 30 g cornflour • 1 teaspoon ground turmeric • ½ teaspoon salt • 1 teaspoon sugar • 200 ml coconut milk • 400 ml water • 4 spring onions • 50 g mixed mushrooms • 30 g firm tofu • 30 g sliced onion • 50 g beansprouts • Pinch salt • 4 tablespoons vegetable oil • 4 tablespoons light soy sauce • 3 tablespoons rice vinegar • 2 tablespoons sugar • 4 tablespoons water • Juice of 1 lime • 2 garlic cloves • 1 bird's-eye chilli • 1 carrot • 1 head butter lettuce • A large handful mint • A large handful coriander • A large handful Thai basil

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Go Bo Hoi An
recipes.overview: Go Bo Hoi An is a piquant Vietnamese beef salad featuring thinly sliced seared beef tossed with crisp vegetables, fresh herbs, and a bright tamarind-lime dressing. This dish has delicate undertones of lime and garlic which carry through the tamarind flavours perfectly. The combination of tender beef, crunchy vegetables, aromatic herbs, and crispy rice papers creates a textural and flavourful celebration of Vietnamese cuisine. Quick to make but requires advance preparation, ensure the salad, dressing, and toppings are made and ready to use before cooking the beef.
recipes.ingredients: 200 grams beef sirloin or tenderloin • 1 tablespoon tamarind paste • 1 teaspoon sugar • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste • 1 garlic clove • 75 grams lettuce • 75 grams green papaya • 75 grams tomatoes • 75 grams cucumber • ½ white onion • 4 fresh red chillies • Juice of 1 lime • 1 tablespoon Thai fish sauce • 3 tablespoons sugar • 1 garlic clove • 30 grams fresh mint • 30 grams fresh coriander • 15 grams fresh basil • 2 tablespoons fried shallot slices • 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts • 2 roasted rice papers
ingredientNames: salt and freshly ground black pepper

winePairings.note: Bold, tannic reds stand up to red meat and roasts.

recipes.title: Gỏi Cuốn Chay
recipes.overview: Vermicelli noodles cook briefly and get tossed with sesame oil to stop sticking. Tofu pan-fries until golden. Rice paper rounds soak in warm water until pliable; everything stacks on each round and rolls tightly. The peanut sauce blends in seconds.
recipes.ingredients: 100 g rice vermicelli noodles • 2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil • 200 g firm tofu • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil • 1 tablespoon light soy sauce • 12 sheets rice paper • 1 small head butter lettuce or little gem • 100 g beansprouts • 1 carrot • 1 cucumber • A large handful mint leaves • A large handful coriander leaves • A large handful Thai basil leaves • A handful chives • 4 tablespoons hoisin sauce • 2 tablespoons smooth peanut butter • 2 tablespoons rice vinegar • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • 1 garlic clove • 60-100 ml hot water • 1 bird's-eye chilli • 1 tablespoon crushed roasted peanuts
ingredientNames: small head butter lettuce or little gem

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Phở Chay
recipes.overview: Onion and ginger char black on a flame to give the broth its signature smoky depth. They go into water with toasted spices, dried mushrooms, kombu (kelp) and a few aromatics, simmering for an hour to extract flavour. The broth strains clear; tofu poaches in it briefly. Rice noodles cook separately; everything assembles in bowls with a pile of fresh herbs and chillies.
recipes.ingredients: 2 onions • 8 cm fresh ginger • 4 star anise • 1 cinnamon stick • 5 cloves • 1 tablespoon coriander seeds • 1 tablespoon black peppercorns • 25 g dried shiitake mushrooms • 1 piece kombu • 250 g daikon — optional but classic • 2 large carrots • 2.5 litres water • 60 ml light soy sauce • 2 teaspoons salt • 1 tablespoon brown sugar • 400 g flat rice noodles • 300 g firm tofu • A large handful Thai basil leaves • A large handful coriander • A large handful mint • 200 g beansprouts • 2 limes • 2 bird's-eye chillies • Hoisin sauce • Sriracha or other chilli sauce

winePairings.note: A touch of sweetness tames chilli heat better than tannic reds.

recipes.title: Vietnamese Chicken Curry
recipes.overview: A fragrant Vietnamese curry that blends French colonial influences with traditional Southeast Asian spicing. This dish showcases the Vietnamese love of balance, aromatic spices like star anise, cardamom, and cinnamon partner with earthy lemongrass and fresh chillies. The result is a warm, comforting curry with subtle sweetness and complex layered flavours. Even though Vietnam was colonized by the French, the traditional cuisine has more in common with their Chinese neighbours.
recipes.ingredients: 800 grams skinless chicken breasts • 3 tablespoons sunflower oil • 4 garlic cloves • 1 red chilli • 2 star anise • 4 tablespoons lemongrass • 1 teaspoon cardamom seeds • 1 cinnamon stick • 1 tablespoon ground bean sauce • 12 spring onions • 300 grams green beans • 1 red bell pepper • 400 ml coconut milk • 100 ml chicken stock • 2 tablespoons fish sauce • 1 tablespoon palm sugar • 2 tablespoons fresh coriander • 2 tablespoons fresh mint • 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts

winePairings.note: Versatile pairings — pick lighter wines for poached or roasted, fuller for creamy or charred.

recipes.title: Vietnamese Pork Bun Cha
recipes.overview: Two cuts of pork (sliced belly and seasoned mince patties) are marinated in a fish-sauce, garlic and shallot mixture, then char-grilled fast over high heat to keep them juicy. They land in bowls of warm fish-sauce dressing alongside cooked rice vermicelli, fresh mint, Thai basil, lettuce, pickled green papaya and a sprinkle of garlic and chilli. Diners assemble each spoonful at the table from the components, lifting noodles and herbs into the broth.
recipes.ingredients: ¼ cup fish sauce • 4 small Asian shallots • 6 garlic cloves • 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce • 1 tablespoon white sugar • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper • 500 grams pork mince • 500 grams pork belly • Vegetable oil • ½ cup white vinegar • ½ cup white sugar • 1 teaspoon sea salt • 100 grams thinly sliced green papaya • ½ cup fish sauce • 3 tablespoons white vinegar • ¾ cup white sugar • 1 cup water • 2 tablespoons lime juice • 500 grams dried vermicelli noodles • 3 red chillies • 5 garlic cloves • Fresh mint • Thai basil • Lettuce leaves

winePairings.note: Pork works with both fruity reds and off-dry whites; pick by sauce richness.

subcategories.overview: Light, herb-driven cooking shaped by Chinese and French influences. Fish sauce, lime, mint, coriander, Thai basil and chilli give every dish its lift; rice noodles and rice paper supply the structure. Long-simmered broths like pho, grilled and fresh-rolled meats, and table-side assembly with herbs and lettuce define the experience.

categories.overview: A region-by-region tour through global cooking traditions, from Cajun gumbos and Indian curries to Italian ragùs and Vietnamese pho. Each section captures its cuisine's signature flavours, key ingredients and the techniques that shape its character.

ingredientIndex.name: salt and freshly ground black pepper
salt and freshly ground white pepper
fine sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

double cream: Evaporated milk + 1 tbsp butter per 100 ml
Crème fraîche (tangier, won't whip as stiffly)
single cream: Whole milk + 1 tbsp butter per 100 ml
Evaporated milk diluted with a little water
soured cream: Whole milk + 1 tsp lemon juice per 100 ml
buttermilk: 1 cup whole milk + 1 tbsp lemon juice or white vinegar (rest 5 min)
Plain yoghurt thinned with milk to pouring consistency
creme fraiche: Heavy cream + 1 tsp lemon juice per 100 ml, rested 30 min
butter: Coconut oil (tropical note in sweet bakes)
shallots: Red onion + ½ garlic clove (1 shallot ≈ 1 small red onion)
Yellow onion (milder if briefly soaked in cold water)
spring onions: Chives (use the green tops only, less bite)
garlic: Garlic powder (1 clove ≈ ⅛ tsp powder)
Asafoetida (a pinch, especially in Indian cooking)
ginger: Ground ginger (1 tsp fresh ≈ ¼ tsp ground)
lemon juice: White wine vinegar (¾ of the volume)
Apple cider vinegar diluted 1:1 with water
balsamic vinegar: Red wine vinegar + 1 tsp brown sugar per 2 tbsp
Sherry vinegar + a touch of honey
rice vinegar: Apple cider vinegar diluted 1:1 with water
white wine: Chicken or vegetable stock + 1 tsp lemon juice
White grape juice + a splash of vinegar
red wine: Pomegranate juice + a splash of red wine vinegar
fresh basil: Dried basil (use ⅓ of the fresh quantity)
fresh thyme: Dried thyme (1 tsp fresh ≈ ¼ tsp dried)
fresh mint: Dried mint (¼ of the fresh quantity)
garam masala: Curry powder + a pinch each of cinnamon and cardamom
Mild curry powder + a touch of black pepper
saffron: Pinch of turmeric (colour only, very different flavour)
caster sugar: Granulated sugar pulsed briefly in a blender
brown sugar: White sugar + 1 tbsp molasses per cup
icing sugar: Granulated sugar blended fine + 1 tbsp cornflour per cup
Caster sugar (won't dissolve smoothly into glazes)
chicken stock: Beef stock (deeper, less suited to delicate dishes)
beef stock: Vegetable stock + 1 tsp Marmite + ½ tsp Worcestershire
vegetable stock: Water + a pinch of MSG or Marmite
plain flour: Self-raising flour with the baking powder omitted from the recipe
cornflour: Plain flour (use 2x the volume; less glossy result)
fish sauce: Soy sauce + 1 anchovy fillet mashed in (per tbsp)
miso: Soy sauce (saltier, less sweet; use ⅔ the volume)
Marmite or Vegemite (very pungent; use a pinch)
mascarpone: Cream cheese + 2 tbsp double cream per 250 g
